English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, these seven principles for the settling of legal issues come up for recitation. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-as1:2.1

English (brahmali, published): For the settling and resolving of legal issues whenever they arise there is: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-as1:2.2
Pali: sammukhāvinayo dātabbo, 
English (brahmali, published): Resolution face-to-face to be applied;

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:0.5
Pali: 1. Pattasannicayasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 1. The training rule on collecting almsbowls 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:0.6
Pali: Ime kho panāyyāyo tiṁsa nissaggiyā pācittiyā dhammā uddesaṁ āgacchanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerables, these thirty rules on relinquishment and confession come up for recitation. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo bahū patte sannicayaṁ karonti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the nuns from the group of six had collected a large number of almsbowls. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:1.3
Pali: Manussā vihāracārikaṁ āhiṇḍantā passitvā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): When people walking about the dwellings saw this, they complained and criticized those nuns, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:1.4
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo bahū patte sannicayaṁ karissanti, pattavāṇijjaṁ vā bhikkhuniyo karissanti, āmattikāpaṇaṁ vā pasāressantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the nuns collect a large number of bowls? Will they start up as bowl merchants or set up a bowl shop?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:1.5
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:1.6
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized those nuns, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:1.7
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo pattasannicayaṁ karissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the nuns from the group of six collect bowls?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:1.8
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo pattasannicayaṁ karontī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the nuns from the group of six do this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:1.9
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:1.10
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:1.11
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo pattasannicayaṁ karissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the nuns from the group of six collect bowls? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:1.12
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:1.13
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:1.14.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī pattasannicayaṁ kareyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun collects almsbowls, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.5
Pali: Patto nāma 
English (brahmali, published): An almsbowl: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.6
Pali: dve pattā— ayopatto, mattikāpatto. 
English (brahmali, published): there are two kinds of bowls: the iron bowl and the ceramic bowl. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.7
Pali: Tayo pattassa vaṇṇā— ukkaṭṭho patto, majjhimo patto, omako patto. 
English (brahmali, published): And there are three sizes of bowls: the large bowl, the medium bowl, and the small bowl. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.8
Pali: Ukkaṭṭho nāma patto 
English (brahmali, published): The large bowl: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.9
Pali: aḍḍhāḷhakodanaṁ gaṇhāti catubhāgaṁ khādanaṁ tadupiyaṁ byañjanaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): it holds half an <i lang='pi' translate='no'>āḷhaka</i> measure of cooked rice, a fourth part of fresh food, and a suitable amount of curry. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.10
Pali: Majjhimo nāma patto 
English (brahmali, published): The medium bowl: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.11
Pali: nāḷikodanaṁ gaṇhāti catubhāgaṁ khādanaṁ tadupiyaṁ byañjanaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): it holds a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>nāḷika</i> measure of cooked rice, a fourth part of fresh food, and a suitable amount of curry. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.12
Pali: Omako nāma patto 
English (brahmali, published): The small bowl: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.13
Pali: patthodanaṁ gaṇhāti catubhāgaṁ khādanaṁ tadupiyaṁ byañjanaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): it holds a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>pattha</i> measure of cooked rice, a fourth part of fresh food, and a suitable amount of curry. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.14
Pali: Tato ukkaṭṭho apatto omako apatto. 
English (brahmali, published): Anything larger than this is not a bowl, nor anything smaller. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.15
Pali: Sannicayaṁ kareyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Collects: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.16
Pali: anadhiṭṭhito avikappito. 
English (brahmali, published): almsbowls that have neither been determined nor assigned to another. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.17
Pali: Nissaggiyo hotīti 
English (brahmali, published): Entailing relinquishment: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.18
Pali: saha aruṇuggamanā nissaggiyo hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): entailing relinquishment at dawn. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.19
Pali: Nissajjitabbo saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā ekabhikkhuniyā vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The bowl should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.20
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.21
Pali: Tāya bhikkhuniyā saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā vuḍḍhānaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): After approaching the Sangha, that nun should arrange her upper robe over one shoulder and pay respect at the feet of the senior nuns. She should then squat on her heels, raise her joined palms, and say: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.22
Pali: “ayaṁ me, ayye, patto rattātikkanto nissaggiyo, imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Venerables, this almsbowl, which I have kept for more than one day, is to be relinquished. I relinquish it to the Sangha.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.23
Pali: Nissajjitvā āpatti desetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): After relinquishing it, she should confess the offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.24
Pali: Byattāya bhikkhuniyā paṭibalāya āpatti paṭiggahetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): The confession should be received by a competent and capable nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.25
Pali: Nissaṭṭhapatto dātabbo—
English (brahmali, published): The relinquished bowl is then to be given back: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.26
Pali: “Suṇātu me, ayye, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.27
Pali: Ayaṁ patto itthannāmāya bhikkhuniyā nissaggiyo saṅghassa nissaṭṭho. 
English (brahmali, published): This almsbowl, which was to be relinquished by the nun so-and-so, has been relinquished to the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.28
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho imaṁ pattaṁ itthannāmāya bhikkhuniyā dadeyyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should give this bowl back to nun so-and-so.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.29
Pali: Tāya bhikkhuniyā sambahulā bhikkhuniyo upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā vuḍḍhānaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassu vacanīyā—
English (brahmali, published): Or: after approaching several nuns, that nun should arrange her upper robe over one shoulder and pay respect at the feet of the senior nuns. She should then squat on her heels, raise her joined palms, and say: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.30
Pali: “ayaṁ me, ayyāyo, patto rattātikkanto nissaggiyo, imāhaṁ ayyānaṁ nissajjāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this almsbowl, which I have kept for more than one day, is to be relinquished. I relinquish it to you.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.31
Pali: Nissajjitvā āpatti desetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): After relinquishing it, she should confess the offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.32
Pali: Byattāya bhikkhuniyā paṭibalāya āpatti paṭiggahetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): The confession should be received by a competent and capable nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.33
Pali: Nissaṭṭhapatto dātabbo—
English (brahmali, published): The relinquished bowl is then to be given back: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.34
Pali: “Suṇantu me, ayyāyo. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please, venerables, I ask you to listen. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.35
Pali: Ayaṁ patto itthannāmāya bhikkhuniyā nissaggiyo ayyānaṁ nissaṭṭho. 
English (brahmali, published): This almsbowl, which was to be relinquished by the nun so-and-so, has been relinquished to you. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.36
Pali: Yadi ayyānaṁ pattakallaṁ, ayyāyo, imaṁ pattaṁ itthannāmāya bhikkhuniyā dadeyyun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If the venerables are ready, you should give this bowl back to nun so-and-so.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.37
Pali: Tāya bhikkhuniyā ekaṁ bhikkhuniṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyā—
English (brahmali, published): Or: after approaching a single nun, that nun should arrange her upper robe over one shoulder, squat on her heels, raise her joined palms, and say: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.38
Pali: “ayaṁ me, ayye, patto rattātikkanto nissaggiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): “This almsbowl, which I have kept for more than one day, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.39
Pali: Imāhaṁ ayyāya nissajjāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to you.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.40
Pali: Nissajjitvā āpatti desetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): After relinquishing it, she should confess the offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.41
Pali: Tāya bhikkhuniyā āpatti paṭiggahetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): The confession should be received by that nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.42
Pali: Nissaṭṭhapatto dātabbo—
English (brahmali, published): The relinquished bowl is then to be given back: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.1.43
Pali: “imaṁ pattaṁ ayyāya dammī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this almsbowl back to you.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.2.1
Pali: Rattātikkante atikkantasaññā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than one day and she perceives it as more, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.2.2
Pali: Rattātikkante vematikā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than one day, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.2.3
Pali: Rattātikkante anatikkantasaññā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than one day, but she perceives it as less, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.2.4
Pali: Anadhiṭṭhite adhiṭṭhitasaññā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been determined, but she perceives that it has, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.2.5
Pali: Avikappite vikappitasaññā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been assigned to another, but she perceives that it has, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.2.6
Pali: Avissajjite vissajjitasaññā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been given away, but she perceives that it has, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.2.7
Pali: Anaṭṭhe naṭṭhasaññā … 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been not lost, but she perceives that it has … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.2.8
Pali: avinaṭṭhe vinaṭṭhasaññā … 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been destroyed, but she perceives that it has … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.2.9
Pali: abhinne bhinnasaññā … 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been broken, but she perceives that it has … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.2.10
Pali: avilutte viluttasaññā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been stolen, but she perceives that it has, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.2.11
Pali: Nissaggiyaṁ pattaṁ anissajjitvā paribhuñjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she uses an almsbowl that should be relinquished without first relinquishing it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.2.12
Pali: Rattānatikkante atikkantasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than one day, but she perceives it as more, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.2.13
Pali: Rattānatikkante vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than one day, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.2.14
Pali: Rattānatikkante anatikkantasaññā anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than one day and she perceives it as less, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.3.2
Pali: antoaruṇe adhiṭṭheti, vikappeti, vissajjeti, nassati, vinassati, bhijjati, acchinditvā gaṇhanti, vissāsaṁ gaṇhanti, 
English (brahmali, published): if, before dawn, it has been determined, assigned to another, given away, lost, destroyed, broken, stolen, or taken on trust; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.3.3
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:2.3.4
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:3.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo nissaṭṭhapattaṁ na denti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the nuns from the group of six did not give back a relinquished bowl. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:3.2
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:3.3
Pali: “Na, bhikkhave, nissaṭṭhapatto na dātabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, a relinquished almsbowl should be given back. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:3.4
Pali: Yā na dadeyya, āpatti dukkaṭassā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If a nun doesn’t give it back, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np1:3.5
Pali: Paṭhamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The first training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:0.2
Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:0.3
Pali: Nissaggiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on relinquishment 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:0.4
Pali: Pattavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on almsbowls 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:0.5
Pali: 2. Akālacīvarabhājanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 2. Distributing out-of-season robe-cloth 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhuniyo gāmakāvāse vassaṁvuṭṭhā sāvatthiṁ agamaṁsu vattasampannā iriyāpathasampannā duccoḷā lūkhacīvarā. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time, after completing the rainy-season residence in a village monastery, a number of nuns were traveling to Sāvatthī. They were perfect in conduct and deportment, but poorly dressed in shabby robes. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.3
Pali: Upāsakā tā bhikkhuniyo passitvā—
English (brahmali, published): Some lay followers who saw them thought, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.4
Pali: “imā bhikkhuniyo vattasampannā iriyāpathasampannā duccoḷā lūkhacīvarā, imā bhikkhuniyo acchinnā bhavissantī”ti bhikkhunisaṅghassa akālacīvaraṁ adaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): “These nuns are perfect in conduct and deportment, but poorly dressed in shabby robes; they must’ve been robbed,” and they gave out-of-season robe-cloth to the Sangha of nuns. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.5
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī—
English (brahmali, published): Because they had performed the robe-making ceremony, the nun Thullanandā 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.6
Pali: “amhākaṁ kathinaṁ atthataṁ kālacīvaran”ti adhiṭṭhahitvā bhājāpesi. 
English (brahmali, published): determined it as in-season robe-cloth, and then distributed it. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.7
Pali: Upāsakā tā bhikkhuniyo passitvā etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The lay followers asked those nuns whether 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.8
Pali: “apayyāhi cīvaraṁ laddhan”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): they had obtained any robe-cloth. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.9
Pali: “Na mayaṁ, āvuso, cīvaraṁ labhāma. 
English (brahmali, published): They replied that they hadn’t 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.10
Pali: Ayyā thullanandā—
English (brahmali, published): and told them what had happened. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.11
Pali: ‘amhākaṁ kathinaṁ atthataṁ kālacīvaran’ti adhiṭṭhahitvā bhājāpesī”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.12
Pali: Upāsakā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): Those lay followers then complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.13
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā akālacīvaraṁ ‘kālacīvaran’ti adhiṭṭhahitvā bhājāpessatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Thullanandā determine out-of-season robe-cloth as ‘in-season’, and then distribute it?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.14
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ upāsakānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns heard the complaints of those lay followers, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.15
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.16
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā akālacīvaraṁ ‘kālacīvaran’ti adhiṭṭhahitvā bhājāpessatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Thullanandā do this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.17
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those nuns then told the monks, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.18
Pali: Bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): who in turn told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.19
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī akālacīvaraṁ ‘kālacīvaran’ti adhiṭṭhahitvā bhājāpetī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.20
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.21
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.22
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī akālacīvaraṁ “kālacīvaran”ti adhiṭṭhahitvā bhājāpessati. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nun Thullanandā do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.23
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.24
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:1.25.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī akālacīvaraṁ ‘kālacīvaran’ti adhiṭṭhahitvā bhājāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun determines out-of-season robe-cloth as “in-season”, and then distributes it, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.1.5
Pali: Akālacīvaraṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Out-of-season robe-cloth: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.1.6
Pali: anatthate kathine ekādasamāse uppannaṁ, atthate kathine sattamāse uppannaṁ, kālepi ādissa dinnaṁ, etaṁ akālacīvaraṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): for one who has not participated in the robe-making ceremony, it is robe-cloth given during the eleven months. For one who has participated in the robe-making ceremony, it is robe-cloth given during the seven months. Also, if it is given in the robe season, but the cloth is designated, it is called “out-of-season robe-cloth”. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.1.7
Pali: Akālacīvaraṁ “kālacīvaran”ti adhiṭṭhahitvā bhājāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If she distributes it after determining the out-of-season robe-cloth as “in-season”, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.1.8
Pali: Paṭilābhena nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When she gets it, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.1.9
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā ekabhikkhuniyā vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The robe-cloth should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.1.10
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.1.11
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bi-vb-np1#2.1.21'>Bi NP 1:2.1.21</a>–2.1.43, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.1.12
Pali: “idaṁ me, ayye, akālacīvaraṁ ‘kālacīvaran’ti adhiṭṭhahitvā bhājāpitaṁ nissaggiyaṁ, imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this out-of-season robe-cloth, which I distributed after determining it as “in-season”, is to be relinquished. I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.1.13
Pali: dadeyyā”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.1.14
Pali: dadeyyun”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.1.15
Pali: ayyāya dammī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this robe-cloth back to you.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.2.1
Pali: Akālacīvare akālacīvarasaññā “kālacīvaran”ti adhiṭṭhahitvā bhājāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is out-of-season robe-cloth and she perceives it as such, and she distributes it after determining it as “in-season”, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.2.2
Pali: Akālacīvare vematikā “kālacīvaran”ti adhiṭṭhahitvā bhājāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is out-of-season robe-cloth, but she is unsure of it, and she distributes it after determining it as “in-season”, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.2.3
Pali: Akālacīvare kālacīvarasaññā “kālacīvaran”ti adhiṭṭhahitvā bhājāpeti, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is out-of-season robe-cloth, but she perceives it as in-season robe-cloth, and she distributes it after determining it as “in-season”, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.2.4
Pali: Kālacīvare akālacīvarasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is in-season robe-cloth, but she perceives it as out-of-season robe-cloth, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.2.5
Pali: Kālacīvare vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is in-season robe-cloth, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.2.6
Pali: Kālacīvare kālacīvarasaññā, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is in-season robe-cloth and she perceives it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.3.2
Pali: akālacīvaraṁ kālacīvarasaññā bhājāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she distributes out-of-season robe-cloth that she perceives as in-season; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.3.3
Pali: kālacīvaraṁ kālacīvarasaññā bhājāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she distributes in-season robe-cloth that she perceives as in-season; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.3.4
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.3.5
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np2:2.3.6
Pali: Dutiyasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The second training rule is finished.

Pali: 3. Cīvaraparivattanasikkhāpada

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī aññatarāya bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ cīvaraṁ parivattetvā paribhuñji. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the nun Thullanandā was using a robe she had received after trading with another nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:1.3
Pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī taṁ cīvaraṁ saṅgharitvā nikkhipi. 
English (brahmali, published): But the other nun folded up the robe she had received and put it aside. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:1.4
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī taṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Thullanandā then said to her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:1.5
Pali: “yaṁ te, ayye, mayā saddhiṁ cīvaraṁ parivattitaṁ, kahaṁ taṁ cīvaran”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, where’s that robe that I traded with you?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:1.6
Pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī taṁ cīvaraṁ nīharitvā thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā dassesi. 
English (brahmali, published): She brought out the robe and showed it to her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:1.7
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī taṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): and Thullanandā said, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:1.8
Pali: “handāyye, tuyhaṁ cīvaraṁ, āhara metaṁ cīvaraṁ, yaṁ tuyhaṁ tuyhamevetaṁ, yaṁ mayhaṁ mayhamevetaṁ, āhara metaṁ, sakaṁ paccāharā”ti acchindi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Here’s your robe and give me that robe of mine. That which is yours is yours and that which is mine is mine. Give me that and take back what’s yours.” And she just took it. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:1.9
Pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): That nun then told the nuns what had happened.

English (brahmali, published): “How could the nun Thullanandā trade a robe with a nun and then take it back? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:1.18
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:1.19
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:1.20.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ cīvaraṁ parivattetvā sā pacchā evaṁ vadeyya—‘handāyye, tuyhaṁ cīvaraṁ āhara, metaṁ cīvaraṁ, yaṁ tuyhaṁ tuyhamevetaṁ, yaṁ mayhaṁ mayhamevetaṁ, āhara metaṁ, sakaṁ paccāharā’ti acchindeyya vā acchindāpeyya vā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun trades robes with a nun and then says, “Here’s your robe; give me that robe of mine. That which is yours is yours, and that which is mine is mine. Give me that, and take back what’s yours;” and she just takes it or has it taken, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhuniyā saddhinti 
English (brahmali, published): With a nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.1.6
Pali: aññāya bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): with another nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.1.7
Pali: Cīvaraṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Robes: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.1.8
Pali: channaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ vikappanupagaṁ pacchimaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one of the six kinds of robe-cloth, but not smaller than what can be assigned to another. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.1.9
Pali: Parivattetvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Trades: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.1.10
Pali: parittena vā vipulaṁ, vipulena vā parittaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): much with little or little with much. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.1.11
Pali: Acchindeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Just takes it: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.1.12
Pali: sayaṁ acchindati nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if she just takes it herself, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.1.13
Pali: Acchindāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Has it taken: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.1.14
Pali: aññaṁ āṇāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she asks another, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.1.15
Pali: Sakiṁ āṇattā bahukampi acchindati, nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): If she only asks once, then even if the other takes back many, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.1.16
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā ekabhikkhuniyā vā. 
English (brahmali, published): The robe-cloth should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.1.17
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.1.18
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bi-vb-np1#2.1.21'>Bi NP 1:2.1.21</a>–2.1.43, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.1.19
Pali: “idaṁ me, ayye, cīvaraṁ bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ parivattetvā acchinnaṁ nissaggiyaṁ, imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this robe-cloth, which I took back after trading it with a nun, is to be relinquished. I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.1.20
Pali: dadeyyā”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.1.21
Pali: dadeyyun”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.1.22
Pali: ayyāya dammī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this robe-cloth back to you.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.2.1
Pali: Upasampannāya upasampannasaññā cīvaraṁ parivattetvā acchindati vā acchindāpeti vā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained and she perceives her as such, and after trading robe-cloth with her she takes it back or has it taken back, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.2.2
Pali: Upasampannāya vematikā cīvaraṁ parivattetvā acchindati vā acchindāpeti vā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, and after trading robe-cloth with her she takes it back or has it taken back, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.2.3
Pali: Upasampannāya anupasampannasaññā cīvaraṁ parivattetvā acchindati vā acchindāpeti vā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, but she does not perceive her as such, and after trading robe-cloth with her she takes it back or has it taken back, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.2.4
Pali: Aññaṁ parikkhāraṁ parivattetvā acchindati vā acchindāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she trades another requisite, and then takes it back or has it taken back, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.2.5
Pali: Anupasampannāya saddhiṁ cīvaraṁ vā aññaṁ vā parikkhāraṁ parivattetvā acchindati vā acchindāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she trades robe-cloth or another requisite with someone who is not fully ordained, and then takes it back or has it taken back, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.2.6
Pali: Anupasampannāya upasampannasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but she perceives her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.2.7
Pali: Anupasampannāya vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.2.8
Pali: Anupasampannāya anupasampannasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, and she does not perceive her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.3.2
Pali: sā vā deti, 
English (brahmali, published): if the other nun gives it back; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.3.3
Pali: tassā vā vissasantī gaṇhāti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she takes it on trust from her; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.3.4
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.3.5
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np3:2.3.6
Pali: Tatiyasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The third training rule is finished.

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on relinquishment

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on almsbowls 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:0.5
Pali: 4. Aññaviññāpanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 4. Asking for something else 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī gilānā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): the nun Thullanandā was sick. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.3
Pali: Atha kho aññataro upāsako yena thullanandā bhikkhunī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): A lay follower went to her and asked, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.4
Pali: “kiṁ te, ayye, aphāsu, kiṁ āharīyatū”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, what’s wrong with you? What may I get you?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.5
Pali: “Sappinā me, āvuso, attho”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I need ghee.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.6
Pali: Atha kho so upāsako aññatarassa āpaṇikassa gharā kahāpaṇassa sappiṁ āharitvā thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā adāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): That lay follower then brought back from a shop a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>kahāpaṇa</i>’s worth of ghee and gave it to Thullanandā. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.7
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī evamāha—
English (brahmali, published): Thullanandā said, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.8
Pali: “na me, āvuso, sappinā attho; 
English (brahmali, published): “I don’t need ghee; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.9
Pali: telena me attho”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I need oil.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.10
Pali: Atha kho so upāsako yena so āpaṇiko tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ āpaṇikaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): The lay follower returned to the shopkeeper and said, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.11
Pali: “na kirāyyo, ayyāya sappinā attho, telena attho. 
English (brahmali, published): “It seems the nun doesn’t need ghee, but oil. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.12
Pali: Handa te sappiṁ, telaṁ me dehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Here’s your ghee; please give me oil.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.13
Pali: “Sace mayaṁ, ayyo, vikkītaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ puna ādiyissāma, kadā amhākaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ vikkāyissati; 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, if we were to take back goods that have been sold, when would our goods be sold? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.14
Pali: sappissa kayena sappi haṭaṁ, telassa kayaṁ āhara, telaṁ harissasī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): When ghee is bought, ghee is taken away. Buying oil, you receive that, and you’ll take that away.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.15
Pali: Atha kho so upāsako ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—
English (brahmali, published): That lay follower then complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.16
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā aññaṁ viññāpetvā aññaṁ viññāpessatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Thullanandā ask for one thing and then for something else?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.17
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tassa upāsakassa ujjhāyantassa khiyyantassa vipācentassa. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns heard the complaints of that lay follower, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.18
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.19
Pali: atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those nuns then told the monks, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.20
Pali: Bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): who in turn told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.21
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī aññaṁ viññāpetvā aññaṁ viññāpetī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.22
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.23
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.24
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī aññaṁ viññāpetvā aññaṁ viññāpessati. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nun Thullanandā ask for one thing and then for something else? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.25
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.26
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:1.27.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī aññaṁ viññāpetvā aññaṁ viññāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun asks for one thing and then for something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.1.5
Pali: Aññaṁ viññāpetvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Asks for one thing: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.1.6
Pali: yaṁ kiñci viññāpetvā. 
English (brahmali, published): whatever she asks for. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.1.7
Pali: Aññaṁ viññāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Then for something else: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.1.8
Pali: taṁ ṭhapetvā aññaṁ viññāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): apart from that thing, if she asks for something else, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.1.9
Pali: Paṭilābhena nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When she gets it, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.1.10
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā ekabhikkhuniyā vā. 
English (brahmali, published): It should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.1.11
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.1.12
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bi-vb-np1#2.1.21'>Bi NP 1:2.1.21</a>–2.1.43, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.1.13
Pali: “idaṁ me, ayye, aññaṁ viññāpetvā aññaṁ viññāpitaṁ nissaggiyaṁ, imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this thing, which I asked for after asking for something else, is to be relinquished. I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.1.14
Pali: dadeyyā”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.1.15
Pali: dadeyyun”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.1.16
Pali: ayyāya dammī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this back to you.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.2.1
Pali: Aññe aññasaññā aññaṁ viññāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is something else and she perceives it as such, and she asks for that, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.2.2
Pali: Aññe vematikā aññaṁ viññāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is something else, but she is unsure of it, and she asks for that, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.2.3
Pali: Aññe anaññasaññā aññaṁ viññāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is something else, but she does not perceive it as such, and she asks for that, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.2.4
Pali: Anaññe aññasaññā anaññaṁ viññāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not something else, but she perceives it as such, and she asks for that, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.2.5
Pali: Anaññe vematikā anaññaṁ viññāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not something else, but she is unsure of it, and she asks for that, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.2.6
Pali: Anaññe anaññasaññā, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not something else, and she does not perceive it as such, and she asks for that, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.3.2
Pali: taññeva viññāpeti, aññañca viññāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she asks for both at the same time; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.3.3
Pali: ānisaṁsaṁ dassetvā viññāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she can show a benefit in asking; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.3.4
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.3.5
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np4:2.3.6
Pali: Catutthasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The fourth training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:1.1

English (brahmali, published): the nun Thullanandā was sick. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:1.3

English (brahmali, published): A lay follower went to her and asked, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:1.4
Pali: “kacci, ayye, khamanīyaṁ kacci yāpanīyan”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “I hope you’re bearing up, venerable, I hope you’re getting better?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:1.5
Pali: “Na me, āvuso, khamanīyaṁ, na yāpanīyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I’m not bearing up, and I’m not getting better.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:1.6
Pali: “Amukassa, ayye, āpaṇikassa ghare kahāpaṇaṁ nikkhipissāmi, tato yaṁ iccheyyāsi taṁ āharāpeyyāsī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “We’ll deposit a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>kahāpaṇa</i> coin in such-and-such a shop. Please get whatever you wish from there.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:1.7
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī aññataraṁ sikkhamānaṁ āṇāpesi—
English (brahmali, published): Thullanandā then said to a trainee nun, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:1.8
Pali: “gaccha, sikkhamāne, amukassa āpaṇikassa gharā kahāpaṇassa telaṁ āharā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Go to such-and-such a shop and bring back a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>kahāpaṇa</i>’s worth of oil.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:1.9

English (brahmali, published): That trainee nun did just that and gave it to Thullanandā. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:1.10

English (brahmali, published): Thullanandā said, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:1.11

English (brahmali, published): “I don’t need oil; I need ghee.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:1.12

English (brahmali, published): The trainee nun returned to the shopkeeper and said, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:1.13
Pali: “na kira, āvuso, ayyāya telena attho, sappinā attho, handa te telaṁ, sappiṁ me dehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It seems the nun doesn’t need oil, but ghee. Here’s your oil; please give me ghee.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:1.14
Pali: “Sace mayaṁ, ayye, vikkītaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ puna ādiyissāma, kadā amhākaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ vikkāyissati. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, if we were to take back goods that have been sold, when would our goods be sold? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:1.15
Pali: Telassa kayena telaṁ haṭaṁ, sappissa kayaṁ āhara, sappiṁ harissasī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): When oil is bought, oil is taken away. Buying ghee, you receive that, and you’ll take that away.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:1.16
Pali: Atha kho sā sikkhamānā rodantī aṭṭhāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): The trainee nun started to cry. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:1.17
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo taṁ sikkhamānaṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns asked her 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:1.18
Pali: “kissa tvaṁ, sikkhamāne, rodasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): why, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:1.19
Pali: Atha kho sā sikkhamānā bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): and she told them what had happened. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:1.20
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:1.21
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā aññaṁ cetāpetvā aññaṁ cetāpessatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Thullanandā get one thing in exchange and then something else?” …

English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:1.28.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī aññaṁ cetāpetvā aññaṁ cetāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun gets one thing in exchange and then something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.5
Pali: Aññaṁ cetāpetvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Gets one thing in exchange: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.6
Pali: yaṁ kiñci cetāpetvā. 
English (brahmali, published): whatever she gets in exchange. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.7
Pali: Aññaṁ cetāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Then something else: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.8
Pali: taṁ ṭhapetvā aññaṁ cetāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): apart from that thing, if she gets something else in exchange, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.9
Pali: Paṭilābhena nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When she gets it, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.10
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā ekabhikkhuniyā vā. 
English (brahmali, published): It should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.11
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.12
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bi-vb-np1#2.1.21'>Bi NP 1:2.1.21</a>–2.1.43, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.13
Pali: “idaṁ me, ayye, aññaṁ cetāpetvā aññaṁ cetāpitaṁ nissaggiyaṁ, imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this thing, which I got in exchange, having first gotten something else in exchange, is to be relinquished. I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.14
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.15
Pali: dadeyyunti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.16
Pali: ayyāya dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this back to you.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.17.1
Pali: Aññe aññasaññā aññaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is something else and she perceives it as such, and she gets that in exchange, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.18
Pali: Aññe vematikā aññaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is something else, but she is unsure of it, and she gets that in exchange, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.19
Pali: Aññe anaññasaññā aññaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is something else, but she does not perceive it as such, and she gets that in exchange, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.20
Pali: Anaññe aññasaññā anaññaṁ cetāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not something else, but she perceives it as such, and she gets that in exchange, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.21
Pali: Anaññe vematikā anaññaṁ cetāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not something else, but she is unsure of it, and she gets that in exchange, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.22
Pali: Anaññe anaññasaññā anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not something else, and she does not perceive it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.23.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.24
Pali: taññeva cetāpeti, aññañca cetāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she gets both in exchange at the same time; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.25
Pali: ānisaṁsaṁ dassetvā cetāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she can show a benefit in doing the exchange; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.26
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.27
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np5:2.28
Pali: Pañcamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The fifth training rule is finished.

Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on relinquishment 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:0.4

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on almsbowls 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:0.5
Pali: 6. Saṅghikacetapanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 6. Exchanging designated Sangha property 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena upāsakā bhikkhunisaṅghassa cīvaratthāya chandakaṁ saṅgharitvā aññatarassa pāvārikassa ghare parikkhāraṁ nikkhipitvā bhikkhuniyo upasaṅkamitvā etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): At that time the lay followers collected voluntary contributions to supply the Sangha of nuns with robes. They stored the requisites in a cloth merchant’s shop, and then went to the nuns and said, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:1.3
Pali: “amukassa, ayye, pāvārikassa ghare cīvaratthāya parikkhāro nikkhitto, tato cīvaraṁ āharāpetvā bhājethā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, requisites to be used for robes are stored in such-and-such a cloth merchant’s shop. Please have someone get cloth from there and share it out.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:1.4
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo tena parikkhārena bhesajjaṁ cetāpetvā paribhuñjiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): But the nuns exchanged those requisites for tonics, which they then used. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:1.5
Pali: Upāsakā jānitvā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): When the lay followers found out about this, they complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:1.6
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena saṅghikena aññaṁ cetāpessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “When requisites belonging to the Sangha are designated for a specific purpose, how could the nuns exchange them for something else?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:1.7
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ upāsakānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns heard the complaints of those lay followers, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:1.8
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:1.9
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena saṅghikena aññaṁ cetāpessantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “When requisites belonging to the Sangha are designated for a specific purpose, how could the nuns exchange them for something else?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:1.10
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena saṅghikena aññaṁ cetāpentī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the nuns did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:1.11
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:1.12
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:1.13

English (brahmali, published): “When requisites belonging to the Sangha are designated for a specific purpose, how could the nuns exchange them for something else?

English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” …

English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:1.16.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena saṅghikena aññaṁ cetāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘When a requisite belonging to the Sangha is designated for a specific purpose, if a nun exchanges it for something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’”

Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A:

Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.1.5
Pali: Aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikenāti 
English (brahmali, published): A requisite is designated for a specific purpose: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.1.6
Pali: aññassatthāya dinnena. 
English (brahmali, published): it was given for a specific purpose. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.1.7
Pali: Saṅghikenāti 
English (brahmali, published): Belonging to the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.1.8
Pali: saṅghassa, na gaṇassa, na ekabhikkhuniyā. 
English (brahmali, published): belonging to the Sangha, not to a group, not to an individual nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.1.9
Pali: Aññaṁ cetāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Exchanges it for something else: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.1.10
Pali: yaṁatthāya dinnaṁ, taṁ ṭhapetvā aññaṁ cetāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if, apart from the purpose for which it was given, she exchanges it for something else, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.1.11
Pali: Paṭilābhena nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When she gets it, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.1.12
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā ekabhikkhuniyā vā. 
English (brahmali, published): It should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.1.13
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.1.14
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bi-vb-np1#2.1.21'>Bi NP 1:2.1.21</a>–2.1.43, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.1.15
Pali: “idaṁ me, ayye, aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena saṅghikena aññaṁ cetāpitaṁ nissaggiyaṁ, imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this thing, which I got in exchange for a requisite belonging to the Sangha that was designated for a specific purpose, is to be relinquished. I relinquish it to the Sangha.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.1.16
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.1.17
Pali: dadeyyā”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.1.18
Pali: dadeyyun”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.1.19
Pali: ayyāya dammī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this back to you.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.2.1
Pali: Aññadatthike aññadatthikasaññā aññaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is for a specific purpose and she perceives that it is, and she exchanges it for something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.2.2
Pali: Aññadatthike vematikā aññaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is for a specific purpose, but she is unsure of it, and she exchanges it for something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.2.3
Pali: Aññadatthike anaññadatthikasaññā aññaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is for a specific purpose, but she does not perceive that it is, and she exchanges it for something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.2.4
Pali: Nissaṭṭhaṁ paṭilabhitvā yathādāne upanetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When she receives in return what had been relinquished, it is to be used in accordance with the intention of the donors. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.2.5
Pali: Anaññadatthike aññadatthikasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not for a specific purpose, but she perceives that it is, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.2.6
Pali: Anaññadatthike vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not for a specific purpose, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.2.7
Pali: Anaññadatthike anaññadatthikasaññā, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not for a specific purpose, and she does not perceive that it is, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.3.2
Pali: sesakaṁ upaneti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she uses the remainder; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.3.3
Pali: sāmike apaloketvā upaneti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she uses it after getting permission from the owners; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.3.4.1
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.3.4.2
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.3.5
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np6:2.3.6
Pali: Chaṭṭhasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The sixth training rule is finished.

English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.5
Pali: Aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikenāti 
English (brahmali, published): A requisite is designated for a specific purpose: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.6
Pali: aññassatthāya dinnena. 
English (brahmali, published): it was given for a specific purpose. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.7
Pali: Saṅghikenāti 
English (brahmali, published): Belonging to the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.8
Pali: saṅghassa, na gaṇassa, na ekabhikkhuniyā. 
English (brahmali, published): belonging to the Sangha, not to a group, not to an individual nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.9
Pali: Saññācikenāti 
English (brahmali, published): Was asked for: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.10
Pali: sayaṁ yācitvā. 
English (brahmali, published): that she herself had asked for. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.11
Pali: Aññaṁ cetāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Exchanges it for something else: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.12
Pali: yaṁatthāya dinnaṁ taṁ ṭhapetvā aññaṁ cetāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if, apart from the purpose for which it was given, she exchanges it for something else, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.13
Pali: Paṭilābhena nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When she gets it, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.14
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā ekabhikkhuniyā vā. 
English (brahmali, published): It should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.15
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.16
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bi-vb-np1#2.1.21'>Bi NP 1:2.1.21</a>–2.1.43, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.17
Pali: “idaṁ me, ayye, aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena saṅghikena saññācikena aññaṁ cetāpitaṁ nissaggiyaṁ, imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this thing, which I got in exchange for a requisite belonging to the Sangha that was designated for a specific purpose and had been asked for, is to be relinquished. I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.18
Pali: dadeyyā”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.19
Pali: dadeyyun”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.20
Pali: ayyāya dammī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this back to you.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.21.1
Pali: Aññadatthike aññadatthikasaññā aññaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is for a specific purpose and she perceives that it is, and she exchanges it for something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.22
Pali: Aññadatthike vematikā aññaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is for a specific purpose, but she is unsure of it, and she exchanges it for something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.23
Pali: Aññadatthike anaññadatthikasaññā aññaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is for a specific purpose, but she does not perceive that it is, and she exchanges it for something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.24
Pali: Nissaṭṭhaṁ paṭilabhitvā yathādāne upanetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When she receives in return what had been relinquished, it is to be used in accordance with the intention of the donors. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.25
Pali: Anaññadatthike aññadatthikasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not for a specific purpose, but she perceives that it is, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.26
Pali: Anaññadatthike vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not for a specific purpose, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.27
Pali: Anaññadatthike anaññadatthikasaññā, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not for a specific purpose, and she does not perceive that it is, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.28.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.29
Pali: sesakaṁ upaneti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she uses the remainder; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.30
Pali: sāmike apaloketvā upaneti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she uses it after getting permission from the owners; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.31
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.32
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.33
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np7:2.34
Pali: Sattamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The seventh training rule is finished.

English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on relinquishment 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np8:0.4

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on almsbowls 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np8:0.5
Pali: 8. Gaṇikacetāpanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 8. Exchanging designated collective property 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np8:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np8:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarassa pūgassa pariveṇavāsikā bhikkhuniyo yāguyā kilamanti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the nuns staying in the yard belonging to a certain association were weak because of a lack of congee. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np8:1.3
Pali: Atha kho so pūgo bhikkhunīnaṁ yāguatthāya chandakaṁ saṅgharitvā aññatarassa āpaṇikassa ghare parikkhāraṁ nikkhipitvā bhikkhuniyo upasaṅkamitvā etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Then, after collecting voluntary contributions to supply the nuns with congee, that association stored the ingredients in a shop. They then went to the nuns and said, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np8:1.4
Pali: “amukassa, ayye, āpaṇikassa ghare yāguatthāya parikkhāro nikkhitto, tato taṇḍulaṁ āharāpetvā yāguṁ pacāpetvā paribhuñjathā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, congee ingredients are stored in such-and-such a shop. Please have someone get rice from there, have congee cooked, and then eat it.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np8:1.5
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo tena parikkhārena bhesajjaṁ cetāpetvā paribhuñjiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): But the nuns exchanged those ingredients for tonics, which they then used. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np8:1.6
Pali: So pūgo jānitvā ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—
English (brahmali, published): When that association found out about this, they complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np8:1.7
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena mahājanikena aññaṁ cetāpessantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “When collective ingredients are designated for a specific purpose, how could the nuns exchange them for something else?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np8:1.8

English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the nuns did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np8:1.9
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti.

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.5
Pali: Aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikenāti 
English (brahmali, published): A requisite is designated for a specific purpose: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.6
Pali: aññassatthāya dinnena. 
English (brahmali, published): it was given for a specific purpose. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.7
Pali: Mahājanikenāti 
English (brahmali, published): Collective: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.8
Pali: gaṇassa, na saṅghassa, na ekabhikkhuniyā. 
English (brahmali, published): belonging to a group, not to the Sangha, not to an individual nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.9
Pali: Saññācikenāti 
English (brahmali, published): Was asked for: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.10
Pali: sayaṁ yācitvā. 
English (brahmali, published): that she herself had asked for. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.11
Pali: Aññaṁ cetāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Exchanges it for something else: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.12
Pali: yaṁatthāya dinnaṁ taṁ ṭhapetvā aññaṁ cetāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if, apart from the purpose for which it was given, she exchanges it for something else, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.13
Pali: Paṭilābhena nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When she gets it, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.14
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā ekabhikkhuniyā vā. 
English (brahmali, published): It should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.15
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.16
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bi-vb-np1#2.1.21'>Bi NP 1:2.1.21</a>–2.1.43, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.17
Pali: “idaṁ me, ayye, aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena mahājanikena saññācikena aññaṁ cetāpitaṁ nissaggiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this thing, which I got in exchange for a collective requisite that was designated for a specific purpose and had been asked for, is to be relinquished. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.18
Pali: Imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.19
Pali: dadeyyā”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.20
Pali: dadeyyun”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.21
Pali: ayyāya dammī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this back to you.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.22.1
Pali: Aññadatthike aññadatthikasaññā aññaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is for a specific purpose and she perceives that it is, and she exchanges it for something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.23
Pali: Aññadatthike vematikā aññaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is for a specific purpose, but she is unsure of it, and she exchanges it for something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.24
Pali: Aññadatthike anaññadatthikasaññā aññaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is for a specific purpose, but she does not perceive that it is, and she exchanges it for something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.25
Pali: Nissaṭṭhaṁ paṭilabhitvā yathādāne upanetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When she receives in return what had been relinquished, it is to be used in accordance with the intention of the donors. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.26
Pali: Anaññadatthike aññadatthikasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not for a specific purpose, but she perceives that it is, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.27
Pali: Anaññadatthike vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not for a specific purpose, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.28
Pali: Anaññadatthike anaññadatthikasaññā anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not for a specific purpose, and she does not perceive that it is, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.29.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.30
Pali: sesakaṁ upaneti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she uses the remainder; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.31
Pali: sāmike apaloketvā upaneti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she uses it after getting permission from the owners; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.32
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.33
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.34
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np9:2.35
Pali: Navamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The ninth training rule is finished.

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on relinquishment

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on almsbowls 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np10:0.5
Pali: 10. Puggalikacetāpanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 10. Exchanging designated personal property that was asked for 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np10:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np10:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī bahussutā hoti bhāṇikā visāradā paṭṭā dhammiṁ kathaṁ kātuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the nun Thullanandā was a learned reciter, and she was confident and skilled at giving teachings. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np10:1.3
Pali: Bahū manussā thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ payirupāsanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Many people visited her. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np10:1.4
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā pariveṇaṁ undriyati. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then the yard of Thullanandā’s dwelling was deteriorating. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np10:1.5
Pali: Manussā thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): People asked her why, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np10:1.6
Pali: “kissidaṁ te, ayye, pariveṇaṁ undriyatī”ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np10:1.7
Pali: “Natthāvuso, dāyakā, natthi kārakā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and she replied, “I have neither donor nor worker.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np10:1.8
Pali: Atha kho te manussā thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā pariveṇatthāya chandakaṁ saṅgharitvā thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā parikkhāraṁ adaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Then, after collecting voluntary contributions for the yard of Thullanandā’s dwelling, the people gave the collected requisites to Thullanandā. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np10:1.9
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī tena ca parikkhārena sayampi yācitvā bhesajjaṁ cetāpetvā paribhuñji. 
English (brahmali, published): But even though she had asked for them, Thullanandā exchanged those requisites for tonics, which she then used. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np10:1.10
Pali: Manussā jānitvā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): When the people found out about this, they complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np10:1.11
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena puggalikena saññācikena aññaṁ cetāpessatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “When personal requisites are designated for a specific purpose and were asked for, how could the nun Thullanandā exchange them for something else?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np10:1.12
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena puggalikena saññācikena aññaṁ cetāpetī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np10:1.13
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np10:1.14
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np10:1.15

English (brahmali, published): “When personal requisites are designated for a specific purpose and were asked for, how could the nun Thullanandā exchange them for something else? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np10:1.16
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np10:1.17
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np10:1.18.1
