English (brahmali, published): ‘When a personal requisite is designated for a specific purpose and was asked for, if a nun exchanges it for something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’”

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11
Root: root/pli/ms/vinaya/pli-tv-bi-vb/pli-tv-bi-vb-np/pli-tv-bi-vb-np11_root-pli-ms.json

English translation sources:
  - brahmali [published]: translation/en/brahmali/vinaya/pli-tv-bi-vb/pli-tv-bi-vb-np/pli-tv-bi-vb-np11_translation-en-brahmali.json

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:0.2
Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:0.3
Pali: Nissaggiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on relinquishment 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:0.4
Pali: Cīvaravagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on robes 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:0.5
Pali: 11. Garupāvuraṇasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 11. Getting an expensive heavy cloak in exchange 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī bahussutā hoti bhāṇikā visāradā paṭṭā dhammiṁ kathaṁ kātuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the nun Thullanandā was a learned reciter, and she was confident and skilled at giving teachings. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:1.3
Pali: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo sītakāle mahagghaṁ kambalaṁ pārupitvā yena thullanandā bhikkhunī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion when the weather was cold, King Pasenadi of Kosala put on an expensive woolen cloak and went to Thullanandā. He bowed and sat down. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:1.4
Pali: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho rājānaṁ pasenadiṁ kosalaṁ thullanandā bhikkhunī dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi samādapesi samuttejesi sampahaṁsesi. 
English (brahmali, published): And Thullanandā instructed, inspired, and gladdened him with a teaching. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:1.5
Pali: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā dhammiyā kathāya sandassito samādapito samuttejito sampahaṁsito thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): He then said, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:1.6
Pali: “vadeyyāsi, ayye, yena attho”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, please say what you need.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:1.7
Pali: “Sace me tvaṁ, mahārāja, dātukāmosi, imaṁ kambalaṁ dehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Great king, if you wish to give me something, then give me this woolen cloak.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:1.8
Pali: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā kambalaṁ datvā uṭṭhāyāsanā thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): The king gave her his cloak. He then got up from his seat, bowed down, circumambulated her with his right side toward her, and left. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:1.9
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:1.10
Pali: “mahicchā imā bhikkhuniyo asantuṭṭhā. 
English (brahmali, published): “These nuns have great desires; they are not content. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:1.11
Pali: Kathañhi nāma rājānaṁ kambalaṁ viññāpessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): How can they ask the king for his woolen cloak?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:1.12
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:1.13
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:1.14
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā rājānaṁ kambalaṁ viññāpessatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Thullanandā ask the king for his woolen cloak?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:1.15
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī rājānaṁ kambalaṁ viññāpetī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā asked for this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:1.16
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:1.17
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:1.18
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī rājānaṁ kambalaṁ viññāpessati. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nun Thullanandā ask the king for his woolen cloak? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:1.19
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:1.20
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:1.21.1
Pali: “Garupāvuraṇaṁ pana bhikkhuniyā cetāpentiyā catukkaṁsaparamaṁ cetāpetabbaṁ. Tato ce uttari cetāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun carries out an exchange to get a heavy cloak, it is to be worth at most four <i lang='pi' translate='no'>kaṁsa</i> coins. If she gets one in exchange that is worth more than that, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.1.1
Pali: Garupāvuraṇaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A heavy cloak: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.1.2
Pali: yaṁ kiñci sītakāle pāvuraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): whatever cloak is used in cold weather. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.1.3
Pali: Cetāpentiyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Carries out an exchange to get: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.1.4
Pali: viññāpentiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): asks for. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.1.5
Pali: Catukkaṁsaparamaṁ cetāpetabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): It is to be worth at most four <i lang='pi' translate='no'>kaṁsa</i> coins: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.1.6
Pali: soḷasakahāpaṇagghanakaṁ cetāpetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): it may be worth sixteen <i lang='pi' translate='no'>kahāpaṇa</i> coins. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.1.7
Pali: Tato ce uttari cetāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): If she gets one in exchange that is worth more than that: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.1.8
Pali: tatuttari viññāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if she asks for one worth more than that, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.1.9
Pali: Paṭilābhena nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): When she gets it, it becomes subject to relinquishment. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.1.10
Pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā ekabhikkhuniyā vā. 
English (brahmali, published): It should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.1.11
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, nissajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.1.12
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as in <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-bi-vb-np1#2.1.21'>Bi NP 1:2.1.21</a>–2.1.43, with appropriate substitutions.) 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.1.13
Pali: “idaṁ me, ayye, garupāvuraṇaṁ atirekacatukkaṁsaparamaṁ cetāpitaṁ nissaggiyaṁ, imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this heavy cloak worth more than four <i lang='pi' translate='no'>kaṁsa</i> coins, which I got in exchange, is to be relinquished. I relinquish it to the Sangha.” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.1.14
Pali: dadeyyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “the Sangha should give …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.1.15
Pali: dadeyyunti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “you should give …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.1.16
Pali: ayyāya dammīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I give this back to you.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.2.1
Pali: Atirekacatukkaṁse atirekasaññā cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is worth more than four <i lang='pi' translate='no'>kaṁsa</i> coins, and she perceives it as such, and she gets it in exchange, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.2.2
Pali: Atirekacatukkaṁse vematikā cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is worth more than four <i lang='pi' translate='no'>kaṁsa</i> coins, but she is unsure of it, and she gets it in exchange, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.2.3
Pali: Atirekacatukkaṁse ūnakasaññā cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is worth more than four <i lang='pi' translate='no'>kaṁsa</i> coins, but she perceives it as being worth less, and she gets it in exchange, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.2.4
Pali: Ūnakacatukkaṁse atirekasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is worth less than four <i lang='pi' translate='no'>kaṁsa</i> coins, but she perceives it as being worth more, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.2.5
Pali: Ūnakacatukkaṁse vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is worth less than four <i lang='pi' translate='no'>kaṁsa</i> coins, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.2.6
Pali: Ūnakacatukkaṁse ūnakasaññā, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is worth less than four <i lang='pi' translate='no'>kaṁsa</i> coins, and she perceives it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.3.2
Pali: catukkaṁsaparamaṁ cetāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she carries out an exchange for one worth at most four <i lang='pi' translate='no'>kaṁsa</i> coins; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.3.3
Pali: ūnakacatukkaṁsaparamaṁ cetāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she carries out an exchange for one worth less than four <i lang='pi' translate='no'>kaṁsa</i> coins; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.3.4
Pali: ñātakānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from relatives; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.3.5
Pali: pavāritānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from those who have given an invitation; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.3.6
Pali: aññassatthāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is for the benefit of someone else; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.3.7
Pali: attano dhanena, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is by means of her own property; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.3.8
Pali: mahagghaṁ cetāpetukāmassa appagghaṁ cetāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she gets one in exchange that has little value from someone who wants to exchange one of great value; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.3.9
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.3.10
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np11:2.3.11
Pali: Ekādasamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The eleventh training rule is finished.

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on robes 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:0.5
Pali: 12. Lahupāvuraṇasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 12. Getting an expensive light cloak in exchange 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī bahussutā hoti bhāṇikā visāradā paṭṭā dhammiṁ kathaṁ kātuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the nun Thullanandā was a learned reciter, and she was confident and skilled at giving teachings. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:1.3
Pali: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo uṇhakāle mahagghaṁ khomaṁ pārupitvā yena thullanandā bhikkhunī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion when the weather was warm, King Pasenadi of Kosala put on an expensive linen cloak and went to Thullanandā. He bowed and sat down. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:1.4
Pali: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho rājānaṁ pasenadiṁ kosalaṁ thullanandā bhikkhunī dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi samādapesi samuttejesi sampahaṁsesi. 
English (brahmali, published): And Thullanandā instructed, inspired, and gladdened him with a teaching. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:1.5
Pali: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā dhammiyā kathāya sandassito samādapito samuttejito sampahaṁsito thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): He then said, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:1.6
Pali: “vadeyyāsi, ayye, yena attho”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, please say what you need.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:1.7
Pali: “Sace me tvaṁ, mahārāja, dātukāmosi, imaṁ khomaṁ dehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Great king, if you wish to give me something, then give me this linen cloak.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:1.8
Pali: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā khomaṁ datvā uṭṭhāyāsanā thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): The king gave her his cloak. He then got up from his seat, bowed down, circumambulated her with his right side toward her, and left. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:1.9
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:1.10
Pali: “mahicchā imā bhikkhuniyo asantuṭṭhā. 
English (brahmali, published): “These nuns have great desires; they are not content. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:1.11
Pali: Kathañhi nāma rājānaṁ khomaṁ viññāpessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): How can they ask the king for his linen cloak?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:1.12
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns heard the complaints of those people,

English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun carries out an exchange to get a light cloak, it is to be worth at most two-and-a-half <i lang='pi' translate='no'>kaṁsa</i> coins. If she gets one in exchange that is worth more than that, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’”

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:2.2.11

English (brahmali, published): The twelfth training rule is finished. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:2.2.12.0
Pali: (Dutiya) Pattavagga 
English: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:2.2.13.0
Pali: 10 Pariṇatasikkhāpada 
English: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:2.2.14
Pali: Uddiṭṭhā kho, ayyāyo, tiṁsa nissaggiyā pācittiyā dhammā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, the thirty rules on relinquishment and confession have been recited. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:2.2.15
Pali: Tatthāyyāyo pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): In regard to this I ask you, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:2.2.16
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:2.2.17
Pali: Dutiyampi pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): A second time I ask, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:2.2.18
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:2.2.19
Pali: Tatiyampi pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): A third time I ask, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:2.2.20
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:2.2.21
Pali: Parisuddhetthāyyāyo, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmīti. 
English (brahmali, published): You are pure in this and therefore silent. I will remember it thus.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-np12:2.2.22
Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅge nissaggiyakaṇḍaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing relinquishment in the Nuns’ Analysis is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:0.6
Pali: Ime kho panāyyāyo chasaṭṭhisatā pācittiyā dhammā uddesaṁ āgacchanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerables, these one hundred and sixty-six rules on confession come up for recitation. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarena upāsakena bhikkhunisaṅgho lasuṇena pavārito hoti—
English (brahmali, published): At that time a lay follower had invited the Sangha of nuns to ask for garlic: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.3
Pali: “yāsaṁ ayyānaṁ lasuṇena attho, ahaṁ lasuṇenā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “If any of the nuns need garlic, I’ll supply it.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.4
Pali: Khettapālo ca āṇatto hoti—
English (brahmali, published): And he had told his field-keeper, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.5
Pali: “sace bhikkhuniyo āgacchanti, ekamekāya bhikkhuniyā dvetayo bhaṇḍike dehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “If the nuns come, give two or three bulbs to each nun.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.6
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sāvatthiyaṁ ussavo hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): On that occasion they were holding a celebration in Sāvatthī, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.7
Pali: Yathābhataṁ lasuṇaṁ parikkhayaṁ agamāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): and the garlic was used up as soon as it arrived there. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.8
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo taṁ upāsakaṁ upasaṅkamitvā etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): Just then the nuns went to that lay follower and said, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.9
Pali: “lasuṇena, āvuso, attho”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “We need garlic.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.10
Pali: “Natthāyye. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, there isn’t any. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.11
Pali: Yathābhataṁ lasuṇaṁ parikkhīṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): As soon as the garlic arrives, it’s used up. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.12
Pali: Khettaṁ gacchathā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Please go to the field.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.13
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī khettaṁ gantvā na mattaṁ jānitvā bahuṁ lasuṇaṁ harāpesi. 
English (brahmali, published): The nun Thullanandā went to the field, and not having any sense of moderation she took a large amount of garlic. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.14
Pali: Khettapālo ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—
English (brahmali, published): The field-keeper complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.15
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo na mattaṁ jānitvā bahuṁ lasuṇaṁ harāpessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nuns not have any sense of moderation and take a large amount of garlic?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.16
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tassa khettapālassa ujjhāyantassa khiyyantassa vipācentassa. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns heard the complaints of that field-keeper, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.17
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.18

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.19

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.20
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.21
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her,

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.23
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.24
Pali: dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): and after giving a teaching he addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.25.1

English (brahmali, published): “Once upon a time, monks, Thullanandā was the wife of a brahmin. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.26

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.27

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.28
Pali: Atha kho, bhikkhave, so brāhmaṇo kālaṁ katvā aññataraṁ haṁsayoniṁ upapajji. 
English (brahmali, published): When that brahmin eventually died, he was reborn as a goose, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.29
Pali: Tassa sabbasovaṇṇamayā pattā ahesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): whose feathers were all made of gold. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.30
Pali: So tāsaṁ ekekaṁ pattaṁ deti. 
English (brahmali, published): And he gave his former family members one feather at a time. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.31
Pali: Atha kho, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī ‘ayaṁ haṁso amhākaṁ ekekaṁ pattaṁ detī’ti taṁ haṁsarājaṁ gahetvā nippattaṁ akāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): Thullanandā considered this. She then grabbed hold of that king of geese and plucked him. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.32
Pali: Tassa puna jāyamānā pattā setā sampajjiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): But when his feathers regrew they were white. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.33
Pali: Tadāpi, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī atilobhena suvaṇṇā parihīnā. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time too, monks, Thullanandā lost her gold because she was too greedy. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.34
Pali: Idāni lasuṇā parihāyissatīti. 
English (brahmali, published): Now she will lose her garlic.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.35
Pali: Yaṁ laddhaṁ tena tuṭṭhabbaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “One should be content with what one gets, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.36
Pali: Atilobho hi pāpako; 
English (brahmali, published): Because excessive greed is bad. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.37
Pali: Haṁsarājaṁ gahetvāna, 
English (brahmali, published): After grabbing hold of the king of geese, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.38
Pali: Suvaṇṇā parihāyathā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The gold came to an end.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.39
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā dubbharatāya …pe…

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.40

English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:1.41.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī lasuṇaṁ khādeyya pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun eats garlic, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.1.5
Pali: Lasuṇaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Garlic: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.1.6
Pali: māgadhakaṁ vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): from Magadha is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.1.7
Pali: “Khādissāmīti paṭiggaṇhā”ti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she receives it with the intention of eating it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.1.8
Pali: Ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For every mouthful swallowed, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.2.1
Pali: Lasuṇe lasuṇasaññā khādati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is garlic and she perceives it as such, and she eats it, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.2.2
Pali: Lasuṇe vematikā khādati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is garlic, but she is unsure of it, and she eats it, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.2.3
Pali: Lasuṇe alasuṇasaññā khādati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is garlic, but she does not perceive it as such, and she eats it, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.2.4
Pali: Alasuṇe lasuṇasaññā khādati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not garlic, but she perceives it as such, and she eats it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.2.5
Pali: Alasuṇe vematikā khādati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not garlic, but she is unsure of it, and she eats it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.2.6
Pali: Alasuṇe alasuṇasaññā khādati, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not garlic and she does not perceive it as such, and she eats it, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.3.2
Pali: palaṇḍuke, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is an onion; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.3.3
Pali: bhañjanake, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a shallot; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.3.4
Pali: harītake, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is chebulic myrobalan; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.3.5
Pali: cāpalasuṇe, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a spring onion; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.3.6
Pali: sūpasampāke, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is cooked in a bean curry; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.3.7
Pali: maṁsasampāke, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is cooked with meat; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.3.8
Pali: telasampāke, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is cooked with oil; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.3.9
Pali: sāḷave, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is in sweets; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.3.10
Pali: uttaribhaṅge, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a special curry; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.3.11
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.3.12
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc1:2.3.13
Pali: Lasuṇasikkhāpadaṁ paṭhamaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The training rule, the first, is finished.

English (brahmali, published): At that time the nuns from the group of six removed the hair from their private parts. They then bathed naked at a ford in the river Aciravatī together with sex workers. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc2:1.3

English (brahmali, published): “How can the nuns remove the hair from their private parts? They’re just like householders who indulge in worldly pleasures!” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc2:1.5
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tāsaṁ vesiyānaṁ ujjhāyantīnaṁ khiyyantīnaṁ vipācentīnaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns heard the complaints of those sex workers, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc2:1.6

Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo sambādhe lomaṁ saṁharāpessantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the nuns from the group of six remove the hair from their private parts?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc2:1.8

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc2:1.9
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.”

Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them,

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc2:1.12
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc2:1.13

English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc2:1.14.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī sambādhe lomaṁ saṁharāpeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun removes hair from her private parts, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc2:2.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc2:2.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc2:2.3

English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc2:2.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc2:2.5
Pali: Sambādho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The private parts: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc2:2.6
Pali: ubho upakacchakā, muttakaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): both armpits and the groin. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc2:2.7
Pali: Saṁharāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Removes: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc2:2.8
Pali: ekampi lomaṁ saṁharāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she removes one hair, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc2:2.9
Pali: Bahukepi lome saṁharāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc2:2.10.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc2:2.11
Pali: ābādhapaccayā,

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:0.2
Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:0.4
Pali: Lasuṇavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on garlic 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:0.5
Pali: 6. Upatiṭṭhanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 6. Attending on a monk 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena ārohanto nāma mahāmatto bhikkhūsu pabbajito hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): a government official called Ārohanta became a monk 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:1.3
Pali: Tassa purāṇadutiyikā bhikkhunīsu pabbajitā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): and his ex-wife a nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:1.4
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena so bhikkhu tassā bhikkhuniyā santike bhattavissaggaṁ karoti. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion that monk was having his meal in the presence of that nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:1.5
Pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī tassa bhikkhuno bhuñjantassa pānīyena ca vidhūpanena ca upatiṭṭhitvā accāvadati. 
English (brahmali, published): While he was eating, she attended on him with drinking water and a fan, and she flirted with him. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:1.6
Pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu taṁ bhikkhuniṁ apasādeti—
English (brahmali, published): But he dismissed her, saying, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:1.7
Pali: “mā, bhagini, evarūpaṁ akāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Don’t do that; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:1.8
Pali: Netaṁ kappatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): it’s not allowable.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:1.9
Pali: “Pubbe maṁ tvaṁ evañca evañca karosi, idāni ettakaṁ na sahasī”ti—
English (brahmali, published): “Before you did such and such to me, but now you can’t even take this much.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:1.10
Pali: pānīyathālakaṁ matthake āsumbhitvā vidhūpanena pahāraṁ adāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): And she dropped the water vessel on his head and struck him with the fan. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:1.11
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:1.12
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhunī bhikkhussa pahāraṁ dassatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could a nun hit a monk?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:1.13
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī bhikkhussa pahāraṁ adāsī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that a nun did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:1.14
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:1.15
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:1.16
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī bhikkhussa pahāraṁ dassati. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could a nun hit a monk? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:1.17
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:1.18
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:1.19.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhussa bhuñjantassa pānīyena vā vidhūpanena vā upatiṭṭheyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If, when a monk is eating, a nun attends on him with drinking water or a fan, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhussāti 
English (brahmali, published): A monk: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.1.6
Pali: upasampannassa. 
English (brahmali, published): fully ordained. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.1.7
Pali: Bhuñjantassāti 
English (brahmali, published): Is eating: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.1.8
Pali: pañcannaṁ bhojanānaṁ aññataraṁ bhojanaṁ bhuñjantassa. 
English (brahmali, published): is eating any of the five cooked foods. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.1.9
Pali: Pānīyaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Drinking water: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.1.10
Pali: yaṁ kiñci pānīyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): any kind of drink. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.1.11
Pali: Vidhūpanaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A fan: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.1.12
Pali: yā kāci bījanī. 
English (brahmali, published): any kind of fan. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.1.13
Pali: Upatiṭṭheyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Attends on: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.1.14
Pali: hatthapāse tiṭṭhati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she stands within arm’s reach, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.2.1
Pali: Upasampanne upasampannasaññā pānīyena vā vidhūpanena vā upatiṭṭhati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is fully ordained, and she perceives him as such, and she attends on him with drinking water or a fan, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.2.2
Pali: Upasampanne vematikā pānīyena vā vidhūpanena vā upatiṭṭhati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, and she attends on him with drinking water or a fan, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.2.3
Pali: Upasampanne anupasampannasaññā pānīyena vā vidhūpanena vā upatiṭṭhati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is fully ordained, but she does not perceive him as such, and she attends on him with drinking water or a fan, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.2.4
Pali: Hatthapāsaṁ vijahitvā upatiṭṭhati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she attends on him from beyond arm’s reach, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.2.5
Pali: Khādanīyaṁ khādantassa upatiṭṭhati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she attends on him when he is eating fresh food, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.2.6
Pali: Anupasampannassa upatiṭṭhati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she attends on one who is not fully ordained, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.2.7
Pali: Anupasampanne upasampannasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is not fully ordained, but she perceives him as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.2.8
Pali: Anupasampanne vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is not fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.2.9
Pali: Anupasampanne anupasampannasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is not fully ordained, and she does not perceive him as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.3.2
Pali: deti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she gives something; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.3.3
Pali: dāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she has someone else give something; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.3.4
Pali: anupasampannaṁ āṇāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she asks someone who is not fully ordained to do it; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.3.5
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.3.6
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc6:2.3.7
Pali: Chaṭṭhasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The sixth training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:0.5
Pali: 7. Āmakadhaññasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 7. Asking for raw grain 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo sassakāle āmakadhaññaṁ viññāpetvā nagaraṁ atiharanti dvāraṭṭhāne—
English (brahmali, published): it was the harvest season. At that time the nuns had asked for raw grain, which they then carried to town. At the town gate they were detained and told, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:1.3
Pali: “dethāyye, bhāgan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, give a share.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:1.4
Pali: Palibundhetvā muñciṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): After being released, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:1.5
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo upassayaṁ gantvā bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): they went to the nunnery and told the nuns what had happened. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:1.6
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:1.7
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo āmakadhaññaṁ viññāpessantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could nuns ask for raw grain?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:1.8
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo āmakadhaññaṁ viññāpentī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that nuns did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:1.9
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:1.10
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:1.11
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo āmakadhaññaṁ viññāpessanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could nuns do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:1.12
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:1.13
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:1.14.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī āmakadhaññaṁ viññatvā vā viññāpetvā vā bhajjitvā vā bhajjāpetvā vā koṭṭetvā vā koṭṭāpetvā vā pacitvā vā pacāpetvā vā bhuñjeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun asks for or has someone else ask for raw grain, or she roasts it or has it roasted, or she pounds it or has it pounded, or she cooks it or has it cooked, and she then eats it, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.1.5
Pali: Āmakadhaññaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Raw grain: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.1.6
Pali: sāli vīhi yavo godhumo kaṅgu varako kudrusako. 
English (brahmali, published): rice, barley, wheat, millet, wild gram, kodo millet. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.1.7
Pali: Viññatvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Asks for: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.1.8
Pali: sayaṁ viññatvā. 
English (brahmali, published): she asks herself. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.1.9
Pali: Viññāpetvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Has someone ask for: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.1.10
Pali: aññaṁ viññāpetvā. 
English (brahmali, published): she gets someone else to ask. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.1.11
Pali: Bhajjitvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Roasts: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.1.12
Pali: sayaṁ bhajjitvā. 
English (brahmali, published): she roasts it herself. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.1.13
Pali: Bhajjāpetvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Has it roasted: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.1.14
Pali: aññaṁ bhajjāpetvā. 
English (brahmali, published): she gets someone else to roast it. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.1.15
Pali: Koṭṭetvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Pounds: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.1.16
Pali: sayaṁ koṭṭetvā. 
English (brahmali, published): she pounds it herself. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.1.17
Pali: Koṭṭāpetvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Has it pounded: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.1.18
Pali: aññaṁ koṭṭāpetvā. 
English (brahmali, published): she gets someone else to pound it. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.1.19
Pali: Pacitvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Cooks: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.1.20
Pali: sayaṁ pacitvā. 
English (brahmali, published): she cooks it herself. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.1.21
Pali: Pacāpetvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Has it cooked: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.1.22
Pali: aññaṁ pacāpetvā. 
English (brahmali, published): she gets someone else to cook it. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.1.23
Pali: “Bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she receives it with the intention of eating it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.1.24
Pali: Ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For every mouthful swallowed, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.2.2
Pali: ābādhapaccayā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she does it because she is sick; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.2.3
Pali: aparaṇṇaṁ viññāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she asks for vegetables; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.2.4
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.2.5
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc7:2.2.6
Pali: Sattamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The seventh training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:0.2
Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:0.4
Pali: Lasuṇavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on garlic 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:0.5
Pali: 8. Uccārachaḍḍanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 8. Disposing of waste over a wall 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro brāhmaṇo nibbiṭṭharājabhaṭo “taññeva bhaṭapathaṁ yācissāmī”ti sīsaṁ nahāyitvā bhikkhunupassayaṁ nissāya rājakulaṁ gacchati. 
English (brahmali, published): a brahmin who had earned money by working for the king thought, “I’ll ask for my wages.” After washing his hair, he walked past the nunnery on his way to the king’s residence. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.3
Pali: Aññatarā bhikkhunī kaṭāhe vaccaṁ katvā tirokuṭṭe chaḍḍentī tassa brāhmaṇassa matthake āsumbhi. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then, after defecating in a pot, a nun disposed of the feces over a wall, and it landed on the head of that brahmin. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.4
Pali: Atha kho so brāhmaṇo ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—
English (brahmali, published): He complained and criticized the nuns, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.5
Pali: “assamaṇiyo imā muṇḍā bandhakiniyo. 
English (brahmali, published): “They’re not monastics, these shaven-headed sluts! 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.6
Pali: Kathañhi nāma gūthakaṭāhaṁ matthake āsumbhissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): How can they dump shit on my head? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.7
Pali: Imāsaṁ upassayaṁ jhāpessāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I’m gonna burn down the nunnery!” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.8
Pali: Ummukaṁ gahetvā upassayaṁ pavisati. 
English (brahmali, published): And he got hold of a firebrand and entered the nunnery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.9
Pali: Aññataro upāsako upassayā nikkhamanto addasa taṁ brāhmaṇaṁ ummukaṁ gahetvā upassayaṁ pavisantaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then a lay follower who was coming out from the nunnery saw that brahmin with a firebrand on his way in, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.10
Pali: Disvāna taṁ brāhmaṇaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): and he said to him, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.11
Pali: “kissa tvaṁ, bho, ummukaṁ gahetvā upassayaṁ pavisasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, why are you entering the nunnery with a firebrand?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.12
Pali: “Imā maṁ, bho, muṇḍā bandhakiniyo gūthakaṭāhaṁ matthake āsumbhiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): “These shaven-headed sluts dumped shit on my head. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.13
Pali: Imāsaṁ upassayaṁ jhāpessāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I’m gonna burn down the nunnery!” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.14
Pali: “Gaccha, bho brāhmaṇa, maṅgalaṁ etaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “But this is auspicious, brahmin! 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.15
Pali: Sahassaṁ lacchasi tañca bhaṭapathan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): You’ll get your wages and a thousand coins in addition.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.16
Pali: Atha kho so brāhmaṇo sīsaṁ nahāyitvā rājakulaṁ gantvā sahassaṁ alattha tañca bhaṭapathaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): That brahmin then washed his hair, went to the king’s residence, and he got his wages and a thousand coins in addition. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.17
Pali: Atha kho so upāsako upassayaṁ pavisitvā bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocetvā paribhāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): But that lay follower returned to the nunnery, told them what had happened, and then scolded them. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.18
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.19
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo uccāraṁ tirokuṭṭe chaḍḍessantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns dispose of feces over a wall?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.20
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo uccāraṁ tirokuṭṭe chaḍḍentī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.21
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.22
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.23
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo uccāraṁ tirokuṭṭe chaḍḍessanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns dispose of feces over a wall? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.24
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.25
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:1.26.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī uccāraṁ vā passāvaṁ vā saṅkāraṁ vā vighāsaṁ vā tirokuṭṭe vā tiropākāre vā chaḍḍeyya vā chaḍḍāpeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun disposes of feces or urine or trash or food scraps over a wall or over an encircling wall, or she has it disposed of in this way, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.5
Pali: Uccāro nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Feces: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.6
Pali: gūtho vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): excrement is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.7
Pali: Passāvo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Urine: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.8
Pali: muttaṁ vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): pee is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.9
Pali: Saṅkāraṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Trash: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.10
Pali: kacavaraṁ vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): refuse is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.11
Pali: Vighāsaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Food scraps: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.12
Pali: calakāni vā aṭṭhikāni vā ucchiṭṭhodakaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): food remnants or bones or wastewater. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.13
Pali: Kuṭṭo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A wall: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.14
Pali: tayo kuṭṭā— iṭṭhakākuṭṭo, silākuṭṭo, dārukuṭṭo. 
English (brahmali, published): there are three kinds of walls: walls made of bricks, walls made of stone, walls made of wood. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.15
Pali: Pākāro nāma 
English (brahmali, published): An encircling wall: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.16
Pali: tayo pākārā— iṭṭhakāpākāro, silāpākāro, dārupākāro. 
English (brahmali, published): there are three kinds of encircling walls: encircling walls made of bricks, encircling walls made of stone, encircling walls made of wood. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.17
Pali: Tirokuṭṭeti 
English (brahmali, published): Over a wall: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.18
Pali: kuṭṭassa parato. 
English (brahmali, published): to the other side of the wall. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.19
Pali: Tiropākāreti 
English (brahmali, published): Over an encircling wall: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.20
Pali: pākārassa parato. 
English (brahmali, published): to the other side of the encircling wall. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.21
Pali: Chaḍḍeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Disposes of: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.22
Pali: sayaṁ chaḍḍeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she disposes of it herself, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.23
Pali: Chaḍḍāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Has it disposed of: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.24
Pali: aññaṁ āṇāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): in asking another, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.1.25
Pali: Sakiṁ āṇattā bahukampi chaḍḍeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she only asks once, then even if the other disposes of such things many times, she commits one offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.2.2
Pali: oloketvā chaḍḍeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she disposes of it after having looked; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.2.3
Pali: avaḷañje chaḍḍeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she disposes of it at a place where no-one passes by; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.2.4
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.2.5
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc8:2.2.6
Pali: Aṭṭhamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The eighth training rule is finished.

Pali: 9. Dutiyauccārachaḍḍanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 9. Disposing of waste on plants 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc9:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

English (brahmali, published): At that time a brahmin had a barley field next to the nunnery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc9:1.3

English (brahmali, published): Food scraps: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc9:2.1.12
Pali: calakāni vā aṭṭhikāni vā ucchiṭṭhodakaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): food remnants or bones or wastewater. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc9:2.1.13
Pali: Haritaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Cultivated plants: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc9:2.1.14
Pali: pubbaṇṇaṁ aparaṇṇaṁ yaṁ manussānaṁ upabhogaparibhogaṁ ropimaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): grain, vegetables, whatever cultivated plants people consider valuable or useful. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc9:2.1.15
Pali: Chaḍḍeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Disposes of: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc9:2.1.16
Pali: sayaṁ chaḍḍeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she disposes of it herself, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc9:2.1.17
Pali: Chaḍḍāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Has it disposed of: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc9:2.1.18
Pali: aññaṁ āṇāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): in asking another, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc9:2.1.19
Pali: Sakiṁ āṇattā bahukampi chaḍḍeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she only asks once, then even if the other disposes of such things many times, she commits one offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc9:2.2.1
Pali: Harite haritasaññā chaḍḍeti vā chaḍḍāpeti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If they are cultivated plants, and she perceives them as such, and she disposes of it or has it disposed of, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc9:2.2.2
Pali: Harite vematikā chaḍḍeti vā chaḍḍāpeti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If they are cultivated plants, but she is unsure of it, and she disposes of it or has it disposed of, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc9:2.2.3
Pali: Harite aharitasaññā chaḍḍeti vā chaḍḍāpeti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If they are cultivated plants, but she perceives them as uncultivated, and she disposes of it or has it disposed of, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc9:2.2.4
Pali: Aharite haritasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If they are uncultivated plants, but she perceives them as cultivated, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc9:2.2.5
Pali: Aharite vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If they are uncultivated plants, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc9:2.2.6
Pali: Aharite aharitasaññā, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If they are uncultivated plants, and she perceives them as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc9:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc9:2.3.2
Pali: oloketvā chaḍḍeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she disposes of it after having looked; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc9:2.3.3
Pali: khettamariyāde chaḍḍeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she disposes of it at the edge of the field; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc9:2.3.4
Pali: sāmike āpucchitvā apaloketvā chaḍḍeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she disposes of it after asking and getting permission from the owners; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc9:2.3.5
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc9:2.3.6
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc9:2.3.7
Pali: Navamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The ninth training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:0.2
Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:0.4
Pali: Lasuṇavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on garlic 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:0.5
Pali: 10. Naccagītasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 10. Watching entertainment 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Rājagaha in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrel sanctuary. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena rājagahe giraggasamajjo hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time in Rājagaha there was a hilltop fair, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:1.3
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo giraggasamajjaṁ dassanāya agamaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): and the nuns from the group of six went to see it. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:1.4
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:1.5
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo naccampi gītampi vāditampi dassanāya gacchissanti, seyyathāpi gihiniyo kāmabhoginiyo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns go to see dancing, singing, and music? They’re just like householders who indulge in worldly pleasures!” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:1.6
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:1.7
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:1.8
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo naccampi gītampi vāditampi dassanāya gacchissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nuns from the group of six go to see dancing, singing, and music?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:1.9
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo naccampi gītampi vāditampi dassanāya gacchantī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that those nuns did that?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:1.10
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:1.11
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:1.12
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo naccampi gītampi vāditampi dassanāya gacchissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nuns from the group of six do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:1.13
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:1.14
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:1.15.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī naccaṁ vā gītaṁ vā vāditaṁ vā dassanāya gaccheyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun goes to see dancing or singing or music, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.1.5
Pali: Naccaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Dancing: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.1.6
Pali: yaṁ kiñci naccaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): any kind of dancing. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.1.7
Pali: Gītaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Singing: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.1.8
Pali: yaṁ kiñci gītaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): any kind of singing. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.1.9
Pali: Vāditaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Music: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.1.10
Pali: yaṁ kiñci vāditaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): any kind of music. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.1.11
Pali: Dassanāya gacchati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is on her way to see it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.1.12
Pali: Yattha ṭhitā passati vā suṇāti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Wherever she stands to see it or hear it, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.1.13
Pali: Dassanūpacāraṁ vijahitvā punappunaṁ passati vā suṇāti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Every time she goes beyond the range of sight and then sees it or hears it again, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.1.14
Pali: Ekamekaṁ dassanāya gacchati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is on her way to see any one of the three, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.1.15
Pali: Yattha ṭhitā passati vā suṇāti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Wherever she stands to see it or hear it, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.1.16
Pali: Dassanūpacāraṁ vijahitvā punappunaṁ passati vā suṇāti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Every time she goes beyond the range of sight and then sees it or hears it again, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.2.2
Pali: ārāme ṭhitā passati vā suṇāti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she sees it or hears it while remaining in the monastery; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.2.3
Pali: bhikkhuniyā ṭhitokāsaṁ vā nisinnokāsaṁ vā nipannokāsaṁ vā āgantvā naccanti vā gāyanti vā vādenti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if the dancing, singing, or music comes to the place where the nun is standing, sitting, or lying down; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.2.4
Pali: paṭipathaṁ gacchantī passati vā suṇāti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she sees it or hears it while walking in the opposite direction; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.2.5
Pali: sati karaṇīye gantvā passati vā suṇāti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she goes there because there is something to be done and she then sees it or hears it; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.2.6
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.2.7
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.2.8
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.2.9
Pali: Dasamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The tenth training rule is finished. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc10:2.2.10
Pali: Lasuṇavaggo paṭhamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The first subchapter on garlic is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:0.5
Pali: 11. Rattandhakārasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 11. Meeting a man in the dark 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhaddāya kāpilāniyā antevāsiniyā bhikkhuniyā ñātako puriso gāmakā sāvatthiṁ agamāsi kenacideva karaṇīyena. 
English (brahmali, published): a male relative of a nun who was a pupil of Bhaddā Kāpilānī went from his own village to Sāvatthī on some business. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:1.3
Pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī tena purisena saddhiṁ rattandhakāre appadīpe ekenekā santiṭṭhatipi sallapatipi. 
English (brahmali, published): Then, in the dark of the night and without a lamp, that nun stood and talked alone with that man. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:1.4
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:1.7

English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:1.8

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:1.10

English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:1.12.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī rattandhakāre appadīpe purisena saddhiṁ ekenekā santiṭṭheyya vā sallapeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If, in the dark of the night without a lamp, a nun stands or talks alone with a man, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A:

Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever …

Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:2.1.5
Pali: Rattandhakāreti 
English (brahmali, published): In the dark of the night: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:2.1.6
Pali: oggate sūriye. 
English (brahmali, published): when the sun has set. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:2.1.7
Pali: Appadīpeti 
English (brahmali, published): Without a lamp: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:2.1.8
Pali: anāloke. 
English (brahmali, published): without light. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:2.1.9
Pali: Puriso nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A man: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:2.1.10
Pali: manussapuriso na yakkho na peto na tiracchānagato viññū paṭibalo santiṭṭhituṁ sallapituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): a human male, not a male spirit, not a male ghost, not a male animal. He understands and is capable of standing together and talking. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:2.1.11
Pali: Saddhinti 
English (brahmali, published): With: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:2.1.12
Pali: ekato. 
English (brahmali, published): together. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:2.1.13
Pali: Ekenekāti 
English (brahmali, published): Alone: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:2.1.14
Pali: puriso ceva hoti bhikkhunī ca. 
English (brahmali, published): just the man and the nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:2.1.15
Pali: Santiṭṭheyya vāti 
English (brahmali, published): Stands with: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:2.1.16
Pali: purisassa hatthapāse tiṭṭhati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she stands within arm’s reach of the man, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:2.1.17
Pali: Sallapeyya vāti 
English (brahmali, published): Talks with: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:2.1.18
Pali: purisassa hatthapāse ṭhitā sallapati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she stands talking within arm’s reach of the man, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:2.1.19
Pali: Hatthapāsaṁ vijahitvā santiṭṭhati vā sallapati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she stands or talks outside of arm’s reach, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:2.1.20
Pali: Yakkhena vā petena vā paṇḍakena vā tiracchānagatamanussaviggahena vā saddhiṁ santiṭṭhati vā sallapati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she stands or talks with a male spirit, a male ghost, a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍaka</i>, or a male animal in human form, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:2.2.2
Pali: yo koci viññū dutiyo hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she has a companion who understands; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:2.2.3
Pali: arahopekkhā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is not seeking privacy; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:2.2.4
Pali: aññavihitā santiṭṭhati vā sallapati vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she stands or talks thinking of something else; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:2.2.5
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:2.2.6
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc11:2.2.7
Pali: Paṭhamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The first training rule is finished.

English (brahmali, published): 12. Meeting a man in a concealed place 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc12:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc12:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhaddāya kāpilāniyā antevāsiniyā bhikkhuniyā ñātako puriso gāmakā sāvatthiṁ agamāsi kenacideva karaṇīyena.

English (brahmali, published): knowing that the Buddha had prohibited standing or talking alone with a man in the dark of the night without a lamp, she instead stood and talked alone with that man in a concealed place.

English (brahmali, published): In a concealed place:

English (brahmali, published): it is concealed by a wall, a door, a screen, a cloth screen, a tree, a pillar, a grain container, or anything else.

Pali: Puriso nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A man:

English (brahmali, published): a human male, not a male spirit, not a male ghost, not a male animal. He understands and is capable of standing together and talking.

English (brahmali, published): With:

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc12:2.18
Pali: Yakkhena vā petena vā paṇḍakena vā tiracchānagatamanussaviggahena vā saddhiṁ santiṭṭhati vā sallapati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she stands or talks with a male spirit, a male ghost, a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍaka</i>, or a male animal in human form, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc12:2.19.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc12:2.20
Pali: yo koci viññū dutiyo hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she has a companion who understands; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc12:2.21
Pali: arahopekkhā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is not seeking privacy; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc12:2.22
Pali: aññavihitā santiṭṭhati vā sallapati vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she stands or talks thinking of something else; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc12:2.23
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc12:2.24
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc12:2.25
Pali: Dutiyasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The second training rule is finished.

Root: root/pli/ms/vinaya/pli-tv-bi-vb/pli-tv-bi-vb-pc/pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13_root-pli-ms.json

English translation sources:

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:0.2
Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:0.4
Pali: Rattandhakāravagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on the dark of the night 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:0.5
Pali: 13. Ajjhokāsasallapanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 13. Meeting a man in the open 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhaddāya kāpilāniyā antevāsiniyā bhikkhuniyā ñātako puriso gāmakā sāvatthiṁ agamāsi kenacideva karaṇīyena. 
English (brahmali, published): a male relative of a nun who was a pupil of Bhaddā Kāpilānī went from his own village to Sāvatthī on some business. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:1.3
Pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī—
English (brahmali, published): Then, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:1.4
Pali: “bhagavatā paṭikkhittaṁ paṭicchanne okāse purisena saddhiṁ ekenekā santiṭṭhituṁ sallapitun”ti teneva purisena saddhiṁ ajjhokāse ekenekā santiṭṭhatipi sallapatipi. 
English (brahmali, published): knowing that the Buddha had prohibited standing or talking alone with a man in a concealed place, she instead stood and talked alone with that man out in the open. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:1.5
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:1.6
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhunī ajjhokāse purisena saddhiṁ ekenekā santiṭṭhissatipi sallapissatipī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could a nun do such a thing?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:1.7
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī ajjhokāse purisena saddhiṁ ekenekā santiṭṭhatipi sallapatipī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that a nun did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:1.8
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:1.9
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:1.10
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī ajjhokāse purisena saddhiṁ ekenekā santiṭṭhissatipi sallapissatipi. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could a nun do such a thing? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:1.11
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:1.12
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:1.13.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī ajjhokāse purisena saddhiṁ ekenekā santiṭṭheyya vā sallapeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun stands or talks alone with a man out in the open, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.5
Pali: Ajjhokāso nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Out in the open: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.6
Pali: appaṭicchanno hoti kuṭṭena vā kavāṭena vā kilañjena vā sāṇipākārena vā rukkhena vā thambhena vā kotthaḷiyā vā, yena kenaci appaṭicchanno hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): not concealed by a wall, a door, a screen, a cloth screen, a tree, a pillar, a grain container, or anything else. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.7
Pali: Puriso nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A man: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.8
Pali: manussapuriso, na yakkho na peto na tiracchānagato, viññū paṭibalo santiṭṭhituṁ sallapituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): a human male, not a male spirit, not a male ghost, not a male animal. He understands and is capable of standing together and talking. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.9
Pali: Saddhinti 
English (brahmali, published): With: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.10
Pali: ekato. 
English (brahmali, published): together. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.11
Pali: Ekenekāti 
English (brahmali, published): Alone: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.12
Pali: puriso ceva hoti bhikkhunī ca. 
English (brahmali, published): just the man and the nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.13
Pali: Santiṭṭheyya vāti 
English (brahmali, published): Stands with: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.14
Pali: purisassa hatthapāse tiṭṭhati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she stands within arm’s reach of the man, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.15
Pali: Sallapeyya vāti 
English (brahmali, published): Talks with: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.16
Pali: purisassa hatthapāse ṭhitā sallapati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she stands talking within arm’s reach of the man, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.17
Pali: Hatthapāsaṁ vijahitvā santiṭṭhati vā sallapati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she stands or talks outside of arm’s reach, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.18
Pali: Yakkhena vā petena vā paṇḍakena vā tiracchānagatamanussaviggahena vā saddhiṁ santiṭṭhati vā sallapati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she stands or talks with a male spirit, a male ghost, a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍaka</i>, or a male animal in human form, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.19.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.20
Pali: yo koci viññū dutiyo hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she has a companion who understands; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.21
Pali: arahopekkhā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is not seeking privacy; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.22
Pali: aññavihitā santiṭṭhati vā sallapati vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she stands or talks thinking of something else; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.23
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.24
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc13:2.25
Pali: Tatiyasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The third training rule is finished.

Pali: 14. Dutiyikauyyojanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 14. Meeting a man on a street 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī rathikāyapi byūhepi siṅghāṭakepi purisena saddhiṁ ekenekā santiṭṭhatipi sallapatipi nikaṇṇikampi jappeti dutiyikampi bhikkhuniṁ uyyojeti.

English (brahmali, published): “How can Venerable Thullanandā do such things?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:1.5

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:1.11.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī rathikāya vā byūhe vā siṅghāṭake vā purisena saddhiṁ ekenekā santiṭṭheyya vā sallapeyya vā nikaṇṇikaṁ vā jappeyya dutiyikaṁ vā bhikkhuniṁ uyyojeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun stands or talks alone with a man on a street or in a cul-de-sac or at an intersection, or she whispers in his ear or dismisses her companion nun, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.1.1

English (brahmali, published): A:
