pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.1.5
Pali: Rathikā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A street: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.1.6
Pali: racchā vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): a carriage road is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.1.7
Pali: Byūhaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A cul-de-sac: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.1.8
Pali: yeneva pavisanti teneva nikkhamanti. 
English (brahmali, published): one departs the same way one enters. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.1.9
Pali: Siṅghāṭako nāma 
English (brahmali, published): An intersection: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.1.10
Pali: caccaraṁ vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): a crossroads is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.1.11
Pali: Puriso nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A man: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.1.12
Pali: manussapuriso na yakkho na peto na tiracchānagato viññū paṭibalo santiṭṭhituṁ sallapituṁ.

Pali: Saddhinti 
English (brahmali, published): With: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.1.14
Pali: ekato. 
English (brahmali, published): together. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.1.15
Pali: Ekenekāti 
English (brahmali, published): Alone: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.1.16
Pali: puriso ceva hoti bhikkhunī ca. 
English (brahmali, published): just the man and the nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.1.17
Pali: Santiṭṭheyya vāti 
English (brahmali, published): Stands with: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.1.18
Pali: purisassa hatthapāse tiṭṭhati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she stands within arm’s reach of the man, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.1.19
Pali: Sallapeyya vāti 
English (brahmali, published): Talks with: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.1.20
Pali: purisassa hatthapāse ṭhitā sallapati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she stands talking within arm’s reach of the man, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.1.21
Pali: Nikaṇṇikaṁ vā jappeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Whispers in his ear: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.1.22
Pali: purisassa upakaṇṇake āroceti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she speaks into the ear of a man, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.1.23
Pali: Dutiyikaṁ vā bhikkhuniṁ uyyojeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Dismisses her companion nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.1.24
Pali: anācāraṁ ācaritukāmā dutiyikampi bhikkhuniṁ uyyojeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if, wanting to misbehave, she dismisses her companion nun, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.1.25
Pali: Dassanūpacāraṁ vā savanūpacāraṁ vā vijahantiyā āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the companion nun is in the process of going beyond the range of sight or the range of hearing, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.1.26
Pali: Vijahite āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When the companion nun has gone beyond, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.2.1
Pali: Hatthapāsaṁ vijahitvā santiṭṭhati vā sallapati vā āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she stands or talks outside of arm’s reach, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.2.2
Pali: Yakkhena vā petena vā paṇḍakena vā tiracchānagatamanussaviggahena vā saddhiṁ santiṭṭhati vā sallapati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she stands or talks with a male spirit, a male ghost, a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍaka</i>, or a male animal in human form, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.2.4
Pali: yo koci viññū dutiyo hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she has a companion who understands; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.2.5
Pali: arahopekkhā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is not seeking privacy; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.2.6
Pali: aññavihitā santiṭṭhati vā sallapati vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she stands or talks thinking of something else; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.2.7
Pali: na anācāraṁ ācaritukāmā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she does not want to misbehave; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.2.8
Pali: sati karaṇīye dutiyikaṁ bhikkhuniṁ uyyojeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she dismisses her companion nun when there is something to be done; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.2.9
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.2.10
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc14:2.2.11
Pali: Catutthasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The fourth training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:0.5
Pali: 15. Anāpucchāpakkamanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 15. Departing without taking leave 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarā bhikkhunī aññatarassa kulassa kulūpikā hoti niccabhattikā. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time a certain nun was associating with a family from which she received a regular meal. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:1.3
Pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena taṁ kulaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āsane nisīditvā sāmike anāpucchā pakkāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): Then, after robing up one morning, she took her bowl and robe and went to that family where she sat down on a seat. She then departed without informing the owners. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:1.4
Pali: Tassa kulassa dāsī gharaṁ sammajjantī taṁ āsanaṁ bhājanantarikāya pakkhipi. 
English (brahmali, published): A slave-woman who was sweeping the house put that seat in between some vessels. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:1.5
Pali: Manussā taṁ āsanaṁ apassantā taṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): Not seeing the seat, soon afterwards the people there asked that nun, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:1.6
Pali: “kahaṁ taṁ, ayye, āsanan”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, where’s that seat?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:1.7
Pali: “Nāhaṁ taṁ, āvuso, āsanaṁ passāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I don’t know.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:1.8
Pali: “Dethāyye, taṁ āsanan”ti paribhāsitvā niccabhattaṁ pacchindiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): “Give back the seat, venerable.” And after scolding her, they made an end of her regular meal. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:1.9
Pali: Atha kho te manussā gharaṁ sodhentā taṁ āsanaṁ bhājanantarikāya passitvā taṁ bhikkhuniṁ khamāpetvā niccabhattaṁ paṭṭhapesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Then, while those people were cleaning the house, they saw that seat in between those vessels. They asked that nun for forgiveness and restored her regular meal. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:1.10
Pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): That nun then told the nuns what had happened. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:1.11
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:1.12
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhunī purebhattaṁ kulāni upasaṅkamitvā āsane nisīditvā sāmike anāpucchā pakkamissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could a nun visit a family before the meal, sit down on a seat, and then depart without informing the owners?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:1.13
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī purebhattaṁ kulāni upasaṅkamitvā āsane nisīditvā sāmike anāpucchā pakkamatī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that a nun did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:1.14
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:1.15
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:1.16
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave bhikkhunī purebhattaṁ kulāni upasaṅkamitvā āsane nisīditvā sāmike anāpucchā pakkamissati. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could a nun act like this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:1.17
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:1.18
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:1.19.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī purebhattaṁ kulāni upasaṅkamitvā āsane nisīditvā sāmike anāpucchā pakkameyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun visits families before the meal, sits down on a seat, and then departs without informing the owners, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.1.5
Pali: Purebhattaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Before the meal: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.1.6
Pali: aruṇuggamanaṁ upādāya yāva majjhanhikā. 
English (brahmali, published): from dawn until midday. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.1.7
Pali: Kulaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A family: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.1.8
Pali: cattāri kulāni— khattiyakulaṁ, brāhmaṇakulaṁ, vessakulaṁ, suddakulaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there are four kinds of families: the aristocratic family, the brahmin family, the merchant family, the worker family. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.1.9
Pali: Upasaṅkamitvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Visits: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.1.10
Pali: tattha gantvā. 
English (brahmali, published): goes there. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.1.11
Pali: Āsanaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A seat: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.1.12
Pali: pallaṅkassa okāso vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): a place for sitting cross-legged is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.1.13
Pali: Nisīditvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Sits down: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.1.14
Pali: tasmiṁ nisīditvā. 
English (brahmali, published): sits down on that seat. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.1.15
Pali: Sāmike anāpucchā pakkameyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Departs without informing the owners: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.1.16
Pali: yo tasmiṁ kule manusso viññū taṁ anāpucchā anovassakaṁ atikkāmentiyā āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if, without informing a person who understands in that family, she goes beyond the roof cover of that house, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.1.17
Pali: Ajjhokāse upacāraṁ atikkāmentiyā āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is out in the open and she goes beyond the vicinity of the seat, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpucchite anāpucchitasaññā pakkamati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she has not informed, and she does not perceive that she has, and she departs, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.2.2
Pali: Anāpucchite vematikā pakkamati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she has not informed, but she is unsure of it, and she departs, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.2.3
Pali: Anāpucchite āpucchitasaññā pakkamati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she has not informed, but she perceives that she has, and she departs, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.2.4
Pali: Pallaṅkassa anokāse āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a place for sitting cross-legged, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.2.5
Pali: Āpucchite anāpucchitasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she has informed, but she does not perceive that she has, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.2.6
Pali: Āpucchite vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she has informed, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.2.7
Pali: Āpucchite āpucchitasaññā, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If she has informed, and she perceives that she has, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.3.2
Pali: āpucchā gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she departs after informing someone; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.3.3
Pali: asaṁhārime, 
English (brahmali, published): if the seat is not movable; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.3.4
Pali: gilānāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is sick; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.3.5
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.3.6
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.3.7
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc15:2.3.8
Pali: Pañcamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The fifth training rule is finished.

Pali: 16. Anāpucchāabhinisīdanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 16. Sitting down without permission 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī pacchābhattaṁ kulāni upasaṅkamitvā sāmike anāpucchā āsane abhinisīdatipi abhinipajjatipi. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the nun Thullanandā visited families after the meal, and she sat down and lay down on the seats without asking permission of the owners. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:1.3
Pali: Manussā thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ hirīyamānā āsane neva abhinisīdanti na abhinipajjanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Because of Thullanandā, the people there had qualms,  neither sitting nor lying down. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:1.4
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): They then complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:1.5
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā pacchābhattaṁ kulāni upasaṅkamitvā sāmike anāpucchā āsane abhinisīdissatipi abhinipajjissatipī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Thullanandā visit families after the meal, and then sit down and lie down on the seats without asking the owners for permission?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:1.6
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns heard the complaints of those people. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:1.7

English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Thullanandā act in this way?” …

English (brahmali, published): “How could the nun Thullanandā act in this way?

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:1.15.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī pacchābhattaṁ kulāni upasaṅkamitvā sāmike anāpucchā āsane abhinisīdeyya vā abhinipajjeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun visits families after the meal, and then sits down or lies down on a seat without asking permission of the owners, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

Pali: Pacchābhattaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): After the meal:

Pali: Upasaṅkamitvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Visits:

Pali: tattha gantvā. 
English (brahmali, published): goes there. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:2.1.11
Pali: Sāmike anāpucchāti 
English (brahmali, published): Without asking permission of the owners: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:2.1.12
Pali: yo tasmiṁ kule manusso sāmiko dātuṁ, taṁ anāpucchā. 
English (brahmali, published): not having asked permission of a person in that family who is an owner and who has the authority to give. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:2.1.13
Pali: Āsanaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A seat: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:2.1.14

English (brahmali, published): Sits down: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:2.1.16
Pali: tasmiṁ abhinisīdati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she sits down on that seat, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:2.1.17
Pali: Abhinipajjeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Lies down: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:2.1.18
Pali: tasmiṁ abhinipajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she lies down on that seat, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpucchite anāpucchitasaññā āsane abhinisīdati vā abhinipajjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she has not asked permission, and she does not perceive that she has, and she sits down or lies down on a seat, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:2.2.2
Pali: Anāpucchite vematikā āsane abhinisīdati vā abhinipajjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she has not asked permission, but she is unsure of it, and she sits down or lies down on a seat, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:2.2.3
Pali: Anāpucchite āpucchitasaññā āsane abhinisīdati vā abhinipajjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she has not asked permission, but she perceives that she has, and she sits down or lies down on a seat, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:2.2.4
Pali: Pallaṅkassa anokāse āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not a place for sitting cross-legged, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:2.2.5
Pali: Āpucchite anāpucchitasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she has asked permission, but she does not perceive that she has, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:2.2.6
Pali: Āpucchite vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she has asked permission, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:2.2.7
Pali: Āpucchite āpucchitasaññā, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If she has asked permission, and she perceives that she has, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:2.3.2
Pali: āpucchā āsane abhinisīdati vā abhinipajjati vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she sits down or lies down after asking permission; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:2.3.3
Pali: dhuvapaññatte, 
English (brahmali, published): if a dedicated seat is permanently ready for her; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:2.3.4
Pali: gilānāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is sick; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:2.3.5
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:2.3.6
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:2.3.7
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc16:2.3.8
Pali: Chaṭṭhasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The sixth training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:0.5
Pali: 17. Anāpucchāsantharaṇasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 17. Putting out bedding without permission 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhuniyo kosalesu janapade sāvatthiṁ gacchantiyo sāyaṁ aññataraṁ gāmaṁ upagantvā aññataraṁ brāhmaṇakulaṁ upasaṅkamitvā okāsaṁ yāciṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time a number of nuns were traveling through the Kosalan country on their way to Sāvatthī, when one evening they arrived at a certain village. There they went to a brahmin family and asked for a place to stay. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:1.3
Pali: Atha kho sā brāhmaṇī tā bhikkhuniyo etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): The brahmin woman told them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:1.4
Pali: “āgametha, ayye, yāva brāhmaṇo āgacchatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please wait, venerables, until my husband returns.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:1.5
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo—
English: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:1.6
Pali: “yāva brāhmaṇo āgacchatī”ti seyyaṁ santharitvā ekaccā nisīdiṁsu ekaccā nipajjiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): While they were waiting, the nuns put out bedding, and some sat down on it while others lay down. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:1.7
Pali: Atha kho so brāhmaṇo rattiṁ āgantvā taṁ brāhmaṇiṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): When the husband returned at night, he said to his wife, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:1.8
Pali: “kā imā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Who are they?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:1.9
Pali: “Bhikkhuniyo, ayyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “They are nuns.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:1.10
Pali: “Nikkaḍḍhatha imā muṇḍā bandhakiniyo”ti, gharato nikkaḍḍhāpesi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Throw out these shaven-headed sluts!”, and he had them thrown out of the house. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:1.11
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo sāvatthiṁ gantvā bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those nuns then went to Sāvatthī, where they told the nuns what had happened. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:1.12
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:1.13
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo vikāle kulāni upasaṅkamitvā sāmike anāpucchā seyyaṁ santharitvā abhinisīdissantipi abhinipajjissantipī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns visit families at the wrong time, put out bedding without asking permission of the owners, and then sit down and lie down on it?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:1.14
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo vikāle kulāni upasaṅkamitvā sāmike anāpucchā seyyaṁ santharitvā abhinisīdantipi abhinipajjantipī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that nuns did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:1.15
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:1.16
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:1.17
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo vikāle kulāni upasaṅkamitvā sāmike anāpucchā seyyaṁ santharitvā abhinisīdissantipi abhinipajjissantipi. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could nuns act like this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:1.18
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:1.19
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:1.20.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī vikāle kulāni upasaṅkamitvā sāmike anāpucchā seyyaṁ santharitvā vā santharāpetvā vā abhinisīdeyya vā abhinipajjeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun visits families at the wrong time, puts out bedding without asking permission of the owners, or has it put out, and then sits down or lies down on it, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.1.5
Pali: Vikālo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The wrong time: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.1.6
Pali: atthaṅgate sūriye yāva aruṇuggamanā. 
English (brahmali, published): from sunset until dawn. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.1.7
Pali: Kulaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A family: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.1.8
Pali: cattāri kulāni— khattiyakulaṁ, brāhmaṇakulaṁ, vessakulaṁ suddakulaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there are four kinds of families: the aristocratic family, the brahmin family, the merchant family, the worker family. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.1.9
Pali: Upasaṅkamitvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Visits: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.1.10
Pali: tattha gantvā. 
English (brahmali, published): goes there. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.1.11
Pali: Sāmike anāpucchāti 
English (brahmali, published): Without asking permission of the owners: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.1.12
Pali: yo tasmiṁ kule manusso sāmiko dātuṁ, taṁ anāpucchā. 
English (brahmali, published): not having asked permission of a person in that family who is an owner and who has the authority to give. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.1.13
Pali: Seyyaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Bedding: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.1.14
Pali: antamaso paṇṇasanthāropi. 
English (brahmali, published): even a spread of leaves. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.1.15
Pali: Santharitvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Puts out: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.1.16
Pali: sayaṁ santharitvā. 
English (brahmali, published): she puts it out herself. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.1.17
Pali: Santharāpetvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Has it put out: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.1.18
Pali: aññaṁ santharāpetvā. 
English (brahmali, published): she has someone else put it out. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.1.19
Pali: Abhinisīdeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Sits down: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.1.20
Pali: tasmiṁ abhinisīdati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she sits down on it, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.1.21
Pali: Abhinipajjeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Lies down: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.1.22
Pali: tasmiṁ abhinipajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she lies down on it, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpucchite anāpucchitasaññā seyyaṁ santharitvā vā santharāpetvā vā abhinisīdati vā abhinipajjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she has not asked permission, and she does not perceive that she has, and she sits down or lies down on bedding after putting it out or having it put out, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.2.2
Pali: Anāpucchite vematikā seyyaṁ santharitvā vā santharāpetvā vā abhinisīdati vā abhinipajjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she has not asked permission, but she is unsure of it, and she sits down or lies down on bedding after putting it out or having it put out, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.2.3
Pali: Anāpucchite āpucchitasaññā seyyaṁ santharitvā vā santharāpetvā vā abhinisīdati vā abhinipajjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she has not asked permission, but she perceives that she has, and she sits down or lies down on bedding after putting it out or having it put out, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.2.4
Pali: Āpucchite anāpucchitasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she has asked permission, but she does not perceive that she has, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.2.5
Pali: Āpucchite vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she has asked permission, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.2.6
Pali: Āpucchite āpucchitasaññā, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If she has asked permission, and she perceives that she has, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.3.2
Pali: āpucchā seyyaṁ santharitvā vā santharāpetvā vā abhinisīdati vā abhinipajjati vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she first asks permission, and then, after putting out bedding or having it put out, sits down or lies down on it; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.3.3
Pali: gilānāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is sick; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.3.4
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.3.5
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.3.6
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc17:2.3.7
Pali: Sattamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The seventh training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:0.2
Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:0.4
Pali: Rattandhakāravagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on the dark of the night 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:0.5
Pali: 18. Paraujjhāpanakasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 18. Complaining without reflection 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhaddāya kāpilāniyā antevāsinī bhikkhunī bhaddaṁ kāpilāniṁ sakkaccaṁ upaṭṭheti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time a nun who was a pupil of Bhaddā Kāpilānī attended on her with care. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:1.3
Pali: Bhaddā kāpilānī bhikkhuniyo etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Bhaddā Kāpilānī said to the nuns, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:1.4
Pali: “ayaṁ maṁ, ayye, bhikkhunī sakkaccaṁ upaṭṭheti, imissāhaṁ cīvaraṁ dassāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, this nun is attending on me with care. I’ll give her a robe.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:1.5
Pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī duggahitena dūpadhāritena paraṁ ujjhāpesi—
English (brahmali, published): Then, because of a misunderstanding and a lack of proper reflection, that nun complained about Bhaddā Kāpilānī, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:1.6
Pali: “ahaṁ kirāyye, ayyaṁ na sakkaccaṁ upaṭṭhemi, na kira me ayyā cīvaraṁ dassatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, if I didn’t attend on her with care, she wouldn’t give me a robe.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:1.7
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:1.8
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhunī duggahitena dūpadhāritena paraṁ ujjhāpessatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could a nun complain about someone else because of a misunderstanding and a lack of proper reflection?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:1.9
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī duggahitena dūpadhāritena paraṁ ujjhāpetī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that a nun did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:1.10
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:1.11
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:1.12
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī duggahitena dūpadhāritena paraṁ ujjhāpessati. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could a nun act in this way? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:1.13
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:1.14
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:1.15.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī duggahitena dūpadhāritena paraṁ ujjhāpeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun complains about someone else because of a misunderstanding and a lack of proper reflection, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:2.1.5
Pali: Duggahitenāti 
English (brahmali, published): Because of a misunderstanding: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:2.1.6
Pali: aññathā gahitena. 
English (brahmali, published): because of wrong understanding. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:2.1.7
Pali: Dūpadhāritenāti 
English (brahmali, published): Because of a lack of proper reflection: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:2.1.8
Pali: aññathā upadhāritena. 
English (brahmali, published): because of wrong reflection. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:2.1.9
Pali: Paranti 
English (brahmali, published): Someone else: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:2.1.10
Pali: upasampannaṁ ujjhāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she complains about one who is fully ordained, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:2.2.1
Pali: Upasampannāya upasampannasaññā ujjhāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, and she perceives them as such, and she complains about them, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:2.2.2
Pali: Upasampannāya vematikā ujjhāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, and she complains about them, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:2.2.3
Pali: Upasampannāya anupasampannasaññā ujjhāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, but she does not perceive them as such, and she complains about them, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:2.2.4
Pali: Anupasampannaṁ ujjhāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she complains about someone who is not fully ordained, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:2.2.5
Pali: Anupasampannāya upasampannasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but she perceives them as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:2.2.6
Pali: Anupasampannāya vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:2.2.7
Pali: Anupasampannāya anupasampannasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, and she does not perceive them as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:2.3.2
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:2.3.3
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc18:2.3.4
Pali: Aṭṭhamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The eighth training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:0.2
Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:0.4
Pali: Rattandhakāravagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on the dark of the night 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:0.5
Pali: 19. Paraabhisapanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 19. Cursing 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo attano bhaṇḍakaṁ apassantiyo caṇḍakāḷiṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): some nuns were unable to find their possessions. They said to the nun Caṇḍakāḷī, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:1.3
Pali: “apāyye, amhākaṁ bhaṇḍakaṁ passeyyāsī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, have you seen our things?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:1.4
Pali: Caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—
English (brahmali, published): Caṇḍakāḷī complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:1.5
Pali: “ahameva nūna corī, ahameva nūna alajjinī, yā ayyāyo attano bhaṇḍakaṁ apassantiyo tā maṁ evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): “Why are you asking me if I’ve seen your things? Am I a thief? Am I shameless? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:1.6
Pali: ‘apāyye, amhākaṁ bhaṇḍakaṁ passeyyāsī’ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:1.7
Pali: Sacāhaṁ, ayye, tumhākaṁ bhaṇḍakaṁ gaṇhāmi, assamaṇī homi, brahmacariyā cavāmi, nirayaṁ upapajjāmi; 
English (brahmali, published): Venerables, if I took your things I wouldn’t be a monastic anymore. I would fall from the spiritual life and be reborn in hell. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:1.8
Pali: yā pana maṁ abhūtena evamāha sāpi assamaṇī hotu, brahmacariyā cavatu, nirayaṁ upapajjatū”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): May anyone who speaks such an untruth about me depart from monasticism, fall from the spiritual life, and be reborn in hell!” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:1.9
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:1.10
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā caṇḍakāḷī attānampi parampi nirayenapi brahmacariyenapi abhisapissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Caṇḍakāḷī curse herself and others, referring to hell and the spiritual life?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:1.11
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī attānampi parampi nirayenapi brahmacariyenapi abhisapatī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the nun Caṇḍakāḷī did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:1.12
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:1.13
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:1.14
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī attānampi parampi nirayenapi brahmacariyenapi abhisapissati. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nun Caṇḍakāḷī act like this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:1.15
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:1.16
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:1.17.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī attānaṁ vā paraṁ vā nirayena vā brahmacariyena vā abhisapeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun curses herself or someone else, referring to hell or the spiritual life, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:2.1.5
Pali: Attānanti 
English (brahmali, published): Herself: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:2.1.6
Pali: paccattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): oneself. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:2.1.7
Pali: Paranti 
English (brahmali, published): Someone else: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:2.1.8
Pali: upasampannaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one who is fully ordained. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:2.1.9
Pali: Nirayena vā brahmacariyena vā abhisapati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she curses, referring to hell or the spiritual life, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:2.2.1
Pali: Upasampannāya upasampannasaññā nirayena vā brahmacariyena vā abhisapati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, and she perceives them as such, and she curses them, referring to hell or the spiritual life, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:2.2.2
Pali: Upasampannāya vematikā nirayena vā brahmacariyena vā abhisapati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, yet she curses them, referring to hell or the spiritual life, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:2.2.3
Pali: Upasampannāya anupasampannasaññā nirayena vā brahmacariyena vā abhisapati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, but she does not perceive them as such, yet she curses them, referring to hell or the spiritual life, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:2.2.4
Pali: Tiracchānayoniyā vā pettivisayena vā manussadobhaggena vā abhisapati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she curses, referring to the animal realm, the ghost realm, or human misfortune, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:2.2.5
Pali: Anupasampannaṁ abhisapati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she curses someone who is not fully ordained, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:2.2.6
Pali: Anupasampannāya upasampannasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but she perceives them as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:2.2.7
Pali: Anupasampannāya vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:2.2.8
Pali: Anupasampannāya anupasampannasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, and she does not perceive them as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:2.3.2
Pali: atthapurekkhārāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is aiming at something beneficial; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:2.3.3
Pali: dhammapurekkhārāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is aiming at giving a teaching; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:2.3.4
Pali: anusāsanipurekkhārāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is aiming at giving an instruction; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:2.3.5
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:2.3.6
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc19:2.3.7
Pali: Navamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The ninth training rule is finished.

English (brahmali, published): The second subchapter on the dark of the night is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:0.4
Pali: Naggavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on nakedness 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:0.5
Pali: 21. Naggasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 21. Bathing naked 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhuniyo aciravatiyā nadiyā vesiyāhi saddhiṁ naggā ekatitthe nahāyanti. 
English (brahmali, published): a number of nuns were bathing naked at a ford in the river Aciravatī together with sex workers. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:1.3
Pali: Vesiyā tā bhikkhuniyo uppaṇḍesuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The sex workers teased them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:1.4
Pali: “kiṁ nu kho nāma tumhākaṁ, ayye, daharānaṁ brahmacariyaṁ ciṇṇena, nanu nāma kāmā paribhuñjitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, why practice the spiritual life when you’re still young? Why not enjoy worldly pleasures? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:1.5
Pali: Yadā jiṇṇā bhavissatha tadā brahmacariyaṁ carissatha. 
English (brahmali, published): You can practice the spiritual life when you’re old. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:1.6
Pali: Evaṁ tumhākaṁ ubho atthā pariggahitā bhavissantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way you’ll get the benefits of both.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:1.7
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo vesiyāhi uppaṇḍiyamānā maṅkū ahesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns felt humiliated. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:1.8
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo upassayaṁ gantvā bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They then went to the nunnery and told the nuns what had happened. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:1.9
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns told the monks, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:1.10
Pali: Bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): who in turn told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:1.11
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:1.12
Pali: “tena hi, bhikkhave, bhikkhunīnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññapessāmi dasa atthavase paṭicca—
English (brahmali, published): “Well then, monks, I will lay down a training rule for the nuns for the following ten reasons: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:1.13
Pali: saṅghasuṭṭhutāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): for the well-being of the Sangha … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:1.14
Pali: vinayānuggahāya. 
English (brahmali, published): for supporting the training. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:1.15
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:1.16.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī naggā nahāyeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun bathes naked, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:2.1.5
Pali: Naggā nahāyeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Bathes naked: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:2.1.6
Pali: anivatthā vā apārutā vā nahāyati, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if she bathes without wearing a sarong or an upper robe, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:2.1.7
Pali: Nahānapariyosāne āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): At the end of the bath, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:2.2.2
Pali: acchinnacīvarikāya vā naṭṭhacīvarikāya vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if her robes have been stolen or lost; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:2.2.3
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:2.2.4
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:2.2.5
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc21:2.2.6
Pali: Paṭhamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The first training rule is finished.

English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:1.7
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo appamāṇikāyo udakasāṭikāyo dhāressantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the nuns from the group of six wear such bathing robes?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:1.8
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo appamāṇikāyo udakasāṭikāyo dhārentī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the nuns from the group of six do this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:1.9
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:1.10
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:1.11
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo appamāṇikāyo udakasāṭikāyo dhāressanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the nuns from the group of six wear such bathing robes? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:1.12
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:1.13
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:1.14.1
Pali: “Udakasāṭikaṁ pana bhikkhuniyā kārayamānāya pamāṇikā kāretabbā. Tatridaṁ pamāṇaṁ—dīghaso catasso vidatthiyo, sugatavidatthiyā; tiriyaṁ dve vidatthiyo. Taṁ atikkāmentiyā chedanakaṁ pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun is having a bathing robe made, it should be made the right size. This is the right size: four standard handspans long and two wide. If it exceeds that, it is to be cut down, and she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:2.1.1
Pali: Udakasāṭikā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A bathing robe: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:2.1.2
Pali: yāya nivatthā nahāyati. 
English (brahmali, published): wearing it as a sarong, she bathes. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:2.1.3
Pali: Kārayamānāyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Is having made: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:2.1.4
Pali: karontiyā vā kārāpentiyā vā. Pamāṇikā kāretabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): making it herself or having someone else make it, it should be made the right size. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:2.1.5
Pali: Tatridaṁ pamāṇaṁ— dīghaso catasso vidatthiyo, sugatavidatthiyā; tiriyaṁ dve vidatthiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the right size: four standard handspans long and two wide. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:2.1.6
Pali: Taṁ atikkāmetvā karoti vā kārāpeti vā, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If she makes one or has one made that exceeds that, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:2.1.7
Pali: Paṭilābhena chinditvā pācittiyaṁ desetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When she gets it, it is to be cut down, and she is then to confess an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:2.1.8.1
Pali: Attanā vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she finishes what she began herself, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:2.1.9
Pali: Attanā vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she has others finish what she began herself, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:2.1.10
Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she finishes herself what was begun by others, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:2.1.11
Pali: Parehi vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she has others finish what was begun by others, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:2.1.12
Pali: Aññassatthāya karoti vā kārāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she makes one or has one made for the benefit of someone else, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:2.1.13
Pali: Aññena kataṁ paṭilabhitvā paribhuñjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she gets one that was made by someone else and then uses it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:2.2.2
Pali: pamāṇikaṁ karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she makes it the right size; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:2.2.3
Pali: ūnakaṁ karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she makes it smaller than the right size; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:2.2.4
Pali: aññena kataṁ pamāṇātikkantaṁ paṭilabhitvā chinditvā paribhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she gets one made by someone else that exceeds the right size and she cuts it down before using it; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:2.2.5
Pali: vitānaṁ vā bhūmattharaṇaṁ vā sāṇipākāraṁ vā bhisiṁ vā bibbohanaṁ vā karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she makes a canopy, a floor cover, a cloth screen, a mattress, or a pillow; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:2.2.6
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:2.2.7
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc22:2.2.8
Pali: Dutiyasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The second training rule is finished.

Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:0.2

English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:0.4
Pali: Naggavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on nakedness 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:0.5
Pali: 23. Cīvarasibbanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 23. Unstitching a robe 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarissā bhikkhuniyā mahagghe cīvaradusse cīvaraṁ dukkaṭaṁ hoti dussibbitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time a robe belonging to a certain nun had been badly made and badly sewn from expensive robe-cloth. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:1.3
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī taṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): The nun Thullanandā said to her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:1.4
Pali: “sundaraṁ kho idaṁ te, ayye, cīvaradussaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, this robe-cloth is beautiful, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:1.5
Pali: cīvarañca kho dukkaṭaṁ dussibbitan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): but the robe has been badly made and badly sewn.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:1.6
Pali: “Visibbemi, ayye, sibbissasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “If I unstitch it, will you sew it back together?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:1.7
Pali: “Āmāyye, sibbissāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sure.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:1.8
Pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī taṁ cīvaraṁ visibbetvā thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā adāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): Then that nun unstitched the robe and gave it to Thullanandā. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:1.9
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī—
English (brahmali, published): Yet although Thullanandā 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:1.10
Pali: “sibbissāmi sibbissāmī”ti neva sibbati na sibbāpanāya ussukkaṁ karoti. 
English (brahmali, published): repeatedly said she would sew it, she neither sewed it herself nor did she make any effort to have someone else do it. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:1.11
Pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): That nun then told the nuns what had happened. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:1.12
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized Thullanandā, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:1.13
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ visibbāpetvā neva sibbissati na sibbāpanāya ussukkaṁ karissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Thullanandā have a nun’s robe unstitched, and then neither sew it herself nor make any effort to have someone else do it?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:1.14
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ visibbāpetvā neva sibbati na sibbāpanāya ussukkaṁ karotī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:1.15
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:1.16
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:1.17
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ visibbāpetvā neva sibbissati na sibbāpanāya ussukkaṁ karissati. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nun Thullanandā act like this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:1.18
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:1.19
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:1.20.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ visibbetvā vā visibbāpetvā vā sā pacchā anantarāyikinī neva sibbeyya na sibbāpanāya ussukkaṁ kareyya, aññatra catūhapañcāhā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun, after unstitching a nun’s robe or having it unstitched, neither sews it herself nor makes any effort to have someone else sew it, and there were no obstacles, then, except if it was no more than four or five days, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhuniyāti 
English (brahmali, published): A nun’s: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.1.6
Pali: aññāya bhikkhuniyā. 
English (brahmali, published): another nun’s. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.1.7
Pali: Cīvaraṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Robe: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.1.8
Pali: channaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one of the six kinds of robes. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.1.9
Pali: Visibbetvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Unstitching: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.1.10
Pali: sayaṁ visibbetvā. 
English (brahmali, published): she unstitches it herself. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.1.11
Pali: Visibbāpetvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Having it unstitched: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.1.12
Pali: aññaṁ visibbāpetvā. 
English (brahmali, published): she has someone else unstitch it. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.1.13
Pali: Sā pacchā anantarāyikinīti 
English (brahmali, published): And there were no obstacles: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.1.14
Pali: asati antarāye. 
English (brahmali, published): when there is no obstacle. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.1.15
Pali: Neva sibbeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): She neither sews: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.1.16
Pali: na sayaṁ sibbeyya. 
English (brahmali, published): she does not sew it herself. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.1.17
Pali: Na sibbāpanāya ussukkaṁ kareyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Nor makes any effort to have someone else sew it: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.1.18
Pali: na aññaṁ āṇāpeyya. 
English (brahmali, published): she does not ask anyone else. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.1.19
Pali: Aññatra catūhapañcāhāti 
English (brahmali, published): Except if it was no more than four or five days: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.1.20
Pali: ṭhapetvā catūhapañcāhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): unless it was no more than four or five days. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.1.21
Pali: “Neva sibbissāmi na sibbāpanāya ussukkaṁ karissāmī”ti dhuraṁ nikkhittamatte āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she thinks, “I’ll neither sew it nor make any effort to have someone else sew it,” then by the mere fact of abandoning her duty, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.2.1
Pali: Upasampannāya upasampannasaññā cīvaraṁ visibbetvā vā visibbāpetvā vā sā pacchā anantarāyikinī neva sibbati na sibbāpanāya ussukkaṁ karoti, aññatra catūhapañcāhā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, and she perceives her as such, and she unstitches her robe or has it unstitched, and then neither sews it herself nor makes any effort to have someone else sew it, and there were no obstacles, then, except if it was no more than four or five days, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.2.2
Pali: Upasampannāya vematikā cīvaraṁ visibbetvā vā visibbāpetvā vā sā pacchā anantarāyikinī neva sibbati na sibbāpanāya ussukkaṁ karoti, aññatra catūhapañcāhā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, and she unstitches her robe or has it unstitched, and then neither sews it herself nor makes any effort to have someone else sew it, and there were no obstacles, then, except if it was no more than four or five days, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.2.3
Pali: Upasampannāya anupasampannasaññā cīvaraṁ visibbetvā vā visibbāpetvā vā sā pacchā anantarāyikinī neva sibbati na sibbāpanāya ussukkaṁ karoti, aññatra catūhapañcāhā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, but she does not perceive her as such, and she unstitches her robe or has it unstitched, and then neither sews it herself nor makes any effort to have someone else sew it, and there were no obstacles, then, except if it was no more than four or five days, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.2.4
Pali: Aññaṁ parikkhāraṁ visibbetvā vā visibbāpetvā vā sā pacchā anantarāyikinī neva sibbati na sibbāpanāya ussukkaṁ karoti, aññatra catūhapañcāhā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she unstitches another requisite or has it unstitched, and then neither sews it herself nor makes any effort to have someone else sew it, and there were no obstacles, then, except if it was no more than four or five days, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.2.5
Pali: Anupasampannāya cīvaraṁ vā aññaṁ vā parikkhāraṁ visibbetvā vā visibbāpetvā vā sā pacchā anantarāyikinī neva sibbati na sibbāpanāya ussukkaṁ karoti, aññatra catūhapañcāhā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, and she unstitches her robe or another requisite, or has it unstitched, and then neither sews it herself nor makes any effort to have someone else sew it, and there were no obstacles, then, except if it was no more than four or five days, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.2.6
Pali: Anupasampannāya upasampannasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but she perceives her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.2.7
Pali: Anupasampannāya vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.2.8
Pali: Anupasampannāya anupasampannasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, and she does not perceive her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.3.2
Pali: sati antarāye, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an obstacle; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.3.3
Pali: pariyesitvā na labhati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she searches for someone to sew, but does not find anyone; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.3.4
Pali: karontī catūhapañcāhaṁ atikkāmeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is doing it, but she takes longer than four or five days; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.3.5
Pali: gilānāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is sick; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.3.6
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.3.7
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.3.8
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc23:2.3.9
Pali: Tatiyasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The third training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:0.2
Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis

Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:0.4
Pali: Naggavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on nakedness 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:0.5
Pali: 24. Saṅghāṭicārasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 24. Moving robes 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo bhikkhunīnaṁ hatthe cīvaraṁ nikkhipitvā santaruttarena janapadacārikaṁ pakkamanti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the nuns stored one of their robes with other nuns and then left to wander the country in a sarong and an upper robe. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:1.3
Pali: Tāni cīvarāni ciraṁ nikkhittāni kaṇṇakitāni honti. 
English (brahmali, published): Because they were stored for a long time, the robes became moldy. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:1.4
Pali: Tāni bhikkhuniyo otāpenti. 
English (brahmali, published): When the nuns put them out to sun them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:1.5
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo tā bhikkhuniyo etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): other nuns asked them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:1.6
Pali: “kassimāni, ayye, cīvarāni kaṇṇakitānī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whose moldy robes are these?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:1.7
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): And they told them what had happened. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:1.8
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:1.9
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo bhikkhunīnaṁ hatthe cīvaraṁ nikkhipitvā santaruttarena janapadacārikaṁ pakkamissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns store one of their robes with other nuns and then go wandering the country in a sarong and an upper robe?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:1.10
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo bhikkhunīnaṁ hatthe cīvaraṁ nikkhipitvā santaruttarena janapadacārikaṁ pakkamantī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:1.11
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:1.12
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:1.13
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo bhikkhunīnaṁ hatthe cīvaraṁ nikkhipitvā santaruttarena janapadacārikaṁ pakkamissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:1.14
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:1.15
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:1.16.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī pañcāhikaṁ saṅghāṭicāraṁ atikkāmeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun does not move her robes for more than five days, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:2.1.5
Pali: Pañcāhikaṁ saṅghāṭicāraṁ atikkāmeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Does not move her robes for more than five days: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:2.1.6
Pali: pañcamaṁ divasaṁ pañca cīvarāni neva nivāseti na pārupati na otāpeti, pañcamaṁ divasaṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she does not wear or sun her five robes on the fifth day, then, when the fifth day has passed, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:2.2.1
Pali: Pañcāhātikkante atikkantasaññā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than five days, and she perceives it as more, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:2.2.2
Pali: Pañcāhātikkante vematikā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than five days, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:2.2.3
Pali: Pañcāhātikkante anatikkantasaññā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is more than five days, but she perceives it as less, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:2.2.4
Pali: Pañcāhānatikkante atikkantasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than five days, but she perceives it as more, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:2.2.5
Pali: Pañcāhānatikkante vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than five days, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:2.2.6
Pali: Pañcāhānatikkante anatikkantasaññā, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is less than five days and she perceives it as less, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:2.3.2
Pali: pañcamaṁ divasaṁ pañca cīvarāni nivāseti vā pārupati vā otāpeti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she wears or suns the five robes on the fifth day; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:2.3.3
Pali: gilānāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is sick; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:2.3.4
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:2.3.5
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:2.3.6
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc24:2.3.7
Pali: Catutthasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The fourth training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:0.5
Pali: 25. Cīvarasaṅkamanīyasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 25. Borrowing a robe 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarā bhikkhunī piṇḍāya caritvā allacīvaraṁ pattharitvā vihāraṁ pāvisi. 
English (brahmali, published): there was a nun who, after walking for almsfood, spread out her damp robe and entered her dwelling. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:1.3
Pali: Aññatarā bhikkhunī taṁ cīvaraṁ pārupitvā gāmaṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi. 
English (brahmali, published): Another nun put on that robe and went to the village for alms. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:1.4
Pali: Sā nikkhamitvā bhikkhuniyo pucchi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the first nun came out and asked the nuns, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:1.5
Pali: “apāyye, mayhaṁ cīvaraṁ passeyyāthā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, have you seen my robe?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:1.6
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo tassā bhikkhuniyā etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns told her what had happened. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:1.7
Pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—
English (brahmali, published): She then complained and criticized the other nun, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:1.8
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhunī mayhaṁ cīvaraṁ anāpucchā pārupissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could a nun put on my robe without asking permission?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:1.9
Pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): She told the nuns what had happened, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:1.10
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:1.12

English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her …

English (brahmali, published): “How could a nun do this?
