Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:1.17
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:1.18.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī cīvarasaṅkamanīyaṁ dhāreyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun wears a robe taken on loan, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:2.1.5
Pali: Cīvarasaṅkamanīyaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A robe taken on loan: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:2.1.6
Pali: upasampannāya pañcannaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ tassā vā adinnaṁ taṁ vā anāpucchā nivāseti vā pārupati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she wears any of the five robes belonging to a fully ordained nun, and it has not been given to her, nor has she asked permission to use it, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:2.2.1
Pali: Upasampannāya upasampannasaññā cīvarasaṅkamanīyaṁ dhāreti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, and she perceives her as such, and she takes on loan a robe belonging to her and then wears it, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:2.2.2
Pali: Upasampannāya vematikā cīvarasaṅkamanīyaṁ dhāreti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, and she takes on loan a robe belonging to her and then wears it, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:2.2.3
Pali: Upasampannāya anupasampannasaññā cīvarasaṅkamanīyaṁ dhāreti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, but she does not perceive her as such, and she takes on loan a robe belonging to her and then wears it, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:2.2.4
Pali: Anupasampannāya cīvarasaṅkamanīyaṁ dhāreti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, and she takes on loan a robe belonging to her and then wears it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:2.2.5
Pali: Anupasampannāya upasampannasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but she perceives her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:2.2.6
Pali: Anupasampannāya vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:2.2.7
Pali: Anupasampannāya anupasampannasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, and she does not perceive her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:2.3.2
Pali: sā vā deti, 
English (brahmali, published): if the robe has been given to her; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:2.3.3
Pali: taṁ vā āpucchā nivāseti vā pārupati vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she wears it after asking permission; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:2.3.4
Pali: acchinnacīvarikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if her own robe has been stolen; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:2.3.5
Pali: naṭṭhacīvarikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if her own robe has been lost; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:2.3.6
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:2.3.7
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:2.3.8
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc25:2.3.9
Pali: Pañcamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The fifth training rule is finished.

English (brahmali, published): 26. Hindering robe-cloth for the community 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc26:1.1

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc26:1.6
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena tassa kulassa gharaṁ ḍayhati.

English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Thullanandā create an obstacle for our gift? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc26:1.9
Pali: Ubhayenāmha paribāhirā bhogehi ca puññena cā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Now we have neither possessions nor merit.”

Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc26:1.18
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc26:1.19.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī gaṇassa cīvaralābhaṁ antarāyaṁ kareyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun creates an obstacle for the community to get robe-cloth, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc26:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc26:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever …

English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc26:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc26:2.1.5
Pali: Gaṇo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The community: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc26:2.1.6
Pali: bhikkhunisaṅgho vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): the Sangha of nuns is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc26:2.1.7
Pali: Cīvaraṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Robe-cloth: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc26:2.1.8
Pali: channaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ vikappanupagaṁ pacchimaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one of the six kinds of robe-cloth, but not smaller than what can be assigned to another. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc26:2.1.9
Pali: Antarāyaṁ kareyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Creates an obstacle: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc26:2.1.10
Pali: “kathaṁ ime cīvaraṁ na dadeyyun”ti antarāyaṁ karoti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she creates an obstacle with the thought, “What can be done so that these people don’t give robe-cloth?”, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc26:2.1.11
Pali: Aññaṁ parikkhāraṁ antarāyaṁ karoti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she creates an obstacle for another requisite, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc26:2.1.12
Pali: Sambahulānaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ vā ekabhikkhuniyā vā anupasampannāya vā cīvaraṁ vā aññaṁ vā parikkhāraṁ antarāyaṁ karoti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she creates an obstacle for a number of nuns or for a single nun or for someone who is not fully ordained to get robe-cloth or another requisite, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc26:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc26:2.2.2
Pali: ānisaṁsaṁ dassetvā nivāreti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she can show a benefit in obstructing them; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc26:2.2.3
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc26:2.2.4
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc26:2.2.5
Pali: Chaṭṭhasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The sixth training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:0.3

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:0.4
Pali: Naggavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on nakedness 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:0.5
Pali: 27. Paṭibāhanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 27. Blocking robe-cloth distribution 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhunisaṅghassa akālacīvaraṁ uppannaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time out-of-season robe-cloth had been given to the Sangha of nuns, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:1.3
Pali: Atha kho bhikkhunisaṅgho taṁ cīvaraṁ bhājetukāmo sannipati. 
English (brahmali, published): and the Sangha gathered to share it out. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:1.4
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā antevāsiniyo bhikkhuniyo pakkantā honti. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then the nuns who were pupils of the nun Thullanandā were away. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:1.5
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī tā bhikkhuniyo etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Thullanandā said to the nuns: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:1.6
Pali: “ayye, bhikkhuniyo pakkantā, na tāva cīvaraṁ bhājīyissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, there are nuns who are away. We cannot distribute the robe-cloth until they get back.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:1.7
Pali: Cīvaravibhaṅgaṁ paṭibāhi. 
English (brahmali, published): And she blocked the distribution of robe-cloth. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:1.8
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo na tāva cīvaraṁ bhājīyissatīti pakkamiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Since the robe-cloth could not be distributed until those nuns returned, other nuns left. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:1.9
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī antevāsinīsu bhikkhunīsu āgatāsu taṁ cīvaraṁ bhājāpesi. 
English (brahmali, published): Then, when her pupils returned, Thullanandā had that robe-cloth distributed. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:1.10
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:1.11
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā dhammikaṁ cīvaravibhaṅgaṁ paṭibāhissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Thullanandā block a legitimate distribution of robe-cloth?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:1.12
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī dhammikaṁ cīvaravibhaṅgaṁ paṭibāhatī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:1.13
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:1.14
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:1.15
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī dhammikaṁ cīvaravibhaṅgaṁ paṭibāhissati. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nun Thullanandā do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:1.16
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:1.17
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:1.18.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī dhammikaṁ cīvaravibhaṅgaṁ paṭibāheyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun blocks a legitimate distribution of robe-cloth, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:2.1.5
Pali: Dhammiko nāma cīvaravibhaṅgo 
English (brahmali, published): A legitimate distribution of robe-cloth: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:2.1.6
Pali: samaggo bhikkhunisaṅgho sannipatitvā bhājeti. 
English (brahmali, published): it is distributed by a unanimous Sangha of nuns. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:2.1.7
Pali: Paṭibāheyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Blocks: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:2.1.8
Pali: kathaṁ imaṁ cīvaraṁ na bhājeyyāti paṭibāhati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she blocks it with the thought, “What can be done so that this robe-cloth isn’t distributed?”, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:2.2.1
Pali: Dhammike dhammikasaññā paṭibāhati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, and she blocks it, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:2.2.2
Pali: Dhammike vematikā paṭibāhati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she blocks it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:2.2.3
Pali: Dhammike adhammikasaññā paṭibāhati, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as illegitimate, and she blocks it, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:2.2.4
Pali: Adhammike dhammikasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as legitimate, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:2.2.5
Pali: Adhammike vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:2.2.6
Pali: Adhammike adhammikasaññā, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:2.3.2
Pali: ānisaṁsaṁ dassetvā paṭibāhati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she blocks it after demonstrating a benefit; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:2.3.3
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:2.3.4
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc27:2.3.5
Pali: Sattamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The seventh training rule is finished.

English (brahmali, published): A householder:

English (brahmali, published): anyone who lives at home. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc28:2.1.7

English (brahmali, published): A male wanderer:

English (brahmali, published): any male wanderer apart from Buddhist monks and novice monks. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc28:2.1.9

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc28:2.1.11

English (brahmali, published): A monastic robe: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc28:2.1.12

English (brahmali, published): a mark has been made is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc28:2.1.13

Root: root/pli/ms/vinaya/pli-tv-bi-vb/pli-tv-bi-vb-pc/pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29_root-pli-ms.json

English translation sources:
  - brahmali [published]: translation/en/brahmali/vinaya/pli-tv-bi-vb/pli-tv-bi-vb-pc/pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29_translation-en-brahmali.json

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:0.2
Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:0.4
Pali: Naggavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on nakedness 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:0.5
Pali: 29. Kālaatikkamanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 29. Letting the robe season expire 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā upaṭṭhākakulaṁ thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): a family that was supporting the nun Thullanandā said to her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:1.3
Pali: “sace mayaṁ, ayye, sakkoma, bhikkhunisaṅghassa cīvaraṁ dassāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “If we’re able, venerable, we’ll give robe-cloth to the Sangha of nuns.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:1.4
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena vassaṁvuṭṭhā bhikkhuniyo cīvaraṁ bhājetukāmā sannipatiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then the nuns who had completed the rainy-season residence had gathered to distribute robe-cloth. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:1.5
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī tā bhikkhuniyo etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): But Thullanandā said to them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:1.6
Pali: “āgametha, ayye, atthi bhikkhunisaṅghassa cīvarapaccāsā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please wait, venerables. The Sangha is expecting more cloth.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:1.7
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns said to her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:1.8
Pali: “gacchāyye, taṁ cīvaraṁ jānāhī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Go and find out what’s happening.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:1.9
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī yena taṁ kulaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā te manusse etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): She then went to that family and said to them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:1.10
Pali: “dethāvuso, bhikkhunisaṅghassa cīvaran”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please give the robe-cloth to the Sangha of nuns.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:1.11
Pali: “Na mayaṁ, ayye, sakkoma bhikkhunisaṅghassa cīvaraṁ dātun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “We’re sorry, but we’re not able to give robe-cloth to the Sangha.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:1.12
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): Thullanandā told the nuns what had happened. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:1.13
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:1.14
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā dubbalacīvarapaccāsāya cīvarakālasamayaṁ atikkāmessatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Thullanandā allow the robe season to expire because of an uncertain expectation of robe-cloth?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:1.15
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī dubbalacīvarapaccāsāya cīvarakālasamayaṁ atikkāmetī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:1.16
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:1.17
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:1.18
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī dubbalacīvarapaccāsāya cīvarakālasamayaṁ atikkāmessati. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nun Thullanandā do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:1.19
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:1.20
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:1.21.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī dubbalacīvarapaccāsāya cīvarakālasamayaṁ atikkāmeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun lets the robe season expire because of an uncertain expectation of robe-cloth, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:2.1.5
Pali: Dubbalacīvarapaccāsā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): An uncertain expectation of robe-cloth: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:2.1.6
Pali: “sace mayaṁ sakkoma, dassāma karissāmā”ti vācā bhinnā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): they have said, “If we’re able, then we’ll give, then we’ll act.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:2.1.7
Pali: Cīvarakālasamayo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Robe season: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:2.1.8
Pali: anatthate kathine vassānassa pacchimo māso, atthate kathine pañca māsā. 
English (brahmali, published): for one who has not participated in the robe-making ceremony, it is the last month of the rainy season; for one who has participated in the robe-making ceremony, it is the five month period. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:2.1.9
Pali: Cīvarakālasamayaṁ atikkāmeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Lets the robe season expire: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:2.1.10
Pali: anatthate kathine vassānassa pacchimaṁ divasaṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): for one who has not participated in the robe-making ceremony, if she goes beyond the last day of the rainy season, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:2.1.11
Pali: Atthate kathine kathinuddhāradivasaṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For one who has participated in the robe-making ceremony, if she goes beyond the day on which the robe season ends, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:2.2.1
Pali: Dubbalacīvare dubbalacīvarasaññā cīvarakālasamayaṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an uncertain expectation of robe-cloth, and she perceives it as such, and she lets the robe season expire, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:2.2.2
Pali: Dubbalacīvare vematikā cīvarakālasamayaṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an uncertain expectation of robe-cloth, but she is unsure of it, and she lets the robe season expire, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:2.2.3
Pali: Dubbalacīvare adubbalacīvarasaññā cīvarakālasamayaṁ atikkāmeti, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an uncertain expectation of robe-cloth, but she does not perceive it as such, and she lets the robe season expire, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:2.2.4
Pali: Adubbalacīvare dubbalacīvarasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not an uncertain expectation of robe-cloth, but she perceives it as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:2.2.5
Pali: Adubbalacīvare vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not an uncertain expectation of robe-cloth, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:2.2.6
Pali: Adubbalacīvare adubbalacīvarasaññā, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is not an uncertain expectation of robe-cloth, and she does not perceive it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:2.3.2
Pali: ānisaṁsaṁ dassetvā nivāreti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she can show a benefit in hindering it; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:2.3.3
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:2.3.4
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc29:2.3.5
Pali: Navamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The ninth training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:0.2
Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:0.4
Pali: Naggavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on nakedness 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:0.5
Pali: 30. Kathinuddhārasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 30. Blocking the ending of the robe season 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarena upāsakena saṅghaṁ uddissa vihāro kārāpito hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time a lay follower had built a dwelling for the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.1.3
Pali: So tassa vihārassa mahe ubhatosaṅghassa akālacīvaraṁ dātukāmo hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): He wanted to give out-of-season robe-cloth to both Sanghas at the presentation ceremony for that dwelling, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.1.4
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena ubhatosaṅghassa kathinaṁ atthataṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): but both Sanghas had already performed the robe-making ceremony. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.1.5
Pali: Atha kho so upāsako saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā kathinuddhāraṁ yāci. 
English (brahmali, published): That lay follower then went to the Sangha and asked it to make an end of the robe season. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.1.6
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.1.7
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.1.8
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, kathinaṁ uddharituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow you to end the robe season. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.1.9
Pali: Evañca pana bhikkhave kathinaṁ uddharitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): And it should be done like this. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.1.10
Pali: Byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): A competent and capable monk should inform the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.1.11
Pali: ‘Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.1.12
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho kathinaṁ uddhareyya. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should end the robe season. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.1.13
Pali: Esā ñatti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the motion. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.1.14
Pali: Suṇātu me, bhante, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.1.15
Pali: Saṅgho kathinaṁ uddharati. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha ends the robe season. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.1.16
Pali: Yassāyasmato khamati kathinassa uddhāro, so tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who approves of ending the robe season should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.1.17
Pali: yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.1.18
Pali: Ubbhataṁ saṅghena kathinaṁ, khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has made an end of the robe season. The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.2.1
Pali: Atha kho so upāsako bhikkhunisaṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā kathinuddhāraṁ yāci. 
English (brahmali, published): That lay follower then went to the Sangha of nuns and asked them to end the robe season. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.2.2
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī—
English: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.2.3
Pali: “cīvaraṁ amhākaṁ bhavissatī”ti kathinuddhāraṁ paṭibāhi. 
English (brahmali, published): But the nun Thullanandā, aiming to get robe-cloth for herself, blocked the Sangha from doing it. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.2.4
Pali: Atha kho so upāsako ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—
English (brahmali, published): That lay follower complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.2.5
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo amhākaṁ kathinuddhāraṁ na dassantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nuns not end the robe season for us?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.2.6
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tassa upāsakassa ujjhāyantassa khiyyantassa vipācentassa. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns heard the complaints of that lay follower. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.2.7
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.2.8
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā dhammikaṁ kathinuddhāraṁ paṭibāhissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Thullanandā block a legitimate ending of the robe season?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.2.9
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī dhammikaṁ kathinuddhāraṁ paṭibāhatī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.2.10
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.2.11
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.2.12
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī dhammikaṁ kathinuddhāraṁ paṭibāhissati. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nun Thullanandā do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.2.13
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.2.14
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:1.2.15.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī dhammikaṁ kathinuddhāraṁ paṭibāheyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun blocks a legitimate ending of the robe season, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:2.1.5
Pali: Dhammiko nāma kathinuddhāro 
English (brahmali, published): A legitimate ending of the robe season: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:2.1.6
Pali: samaggo bhikkhunisaṅgho sannipatitvā uddharati. 
English (brahmali, published): a unanimous Sangha of nuns brings it to an end. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:2.1.7
Pali: Paṭibāheyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Blocks: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:2.1.8
Pali: “kathaṁ idaṁ kathinaṁ na uddhareyyā”ti paṭibāhati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she blocks it with the thought, “What can be done so that the robe season doesn’t end?”, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:2.1.9.1
Pali: Dhammike dhammikasaññā paṭibāhati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, and she blocks it, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:2.1.10
Pali: Dhammike vematikā paṭibāhati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she blocks it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:2.1.11
Pali: Dhammike adhammikasaññā paṭibāhati, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as illegitimate, and she blocks it, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:2.1.12
Pali: Adhammike dhammikasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as legitimate, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:2.1.13
Pali: Adhammike vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:2.1.14
Pali: Adhammike adhammikasaññā, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:2.2.2
Pali: ānisaṁsaṁ dassetvā paṭibāhati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she can show a benefit in blocking it; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:2.2.3
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:2.2.4
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:2.2.5
Pali: Dasamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The tenth training rule is finished. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc30:2.2.6
Pali: Naggavaggo tatiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): The third subchapter on nakedness is finished.

English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns do this?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc31:1.8
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo dve ekamañce tuvaṭṭentī”ti?

Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them …

Pali: bhikkhuniyoti 
English (brahmali, published): Nuns: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc31:2.1.4
Pali: upasampannāyo vuccanti. 
English (brahmali, published): fully ordained is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc31:2.1.5
Pali: Dve ekamañce tuvaṭṭeyyunti 
English (brahmali, published): If two lie down on the same bed: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc31:2.1.6
Pali: ekāya nipannāya aparā nipajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if, when one is lying down, the other lies down, they commit an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc31:2.1.7
Pali: Ubho vā nipajjanti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If both lie down together, they commit an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc31:2.1.8
Pali: Uṭṭhahitvā punappunaṁ nipajjanti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Every time they get up and then lie down again, they commit an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc31:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc31:2.2.2
Pali: ekāya nipannāya aparā nisīdati, 
English (brahmali, published): if, when one is lying down, the other sits down; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc31:2.2.3
Pali: ubho vā nisīdanti, 
English (brahmali, published): if both sit down together; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc31:2.2.4
Pali: ummattikānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if they are insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc31:2.2.5
Pali: ādikammikānanti. 
English (brahmali, published): if they are the first offenders. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc31:2.2.6
Pali: Paṭhamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The first training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:0.5
Pali: 32. Ekattharaṇatuvaṭṭanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 32. Lying on the same sheet 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo dve ekattharaṇapāvuraṇā tuvaṭṭenti. 
English (brahmali, published): two nuns were lying down on the same sheet and under the same cover. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:1.3
Pali: Manussā vihāracārikaṁ āhiṇḍantā passitvā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): When people walking about the dwellings saw this, they complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:1.4
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo dve ekattharaṇapāvuraṇā tuvaṭṭessanti, seyyathāpi gihiniyo kāmabhoginiyo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can two nuns lie down on the same sheet and under the same cover? They’re just like householders who indulge in worldly pleasures!” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:1.5
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:1.6
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:1.7
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo dve ekattharaṇapāvuraṇā tuvaṭṭessantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns do this?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:1.8
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo dve ekattharaṇapāvuraṇā tuvaṭṭentī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:1.9
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:1.10
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:1.11
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo dve ekattharaṇapāvuraṇā tuvaṭṭessanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:1.12
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:1.13
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:1.14.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhuniyo dve ekattharaṇapāvuraṇā tuvaṭṭeyyuṁ, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If two nuns lie down on the same sheet and under the same cover, they commit an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): Two: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhuniyoti 
English (brahmali, published): Nuns: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:2.1.4
Pali: upasampannāyo vuccanti. 
English (brahmali, published): fully ordained is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:2.1.5
Pali: Dve ekattharaṇapāvuraṇā tuvaṭṭeyyunti 
English (brahmali, published): If two lie down on the same sheet and under the same cover: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:2.1.6
Pali: taññeva attharitvā taññeva pārupanti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if they spread out just the one and cover themselves with just the one, they commit an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:2.2.1
Pali: Ekattharaṇapāvuraṇe ekattharaṇapāvuraṇasaññā tuvaṭṭenti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is the same sheet and the same cover, and they perceive them as such, and they lie down, they commit an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:2.2.2
Pali: Ekattharaṇapāvuraṇe vematikā tuvaṭṭenti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is the same sheet and the same cover, but they are unsure of it, and they lie down, they commit an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:2.2.3
Pali: Ekattharaṇapāvuraṇe nānattharaṇapāvuraṇasaññā tuvaṭṭenti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is the same sheet and the same cover, but they perceive them as different, and they lie down, they commit an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:2.2.4
Pali: Ekattharaṇe nānāpāvuraṇe, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is the same sheet but different covers, they commit an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:2.2.5
Pali: Nānattharaṇe ekapāvuraṇe, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is different sheets but the same cover, they commit an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:2.2.6
Pali: Nānattharaṇapāvuraṇe ekattharaṇapāvuraṇasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is different sheets and different covers, but they perceive them as the same, they commit an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:2.2.7
Pali: Nānattharaṇapāvuraṇe vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is different sheets and different covers, but they are unsure of it, they commit an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:2.2.8
Pali: Nānattharaṇapāvuraṇe nānattharaṇapāvuraṇasaññā, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is different sheets and different covers, and they perceive them as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:2.3.2
Pali: vavatthānaṁ dassetvā nipajjanti, 
English (brahmali, published): if they make a partition and then lie down; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:2.3.3
Pali: ummattikānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if they are insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:2.3.4
Pali: ādikammikānanti. 
English (brahmali, published): if they are the first offenders. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc32:2.3.5
Pali: Dutiyasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The second training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:0.2
Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis

Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:0.4
Pali: Tuvaṭṭavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on lying down 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:0.5
Pali: 33. Aphāsukaraṇasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 33. Making a nun ill at ease 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī bahussutā hoti bhāṇikā visāradā paṭṭā dhammiṁ kathaṁ kātuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the nun Thullanandā was a learned reciter, and she was confident and skilled at giving teachings. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:1.3
Pali: Bhaddāpi kāpilānī bahussutā hoti bhāṇikā visāradā paṭṭā dhammiṁ kathaṁ kātuṁ uḷārasambhāvitā. 
English (brahmali, published): Bhaddā Kāpilānī, too, was a learned reciter who was confident and skilled at giving teachings, and she was respected for her excellence. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:1.4
Pali: Manussā—
English: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:1.5
Pali: “ayyā bhaddā kāpilānī bahussutā bhāṇikā visāradā paṭṭā dhammiṁ kathaṁ kātuṁ uḷārasambhāvitā”ti bhaddaṁ kāpilāniṁ paṭhamaṁ payirupāsitvā, pacchā thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ payirupāsanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Because of this, people visited Bhaddā Kāpilānī first and then Thullanandā. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:1.6
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī issāpakatā—
English (brahmali, published): Overcome by jealousy, Thullanandā thought, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:1.7
Pali: “imā kira appicchā santuṭṭhā pavivittā asaṁsaṭṭhā yā imā saññattibahulā viññattibahulā viharantī”ti bhaddāya kāpilāniyā purato caṅkamatipi tiṭṭhatipi nisīdatipi seyyampi kappeti uddisatipi uddisāpetipi sajjhāyampi karoti. 
English (brahmali, published): “These ones, who are supposedly contented and have few desires, who are supposedly secluded and not socializing, are always persuading and convincing people.” And in front of Bhaddā Kāpilānī, she walked back and forth, stood, sat down, and lay down, and she recited and had others recite, and she rehearsed. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:1.8
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:1.9
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā ayyāya bhaddāya kāpilāniyā sañcicca aphāsuṁ karissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Thullanandā intentionally make Bhaddā Kāpilānī ill at ease?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:1.10
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī bhaddāya kāpilāniyā sañcicca aphāsuṁ karotī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:1.11
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:1.12
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:1.13
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī bhaddāya kāpilāniyā sañcicca aphāsuṁ karissati. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nun Thullanandā do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:1.14
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:1.15
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:1.16.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā sañcicca aphāsuṁ kareyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun intentionally makes a nun ill at ease, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:2.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhuniyāti 
English (brahmali, published): A nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:2.1.6
Pali: aññāya bhikkhuniyā. 
English (brahmali, published): another nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:2.1.7
Pali: Sañciccāti 
English (brahmali, published): Intentionally: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:2.1.8
Pali: jānantī sañjānantī cecca abhivitaritvā vītikkamo. 
English (brahmali, published): knowing, perceiving, having intended, having decided, she transgresses. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:2.1.9
Pali: Aphāsuṁ kareyyāti—
English (brahmali, published): Makes ill at ease: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:2.1.10
Pali: “iminā imissā aphāsu bhavissatī”ti anāpucchā purato caṅkamati vā tiṭṭhati vā nisīdati vā seyyaṁ vā kappeti uddisati vā uddisāpeti vā sajjhāyaṁ vā karoti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if, without asking permission, but thinking, “In this way she will be ill at ease,” she walks back and forth in front of her, or she stands, sits down, or lies down in front of her, or she recites, has others recite, or rehearses in front of her, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:2.2.1
Pali: Upasampannāya upasampannasaññā sañcicca aphāsuṁ karoti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, and she perceives her as such, and she intentionally makes her ill at ease, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:2.2.2
Pali: Upasampannāya vematikā sañcicca aphāsuṁ karoti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, and she intentionally makes her ill at ease, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:2.2.3
Pali: Upasampannāya anupasampannasaññā sañcicca aphāsuṁ karoti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, but she does not perceive her as such, and she intentionally makes her ill at ease, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:2.2.4
Pali: Anupasampannāya sañcicca aphāsuṁ karoti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, and she intentionally makes her ill at ease, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:2.2.5
Pali: Anupasampannāya upasampannasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but she perceives her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:2.2.6
Pali: Anupasampannāya vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:2.2.7
Pali: Anupasampannāya anupasampannasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, and she does not perceive her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:2.3.2
Pali: na aphāsuṁ kattukāmā āpucchā purato caṅkamati vā tiṭṭhati vā nisīdati vā seyyaṁ vā kappeti uddisati vā uddisāpeti vā sajjhāyaṁ vā karoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if, after asking permission and not desiring to make her ill at ease, she walks back and forth in front of her, or she stands, sits down, or lies down in front of her, or she recites, has others recite, or rehearses in front of her; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:2.3.3
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:2.3.4
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc33:2.3.5
Pali: Tatiyasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The third training rule is finished.

Root: root/pli/ms/vinaya/pli-tv-bi-vb/pli-tv-bi-vb-pc/pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34_root-pli-ms.json

English translation sources:
  - brahmali [published]: translation/en/brahmali/vinaya/pli-tv-bi-vb/pli-tv-bi-vb-pc/pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34_translation-en-brahmali.json

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:0.2
Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:0.4
Pali: Tuvaṭṭavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on lying down 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:0.5
Pali: 34. Naupaṭṭhāpanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 34. Not nursing a disciple 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī dukkhitaṁ sahajīviniṁ neva upaṭṭheti na upaṭṭhāpanāya ussukkaṁ karoti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time a disciple of the nun Thullanandā was suffering, but Thullanandā neither nursed her nor made any effort to have someone else nurse her. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:1.3
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:1.4
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā dukkhitaṁ sahajīviniṁ neva upaṭṭhessati na upaṭṭhāpanāya ussukkaṁ karissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Thullanandā not nurse a suffering disciple, nor make any effort to have someone else nurse her?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:1.5
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī dukkhitaṁ sahajīviniṁ neva upaṭṭheti na upaṭṭhāpanāya ussukkaṁ karotī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā didn’t do this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:1.6
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:1.7
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:1.8
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī dukkhitaṁ sahajīviniṁ neva upaṭṭhessati na upaṭṭhāpanāya ussukkaṁ karissati. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nun Thullanandā not do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:1.9
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:1.10
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:1.11.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī dukkhitaṁ sahajīviniṁ neva upaṭṭheyya na upaṭṭhāpanāya ussukkaṁ kareyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun neither nurses a suffering disciple, nor makes any effort to have someone else nurse her, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:2.1.5
Pali: Dukkhitā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Suffering: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:2.1.6
Pali: gilānā vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): sick is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:2.1.7
Pali: Sahajīvinī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Disciple: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:2.1.8
Pali: saddhivihārinī vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): a student is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:2.1.9
Pali: Neva upaṭṭheyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Neither nurses: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:2.1.10
Pali: na sayaṁ upaṭṭheyya. 
English (brahmali, published): does not herself nurse her. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:2.1.11
Pali: Na upaṭṭhāpanāya ussukkaṁ kareyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Nor makes any effort to have someone else nurse her: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:2.1.12
Pali: na aññaṁ āṇāpeyya. 
English (brahmali, published): she does not ask anyone else. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:2.1.13
Pali: “Neva upaṭṭhessāmi, na upaṭṭhāpanāya ussukkaṁ karissāmī”ti dhuraṁ nikkhittamatte āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she thinks, “I will neither nurse her nor make any effort to have someone else nurse her,” then by the mere fact of abandoning her duty, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:2.1.14
Pali: Antevāsiniṁ vā anupasampannaṁ vā neva upaṭṭheti, na upaṭṭhāpanāya ussukkaṁ karoti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she neither nurses nor makes any effort to have someone else nurse a pupil or one who is not fully ordained, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:2.2.2
Pali: sati antarāye, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an obstacle; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:2.2.3
Pali: pariyesitvā na labhati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she searches but does not find a nurse; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:2.2.4
Pali: gilānāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is sick; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:2.2.5
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:2.2.6
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:2.2.7
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc34:2.2.8
Pali: Catutthasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The fourth training rule is finished.

Pali: 35. Nikkaḍḍhanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 35. Evicting a nun 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhaddā kāpilānī sākete vassaṁ upagatā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time Bhaddā Kāpilānī had entered the rainy-season residence at Sāketa. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:1.3
Pali: Sā kenacideva karaṇīyena ubbāḷhā thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā santike dūtaṁ pāhesi—
English (brahmali, published): But because she was disturbed by a certain matter, she sent a message to the nun Thullanandā: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:1.4
Pali: “sace me ayyā thullanandā upassayaṁ dadeyya āgaccheyyāmahaṁ sāvatthin”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “If you would give me a dwelling place, I would come to Sāvatthī.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:1.5
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī evamāha—
English (brahmali, published): Thullanandā replied, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:1.6
Pali: “āgacchatu, dassāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please come; I’ll give you one.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:1.7
Pali: Atha kho bhaddā kāpilānī sāketā sāvatthiṁ agamāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): Bhaddā Kāpilānī then traveled from Sāketa to Sāvatthī, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:1.8
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī bhaddāya kāpilāniyā upassayaṁ adāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): and Thullanandā gave her a dwelling place. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:1.9
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī bahussutā hoti bhāṇikā visāradā paṭṭā dhammiṁ kathaṁ kātuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time Thullanandā was a learned reciter, and she was confident and skilled at giving teachings. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:1.10
Pali: Bhaddāpi kāpilānī bahussutā hoti bhāṇikā visāradā paṭṭā dhammiṁ kathaṁ kātuṁ uḷārasambhāvitā. 
English (brahmali, published): Bhaddā Kāpilānī, too, was a learned reciter who was confident and skilled at giving teachings, and she was respected for her excellence. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:1.11
Pali: Manussā—
English: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:1.12
Pali: “ayyā bhaddā kāpilānī bahussutā bhāṇikā visāradā paṭṭā dhammiṁ kathaṁ kātuṁ uḷārasambhāvitā”ti bhaddaṁ kāpilāniṁ paṭhamaṁ payirupāsitvā pacchā thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ payirupāsanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Because of this, people visited Bhaddā Kāpilānī first and then Thullanandā. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:1.13
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī issāpakatā—
English (brahmali, published): Overcome by jealousy, Thullanandā thought, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:1.14
Pali: “imā kira appicchā santuṭṭhā pavivittā asaṁsaṭṭhā yā imā saññattibahulā viññattibahulā viharantī”ti kupitā anattamanā bhaddaṁ kāpilāniṁ upassayā nikkaḍḍhi. 
English (brahmali, published): “These ones, who are supposedly contented and have few desires, who are supposedly secluded and not socializing, are always persuading and convincing people.” And in anger she threw Bhaddā Kāpilānī out of that dwelling place. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:1.15
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:1.16
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā ayyāya bhaddāya kāpilāniyā upassayaṁ datvā kupitā anattamanā nikkaḍḍhissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Thullanandā give a dwelling place to Venerable Bhaddā Kāpilānī and then throw her out in anger?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:1.17
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī bhaddāya kāpilāniyā upassayaṁ datvā kupitā anattamanā nikkaḍḍhatī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:1.18
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.”

English (brahmali, published): “How could the nun Thullanandā do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:1.21

English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” …

English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:1.23.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā upassayaṁ datvā kupitā anattamanā nikkaḍḍheyya vā nikkaḍḍhāpeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun gives a dwelling place to a nun, and then, in anger, throws her out or has her thrown out, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhuniyāti 
English (brahmali, published): To a nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.1.6
Pali: aññāya bhikkhuniyā. 
English (brahmali, published): to another nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.1.7
Pali: Upassayo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A dwelling place: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.1.8
Pali: kavāṭabaddho vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): one that has a door is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.1.9
Pali: Datvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Gives: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.1.10
Pali: sayaṁ datvā. 
English (brahmali, published): she gives it herself. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.1.11
Pali: Kupitā anattamanāti 
English (brahmali, published): In anger: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.1.12
Pali: anabhiraddhā āhatacittā khilajātā. 
English (brahmali, published): discontent, having hatred, hostile. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.1.13
Pali: Nikkaḍḍheyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Throws out: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.1.14
Pali: gabbhe gahetvā pamukhaṁ nikkaḍḍhati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she takes hold of her in a room and throws her out to the entryway, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.1.15
Pali: Pamukhe gahetvā bahi nikkaḍḍhati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she takes hold of her in the entryway and throws her outside, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.1.16
Pali: Ekena payogena bahukepi dvāre atikkāmeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Even if she makes her go through many doors with a single effort, she commits one offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.1.17
Pali: Nikkaḍḍhāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Has thrown out: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.1.18
Pali: aññaṁ āṇāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she asks another, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.1.19
Pali: Sakiṁ āṇattā bahukepi dvāre atikkāmeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she only asks once, then even if the other makes her go through many doors, she commits one offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.2.1
Pali: Upasampannāya upasampannasaññā upassayaṁ datvā kupitā anattamanā nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, and she perceives her as such, and she gives her a dwelling place, and she then throws her out in anger or has her thrown out, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.2.2
Pali: Upasampannāya vematikā upassayaṁ datvā kupitā anattamanā nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, and she gives her a dwelling place, and she then throws her out in anger or has her thrown out, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.2.3
Pali: Upasampannāya anupasampannasaññā upassayaṁ datvā kupitā anattamanā nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, but she does not perceive her as such, and she gives her a dwelling place, and she then throws her out in anger or has her thrown out, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.2.4
Pali: Tassā parikkhāraṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she throws out one of her requisites, or she has it thrown out, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.2.5
Pali: Akavāṭabaddhā nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she throws her out or has her thrown out from a dwelling place without a door, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.2.6
Pali: Tassā parikkhāraṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she throws out one of her requisites from a dwelling place without a door, or she has it thrown out, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.2.7
Pali: Anupasampannaṁ kavāṭabaddhā vā akavāṭabaddhā vā nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she throws out one who is not fully ordained, or she has her thrown out, from a dwelling place with or without a door, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.2.8
Pali: Tassā parikkhāraṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she throws out one of her requisites from that place, or she has it thrown out, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.2.9
Pali: Anupasampannāya upasampannasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but she perceives her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.2.10
Pali: Anupasampannāya vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.2.11
Pali: Anupasampannāya anupasampannasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, and she does not perceive her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.3.2
Pali: alajjiniṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she throws out, or has thrown out, one who is shameless; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.3.3
Pali: tassā parikkhāraṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she throws out, or has thrown out, a requisite belonging to that person; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.3.4
Pali: ummattikaṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she throws out, or has thrown out, one who is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.3.5
Pali: tassā parikkhāraṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she throws out, or has thrown out, a requisite belonging to that person; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.3.6
Pali: bhaṇḍanakārikaṁ kalahakārikaṁ vivādakārikaṁ bhassakārikaṁ saṅghe adhikaraṇakārikaṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she throws out, or has thrown out, one who is quarrelsome and argumentative, and who creates legal issues in the Sangha; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.3.7
Pali: tassā parikkhāraṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she throws out, or has thrown out, a requisite belonging to that person; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.3.8
Pali: antevāsiniṁ vā saddhivihāriniṁ vā na sammā vattantiṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she throws out, or has thrown out, a pupil or student who is not conducting herself properly; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.3.9
Pali: tassā parikkhāraṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she throws out, or has thrown out, a requisite belonging to that person; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.3.10
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.3.11
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc35:2.3.12
Pali: Pañcamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The fifth training rule is finished.

English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis
