pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:0.5
Pali: 36. Saṁsaṭṭhasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 36. Socializing with a householder 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī saṁsaṭṭhā viharati gahapatināpi gahapatiputtenapi. 
English (brahmali, published): the nun Caṇḍakāḷī was socializing with householders and their offspring. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:1.3
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:1.4
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā caṇḍakāḷī saṁsaṭṭhā viharissati gahapatināpi gahapatiputtenapī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can Venerable Caṇḍakāḷī socialize with householders and their offspring?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:1.5
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī saṁsaṭṭhā viharati gahapatināpi gahapatiputtenapī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the nun Caṇḍakāḷī does this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:1.6
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:1.7
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:1.8
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī saṁsaṭṭhā viharissati gahapatināpi gahapatiputtenapi. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the nun Caṇḍakāḷī do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:1.9
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:1.10
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:1.11.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī saṁsaṭṭhā vihareyya gahapatinā vā gahapatiputtena vā sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi evamassa vacanīyā—
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun is socializing with a householder or a householder’s offspring, the nuns should correct her like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:1.12
Pali: ‘māyye, saṁsaṭṭhā vihari gahapatināpi gahapatiputtenapi. Viviccāyye; vivekaññeva bhaginiyā saṅgho vaṇṇetī’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, don’t socialize with householders or householders’ offspring. Be secluded, venerable. The Sangha praises seclusion for the sisters.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:1.13
Pali: Evañca sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi vuccamānā tatheva paggaṇheyya, sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsitabbā tassa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): If that nun continues as before, the nuns should press her up to three times to make her stop. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:1.14
Pali: Yāvatatiyañce samanubhāsīyamānā taṁ paṭinissajjeyya, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ; no ce paṭinissajjeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If she then stops, all is well. If she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.5
Pali: Saṁsaṭṭhā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Socializing: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.6
Pali: ananulomikena kāyikavācasikena saṁsaṭṭhā. 
English (brahmali, published): she socializes with improper bodily and verbal actions. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.7
Pali: Gahapati nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A householder: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.8
Pali: yo koci agāraṁ ajjhāvasati. 
English (brahmali, published): anyone who lives at home. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.9
Pali: Gahapatiputto nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A householder’s offspring: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.10
Pali: ye keci puttabhātaro. 
English (brahmali, published): whoever is an offspring or a sibling. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.11
Pali: Sā bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Her: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.12
Pali: yā sā saṁsaṭṭhā bhikkhunī. 
English (brahmali, published): the nun who is socializing. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.13
Pali: Bhikkhunīhīti 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.14
Pali: aññāhi bhikkhunīhi. Yā passanti yā suṇanti tāhi vattabbā—
English (brahmali, published): other nuns who see it or hear about it. They should correct her like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.15
Pali: “māyye, saṁsaṭṭhā vihari gahapatināpi gahapatiputtenapi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, don’t socialize with householders or householders’ offspring. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.16
Pali: Viviccāyye; 
English (brahmali, published): Be secluded, venerable. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.17
Pali: vivekaññeva bhaginiyā saṅgho vaṇṇetī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha praises seclusion for the sisters.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.18
Pali: Dutiyampi vattabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): And they should correct her a second 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.19
Pali: Tatiyampi vattabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): and a third time. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.20
Pali: Sace paṭinissajjati, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): If she stops, all is well. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.21
Pali: no ce paṭinissajjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she does not stop, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.22
Pali: Sutvā na vadanti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If those who hear about it do not say anything, they commit an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.23
Pali: Sā bhikkhunī saṅghamajjhampi ākaḍḍhitvā vattabbā—
English (brahmali, published): That nun, even if she has to be pulled into the Sangha, should be corrected like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.24
Pali: “māyye, saṁsaṭṭhā vihari gahapatināpi gahapatiputtenapi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, don’t socialize with householders or householders’ offspring. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.25
Pali: Viviccāyye; 
English (brahmali, published): Be secluded, venerable. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.26
Pali: vivekaññeva bhaginiyā saṅgho vaṇṇetī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha praises seclusion for the sisters.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.27
Pali: Dutiyampi vattabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): They should correct her a second 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.28
Pali: Tatiyampi vattabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): and a third time. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.29
Pali: Sace paṭinissajjati, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): If she stops, all is well. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.30
Pali: no ce paṭinissajjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she does not stop, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.31
Pali: Sā bhikkhunī samanubhāsitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): Should press her: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.32
Pali: “Evañca pana, bhikkhave, samanubhāsitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, she should be pressed like this. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.33
Pali: Byattāya bhikkhuniyā paṭibalāya saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): A competent and capable nun should inform the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.34
Pali: ‘Suṇātu me, ayye, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.35
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā bhikkhunī saṁsaṭṭhā viharati gahapatināpi gahapatiputtenapi. 
English (brahmali, published): The nun so-and-so is socializing with householders and their offspring. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.36
Pali: Sā taṁ vatthuṁ na paṭinissajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): And she keeps on doing it. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.37
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuniṁ samanubhāseyya tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should press the nun so-and-so to make her stop. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.38
Pali: Esā ñatti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the motion. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.39
Pali: Suṇātu me, ayye, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.40
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā bhikkhunī saṁsaṭṭhā viharati gahapatināpi gahapatiputtenapi. 
English (brahmali, published): The nun so-and-so is socializing with householders and their offspring. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.41
Pali: Sā taṁ vatthuṁ na paṭinissajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): And she keeps on doing it. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.42
Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuniṁ samanubhāsati tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha presses the nun so-and-so to make her stop. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.43
Pali: Yassā ayyāya khamati itthannāmāya bhikkhuniyā samanubhāsanā tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya, sā tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any nun who approves of pressing the nun so-and-so to make her stop should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.44
Pali: yassā nakkhamati, sā bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any nun who doesn’t approve should speak up. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.45
Pali: Dutiyampi etamatthaṁ vadāmi …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): For the second time, I speak on this matter. … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.46
Pali: tatiyampi etamatthaṁ vadāmi …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): For the third time, I speak on this matter. … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.47
Pali: Samanubhaṭṭhā saṅghena itthannāmā bhikkhunī tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya; 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has pressed the nun so-and-so to make her stop. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.48
Pali: khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.49
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.50
Pali: Dvīhi kammavācāhi dukkaṭā. 
English (brahmali, published): After each of the first two announcements, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.1.51
Pali: Kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When the last announcement is finished, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.2.1
Pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.2.2
Pali: Dhammakamme vematikā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.2.3
Pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as illegitimate, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.2.4
Pali: Adhammakamme dhammakammasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as legitimate, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.2.5
Pali: Adhammakamme vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.2.6
Pali: Adhammakamme adhammakammasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.3.2
Pali: asamanubhāsantiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she has not been pressed; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.3.3
Pali: paṭinissajjantiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she stops; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.3.4
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.3.5
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc36:2.3.6
Pali: Chaṭṭhasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The sixth training rule is finished.

Pali: 37. Antoraṭṭhasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 37. Wandering within the country 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo antoraṭṭhe sāsaṅkasammate sappaṭibhaye asatthikā cārikaṁ caranti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the nuns went wandering without a group of travelers where it was considered risky and dangerous within their own country. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:1.3
Pali: Dhuttā dūsenti. 
English (brahmali, published): Scoundrels raped them. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:1.4
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:1.5
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo antoraṭṭhe sāsaṅkasammate sappaṭibhaye asatthikā cārikaṁ carissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns go wandering without a group of travelers where it’s considered risky and dangerous within their own country?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:1.6
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo antoraṭṭhe sāsaṅkasammate sappaṭibhaye asatthikā cārikaṁ carantī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:1.7
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:1.8
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:1.9
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo antoraṭṭhe sāsaṅkasammate sappaṭibhaye asatthikā cārikaṁ carissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:1.10
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:1.11
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:1.12.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī antoraṭṭhe sāsaṅkasammate sappaṭibhaye asatthikā cārikaṁ careyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun goes wandering without a group of travelers where it is considered risky and dangerous within her own country, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:2.1.2

English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:2.1.3

English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:2.1.5
Pali: Antoraṭṭheti 
English (brahmali, published): Within her own country: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:2.1.6
Pali: yassa vijite viharati, tassa raṭṭhe. 
English (brahmali, published): in the country where she is living. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:2.1.7
Pali: Sāsaṅkaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Risky: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:2.1.8
Pali: tasmiṁ magge corānaṁ niviṭṭhokāso dissati, bhuttokāso dissati, ṭhitokāso dissati, nisinnokāso dissati, nipannokāso dissati. 
English (brahmali, published): a place has been seen along that road where criminals are camping, eating, standing, sitting, or lying down. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:2.1.9
Pali: Sappaṭibhayaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Dangerous: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:2.1.10
Pali: tasmiṁ magge corehi manussā hatā dissanti, viluttā dissanti, ākoṭitā dissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): criminals have been seen along that road, injuring, robbing, or beating people. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:2.1.11
Pali: Asatthikā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Without a group of travelers: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:2.1.12
Pali: vinā satthena. 
English (brahmali, published): in the absence of a group of travelers. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:2.1.13
Pali: Cārikaṁ careyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Goes wandering: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:2.1.14
Pali: kukkuṭasampāte gāme gāmantare gāmantare āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the inhabited areas are a chicken’s flight apart, then for every next inhabited area she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:2.1.15
Pali: Agāmake araññe addhayojane addhayojane āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When it is an uninhabited area, a wilderness, then for every six kilometers she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:2.2.2
Pali: satthena saha gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she travels with a group; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:2.2.3
Pali: kheme appaṭibhaye gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she travels where it is safe and free from danger; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:2.2.4
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:2.2.5
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:2.2.6
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc37:2.2.7
Pali: Sattamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The seventh training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:0.5
Pali: 38. Tiroraṭṭhasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 38. Wandering outside the country 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo tiroraṭṭhe sāsaṅkasammate sappaṭibhaye asatthikā cārikaṁ caranti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the nuns went wandering without a group of travelers where it was considered risky and dangerous outside their own country. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:1.3
Pali: Dhuttā dūsenti. 
English (brahmali, published): Scoundrels raped them. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:1.4
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:1.5
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo tiroraṭṭhe sāsaṅkasammate sappaṭibhaye asatthikā cārikaṁ carissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns go wandering without a group of travelers where it’s considered risky and dangerous outside their own country?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:1.6
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo tiroraṭṭhe sāsaṅkasammate sappaṭibhaye asatthikā cārikaṁ carantī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:1.7
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:1.8
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:1.9
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo tiroraṭṭhe sāsaṅkasammate sappaṭibhaye asatthikā cārikaṁ carissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:1.10
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:1.11
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:1.12.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī tiroraṭṭhe sāsaṅkasammate sappaṭibhaye asatthikā cārikaṁ careyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun goes wandering without a group of travelers where it is considered risky and dangerous outside her own country, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:2.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:2.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:2.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:2.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:2.5
Pali: Tiroraṭṭheti 
English (brahmali, published): Outside her own country: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:2.6
Pali: yassa vijite viharati, taṁ ṭhapetvā aññassa raṭṭhe. 
English (brahmali, published): in any country apart from the one where she is living. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:2.7
Pali: Sāsaṅkaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Risky: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:2.8
Pali: tasmiṁ magge corānaṁ niviṭṭhokāso dissati, bhuttokāso dissati, ṭhitokāso dissati, nisinnokāso dissati, nipannokāso dissati. 
English (brahmali, published): a place has been seen along that road where criminals are camping, eating, standing, sitting, or lying down. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:2.9
Pali: Sappaṭibhayaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Dangerous: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:2.10
Pali: tasmiṁ magge corehi manussā hatā dissanti, viluttā dissanti, ākoṭitā dissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): criminals have been seen along that road, injuring, robbing, or beating people. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:2.11
Pali: Asatthikā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Without a group of travelers: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:2.12
Pali: vinā satthena. 
English (brahmali, published): in the absence of a group of travelers. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:2.13
Pali: Cārikaṁ careyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Goes wandering: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:2.14
Pali: kukkuṭasampāte gāme gāmantare gāmantare, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the inhabited areas are a chicken’s flight apart, then for every next inhabited area she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:2.15
Pali: Agāmake araññe addhayojane addhayojane āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When it is an uninhabited area, a wilderness, then for every six kilometers she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:2.16.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:2.17

English (brahmali, published): if she travels with a group; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:2.18
Pali: kheme appaṭibhaye gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she travels where it is safe and free from danger; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:2.19
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:2.20
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc38:2.21
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40
Root: root/pli/ms/vinaya/pli-tv-bi-vb/pli-tv-bi-vb-pc/pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40_root-pli-ms.json

  - brahmali [published]: translation/en/brahmali/vinaya/pli-tv-bi-vb/pli-tv-bi-vb-pc/pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40_translation-en-brahmali.json

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:0.2
Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:0.4
Pali: Tuvaṭṭavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on lying down 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:0.5
Pali: 40. Cārikanapakkamanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 40. Not wandering after the rainy season 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Rājagaha in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrel sanctuary. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo tattheva rājagahe vassaṁ vasanti, tattha hemantaṁ, tattha gimhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the nuns were staying right there at Rājagaha for the rainy season, the winter, and the summer. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:1.3
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:1.4
Pali: “āhundarikā bhikkhunīnaṁ disā andhakārā, na imāsaṁ disā pakkhāyantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “The nuns are leaving the districts in darkness and obscurity. They don’t brighten them up by their presence.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:1.5
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns heard the complaints of those people. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:1.6
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They then told the monks, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:1.7
Pali: Bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): who in turn told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:1.8
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:1.9
Pali: “tena hi, bhikkhave, bhikkhunīnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññapessāmi dasa atthavase paṭicca—
English (brahmali, published): “Well then, monks, I will lay down a training rule for the nuns for the following ten reasons: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:1.10
Pali: saṅghasuṭṭhutāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): for the well-being of the Sangha … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:1.11
Pali: saddhammaṭṭhitiyā vinayānuggahāya. 
English (brahmali, published): for the longevity of the true Teaching, and for supporting the training. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:1.12
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:1.13.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī vassaṁvuṭṭhā cārikaṁ na pakkameyya antamaso chappañcayojanānipi, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun who has completed the rainy-season residence does not go wandering at least 65 to 80 kilometers, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:2.1.5
Pali: Vassaṁvuṭṭhā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Who has completed the rainy-season residence: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:2.1.6
Pali: purimaṁ vā temāsaṁ pacchimaṁ vā temāsaṁ vuṭṭhā. 
English (brahmali, published): who has completed the first three or the last three months of the rainy-season residence. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:2.1.7
Pali: “Cārikaṁ na pakkamissāmi antamaso chappañcayojanānipī”ti dhuraṁ nikkhittamatte āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she thinks, “I won’t go wandering, not even 65 to 80 kilometers,” then by the mere fact of abandoning her duty, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:2.2.2
Pali: sati antarāye, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an obstacle; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:2.2.3
Pali: pariyesitvā dutiyikaṁ bhikkhuniṁ na labhati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she searches for a companion nun, but is unable to find one; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:2.2.4
Pali: gilānāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is sick; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:2.2.5
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:2.2.6
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:2.2.7
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:2.2.8
Pali: Dasamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The tenth training rule is finished. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc40:2.2.9
Pali: Tuvaṭṭavaggo catuttho. 
English (brahmali, published): The fourth subchapter on lying down is finished.

English translation sources:

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:0.2
Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:0.4
Pali: Cittāgāravagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on galleries 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:0.5
Pali: 41. Rājāgārasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 41. Visiting places of pleasure 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena rañño pasenadissa kosalassa uyyāne cittāgāre paṭibhānacittaṁ kataṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): artwork had been installed in the gallery in King Pasenadi of Kosala’s park. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:1.3
Pali: Bahū manussā cittāgāraṁ dassanāya gacchanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Many people visited the gallery, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:1.4
Pali: Chabbaggiyāpi bhikkhuniyo cittāgāraṁ dassanāya agamaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): as did the nuns from the group of six. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:1.5
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:1.6
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo cittāgāraṁ dassanāya gacchissanti, seyyathāpi gihiniyo kāmabhoginiyo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns visit a gallery? They’re just like householders who indulge in worldly pleasures!” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:1.7
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:1.8
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:1.9
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo cittāgāraṁ dassanāya gacchissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nuns from the group of six do this?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:1.10
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo cittāgāraṁ dassanāya gacchantī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that those nuns did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:1.11
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:1.12
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:1.13
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo cittāgāraṁ dassanāya gacchissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nuns from the group of six do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:1.14
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:1.15
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:1.16.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī rājāgāraṁ vā cittāgāraṁ vā ārāmaṁ vā uyyānaṁ vā pokkharaṇiṁ vā dassanāya gaccheyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun visits a royal house or a gallery or a park or a garden or a lotus pond, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.1.5
Pali: Rājāgāraṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A royal house: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.1.6
Pali: yattha katthaci rañño kīḷituṁ ramituṁ kataṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): wherever one has been built for a king to entertain and enjoy himself. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.1.7
Pali: Cittāgāraṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A gallery: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.1.8
Pali: yattha katthaci manussānaṁ kīḷituṁ ramituṁ kataṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): wherever one has been built for people to entertain and enjoy themselves. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.1.9
Pali: Ārāmo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A park: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.1.10
Pali: yattha katthaci manussānaṁ kīḷituṁ ramituṁ kato hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): wherever one has been made for people to entertain and enjoy themselves. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.1.11
Pali: Uyyānaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A garden: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.1.12
Pali: yattha katthaci manussānaṁ kīḷituṁ ramituṁ kataṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): wherever one has been made for people to entertain and enjoy themselves. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.1.13
Pali: Pokkharaṇī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A lotus pond: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.1.14
Pali: yattha katthaci manussānaṁ kīḷituṁ ramituṁ katā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): wherever one has been made for people to entertain and enjoy themselves. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.1.15
Pali: Dassanāya gacchati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is on her way to visit them, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.1.16
Pali: Yattha ṭhitā passati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Wherever she stands to see them, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.1.17
Pali: Dassanūpacāraṁ vijahitvā punappunaṁ passati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Every time she goes beyond the range of sight and then sees them again, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.1.18
Pali: Ekamekaṁ dassanāya gacchati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is on her way to visit any one of them, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.1.19
Pali: Yattha ṭhitā passati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Wherever she stands to see it, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.1.20
Pali: Dassanūpacāraṁ vijahitvā punappunaṁ passati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Every time she goes beyond the range of sight and then sees it again, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.2.2
Pali: ārāme ṭhitā passati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she sees it while remaining in a monastery; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.2.3
Pali: gacchantī vā āgacchantī vā passati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she sees it while coming or going; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.2.4
Pali: sati karaṇīye gantvā passati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she goes when there is something to be done and then sees it; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.2.5
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.2.6
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.2.7
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc41:2.2.8
Pali: Paṭhamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The first training rule is finished.

Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:0.3

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:0.4
Pali: Cittāgāravagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on galleries 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:0.5
Pali: 42. Āsandiparibhuñjanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 42. Using luxurious furniture 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo āsandimpi pallaṅkampi paribhuñjanti. 
English (brahmali, published): the nuns were using high and luxurious couches. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:1.3
Pali: Manussā vihāracārikaṁ āhiṇḍantā passitvā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): When people walking about the dwellings saw this, they complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:1.4
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo āsandimpi pallaṅkampi paribhuñjissanti, seyyathāpi gihiniyo kāmabhoginiyo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns use high and luxurious couches? They’re just like householders who indulge in worldly pleasures!” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:1.5
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:1.6
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:1.7
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo āsandimpi pallaṅkampi paribhuñjissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns do this?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:1.8
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo āsandimpi pallaṅkampi paribhuñjantī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:1.9
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:1.10
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:1.11
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo āsandimpi pallaṅkampi paribhuñjissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:1.12
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:1.13
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:1.14.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī āsandiṁ vā pallaṅkaṁ vā paribhuñjeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun uses a high or luxurious couch, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:2.1.5
Pali: Āsandī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A high couch: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:2.1.6
Pali: atikkantappamāṇā vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): one that is oversize is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:2.1.7
Pali: Pallaṅko nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A luxurious couch: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:2.1.8
Pali: āharimehi vāḷehi kato hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): one decorated with images of predatory animals. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:2.1.9
Pali: Paribhuñjeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Uses: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:2.1.10
Pali: tasmiṁ abhinisīdati vā abhinipajjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she sits down or lies down on it, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:2.2.2
Pali: āsandiyā pāde chinditvā paribhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she uses a high couch after cutting off the legs; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:2.2.3
Pali: pallaṅkassa vāḷe bhinditvā paribhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she uses a luxurious couch after removing the decorations with images of predatory animals; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:2.2.4
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:2.2.5
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc42:2.2.6
Pali: Dutiyasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The second training rule is finished.

Pali: 43. Suttakantanasikkhāpada

English (brahmali, published): the nuns from the group of six were spinning yarn.

Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns heard the complaints of those people,

English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc43:1.14.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī suttaṁ kanteyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun spins yarn, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc43:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc43:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc43:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc43:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc43:2.1.5
Pali: Suttaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Yarn: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc43:2.1.6
Pali: cha suttāni—
English (brahmali, published): there are six kinds of yarn: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc43:2.1.7
Pali: khomaṁ, kappāsikaṁ, koseyyaṁ, kambalaṁ, sāṇaṁ, bhaṅgaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): linen, cotton, silk, wool, sunn hemp, and hemp. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc43:2.1.8
Pali: Kanteyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Spins: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc43:2.1.9
Pali: sayaṁ kantati, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): if she spins it herself, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc43:2.1.10
Pali: Ujjavujjave āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For every pull, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc43:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc43:2.2.2
Pali: kantitasuttaṁ kantati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she spins yarn that has already been spun; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc43:2.2.3
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc43:2.2.4
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc43:2.2.5
Pali: Tatiyasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The third training rule is finished.

English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” …

English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc44:1.11.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī gihiveyyāvaccaṁ kareyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun provides services for a householder, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc44:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc44:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc44:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc44:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc44:2.1.5
Pali: Gihiveyyāvaccaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Services for a householder: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc44:2.1.6
Pali: agārikassa yāguṁ vā bhattaṁ vā khādanīyaṁ vā pacati, sāṭakaṁ vā veṭhanaṁ vā dhovati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she cooks congee, a meal, or fresh food for a householder, or she washes a wrap garment or a turban for them, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc44:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc44:2.2.2
Pali: yāgupāne, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a congee drink; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc44:2.2.3
Pali: saṅghabhatte, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is a meal for the Sangha; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc44:2.2.4
Pali: cetiyapūjāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is to venerate a shrine; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc44:2.2.5
Pali: attano veyyāvaccakarassa yāguṁ vā bhattaṁ vā khādanīyaṁ vā pacati, sāṭakaṁ vā veṭhanaṁ vā dhovati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she cooks congee, a meal, or fresh food for her own service provider, or she washes a wrap garment or a turban for them; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc44:2.2.6
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc44:2.2.7
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc44:2.2.8
Pali: Catutthasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The fourth training rule is finished.

Pali: 45. Adhikaraṇasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 45. Not resolving a legal issue 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:1.1

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:1.21.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā—‘ehāyye, imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasamehī’ti vuccamānā—‘sādhū’ti paṭissuṇitvā sā pacchā anantarāyikinī neva vūpasameyya na vūpasamāya ussukkaṁ kareyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If, when a nun is requested by a nun to resolve a legal issue, she agrees, but then neither resolves it nor makes any effort to resolve it, then, if there were no obstacles, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhuniyāti 
English (brahmali, published): By a nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.1.6
Pali: aññāya bhikkhuniyā. 
English (brahmali, published): by another nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.1.7
Pali: Adhikaraṇaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A legal issue: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.1.8
Pali: cattāri adhikaraṇāni— vivādādhikaraṇaṁ, anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ, kiccādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there are four kinds of legal issues: legal issues arising from disputes, legal issues arising from accusations, legal issues arising from offenses, legal issues arising from business. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.1.9
Pali: Ehāyye imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasamehīti 
English (brahmali, published): To resolve a legal issue: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.1.10
Pali: ehāyye imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vinicchehi. 
English (brahmali, published): to make a decision on a legal issue. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.1.11
Pali: Sā pacchā anantarāyikinīti 
English (brahmali, published): Then, if there were no obstacles: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.1.12
Pali: asati antarāye. 
English (brahmali, published): when there is no obstacle. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.1.13
Pali: Neva vūpasameyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Neither resolves it: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.1.14
Pali: na sayaṁ vūpasameyya. 
English (brahmali, published): she does not resolve it herself. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.1.15
Pali: Na vūpasamāya ussukkaṁ kareyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Nor makes any effort to resolve it: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.1.16
Pali: na aññaṁ āṇāpeyya. 
English (brahmali, published): she does not ask anyone else. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.1.17
Pali: “Neva vūpasamessāmi na vūpasamāya ussukkaṁ karissāmī”ti dhuraṁ nikkhittamatte, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, thinking, “I’ll neither resolve it nor make any effort to resolve it,” then, by the mere fact of abandoning her duty, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.2.1
Pali: Upasampannāya upasampannasaññā adhikaraṇaṁ neva vūpasameti na vūpasamāya ussukkaṁ karoti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, and she perceives her as such, and she neither resolves the legal issue nor makes any effort to resolve it, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.2.2
Pali: Upasampannāya vematikā adhikaraṇaṁ neva vūpasameti, na vūpasamāya ussukkaṁ karoti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, and she neither resolves the legal issue nor makes any effort to resolve it, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.2.3
Pali: Upasampannāya anupasampannasaññā adhikaraṇaṁ neva vūpasameti, na vūpasamāya ussukkaṁ karoti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is fully ordained, but she does not perceive her as such, and she neither resolves the legal issue nor makes any effort to resolve it, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.2.4
Pali: Anupasampannāya adhikaraṇaṁ neva vūpasameti, na vūpasamāya ussukkaṁ karoti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, and she neither resolves the legal issue nor makes any effort to resolve it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.2.5
Pali: Anupasampannāya upasampannasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but she perceives her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.2.6
Pali: Anupasampannāya vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.2.7
Pali: Anupasampannāya anupasampannasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the other person is not fully ordained, and she does not perceive her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.3.2
Pali: sati antarāye, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an obstacle; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.3.3
Pali: pariyesitvā na labhati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she searches, but is unable to find anyone to settle it; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.3.4
Pali: gilānāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is sick; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.3.5
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.3.6
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.3.7
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc45:2.3.8
Pali: Pañcamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The fifth training rule is finished.

English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:1.15.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī agārikassa vā paribbājakassa vā paribbājikāya vā sahatthā khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā dadeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun personally gives fresh or cooked food to a householder or a male wanderer or a female wanderer, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.1.5
Pali: Agāriko nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A householder: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.1.6
Pali: yo koci agāraṁ ajjhāvasati. 
English (brahmali, published): anyone who lives at home. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.1.7
Pali: Paribbājako nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A male wanderer: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.1.8
Pali: bhikkhuñca sāmaṇerañca ṭhapetvā yo koci paribbājakasamāpanno. 
English (brahmali, published): any male wanderer apart from Buddhist monks and novice monks. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.1.9
Pali: Paribbājikā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A female wanderer: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.1.10
Pali: bhikkhuniñca sikkhamānañca sāmaṇeriñca ṭhapetvā yā kāci paribbājikasamāpannā. 
English (brahmali, published): any female wanderer apart from Buddhist nuns, trainee nuns, and novice nuns. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.1.11
Pali: Khādanīyaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Fresh food: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.1.12
Pali: pañca bhojanāni—
English (brahmali, published): apart from the five cooked foods 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.1.13
Pali: udakadantaponaṁ ṭhapetvā avasesaṁ khādanīyaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): and water and tooth cleaners, the rest is called “fresh food”. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.1.14
Pali: Bhojanīyaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Cooked food: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.1.15
Pali: pañca bhojanāni—odano, kummāso, sattu, maccho, maṁsaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there are five kinds of cooked food: cooked grain, porridge, flour products, fish, and meat. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.1.16
Pali: Dadeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Gives: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.1.17
Pali: kāyena vā kāyapaṭibaddhena vā nissaggiyena vā deti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she gives by body or by what is connected to the body or by releasing, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.1.18
Pali: Udakadantaponaṁ deti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she gives water or tooth cleaners, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.2.2
Pali: dāpeti na deti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she does not give, but has it given; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.2.3
Pali: upanikkhipitvā deti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she gives by placing it near the person; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.2.4
Pali: bāhirālepaṁ deti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she gives ointments for external use; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.2.5
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.2.6
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc46:2.2.7
Pali: Chaṭṭhasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The sixth training rule is finished.

Pali: 47. Āvasathacīvarasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 47. Not relinquishing a communal robe 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī āvasathacīvaraṁ anissajjitvā paribhuñjati. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the nun Thullanandā did not relinquish the communal robe, but continued using it. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:1.3
Pali: Aññā utuniyo bhikkhuniyo na labhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Other menstruating nuns did not get to use it. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:1.4
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:1.7

English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.”

English (brahmali, published): “How could the nun Thullanandā do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:1.10

English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:1.11

English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:1.12.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī āvasathacīvaraṁ anissajjitvā paribhuñjeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:2.1.5
Pali: Āvasathacīvaraṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A communal robe: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:2.1.6
Pali: “utuniyo bhikkhuniyo paribhuñjantū”ti dinnaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): it is given specifically for the use of menstruating nuns. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:2.1.7
Pali: Anissajjitvā paribhuñjeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Does not relinquish, but continues to use it: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:2.1.8
Pali: dvetisso rattiyo paribhuñjitvā catutthadivase dhovitvā bhikkhuniyā vā sikkhamānāya vā sāmaṇeriyā vā anissajjitvā paribhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she uses it for two or three days, washes it on the fourth day, and then uses it again without relinquishing it to a nun or a trainee nun or a novice nun, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:2.2.1
Pali: Anissajjite anissajjitasaññā paribhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been relinquished, and she perceives that it has not, and she uses it, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:2.2.2
Pali: Anissajjite vematikā paribhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been relinquished, but she is unsure of it, and she uses it, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:2.2.3
Pali: Anissajjite nissajjitasaññā paribhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been relinquished, but she perceives that it has, and she uses it, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:2.2.4
Pali: Nissajjite anissajjitasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has been relinquished, but she perceives that it has not, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:2.2.5
Pali: Nissajjite vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has been relinquished, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:2.2.6
Pali: Nissajjite nissajjitasaññā anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has been relinquished, and she perceives that it has, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:2.3.2
Pali: nissajjitvā paribhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she relinquishes it and then uses it; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:2.3.3
Pali: puna pariyāyena paribhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she uses it again at the next turn; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:2.3.4
Pali: aññā utuniyo bhikkhuniyo na honti, 
English (brahmali, published): if there are no other menstruating nuns; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:2.3.5
Pali: acchinnacīvarikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if her robe has been stolen; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:2.3.6
Pali: naṭṭhacīvarikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if her robe has been lost; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:2.3.7
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:2.3.8
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:2.3.9
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc47:2.3.10
Pali: Sattamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The seventh training rule is finished.

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on galleries 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:0.5
Pali: 48. Āvasathavihārasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 48. Not relinquishing a lodging 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī āvasathaṁ anissajjitvā cārikaṁ pakkāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): the nun Thullanandā went wandering without first relinquishing her lodging. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:1.3
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā āvasatho ḍayhati. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards her lodging caught fire. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:1.4
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): Some nuns said, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:1.5
Pali: “handāyye, bhaṇḍakaṁ nīharāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Come, venerables, let’s remove her things.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:1.6
Pali: Ekaccā evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): But others replied, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:1.7
Pali: “na mayaṁ, ayye, nīharissāma. 
English (brahmali, published): “No, let’s not. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:1.8
Pali: Yaṁ kiñci naṭṭhaṁ sabbaṁ amhe abhiyuñjissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): She’ll just make us responsible for anything that gets lost.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:1.9
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī punadeva taṁ āvasathaṁ paccāgantvā bhikkhuniyo pucchi—
English (brahmali, published): When Thullanandā returned to her lodging, she asked the nuns, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:1.10
Pali: “apāyye, bhaṇḍakaṁ nīharitthā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, I hope you removed my things?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:1.11
Pali: “Na mayaṁ, ayye, nīharimhā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “No, we didn’t.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:1.12
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—
English (brahmali, published): Thullanandā complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:1.13
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo āvasathe ḍayhamāne bhaṇḍakaṁ na nīharissantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nuns not remove the contents when a lodging is burning?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:1.14

English (brahmali, published): But the nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:1.20

English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:1.21
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:1.22.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī āvasathaṁ anissajjitvā cārikaṁ pakkameyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun goes wandering without relinquishing her lodging, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.1.5
Pali: Āvasatho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Lodging: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.1.6
Pali: kavāṭabaddho vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): one with a door is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.1.7
Pali: Anissajjitvā cārikaṁ pakkameyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Goes wandering without relinquishing: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.1.8
Pali: bhikkhuniyā vā sikkhamānāya vā sāmaṇeriyā vā anissajjitvā parikkhittassa āvasathassa parikkhepaṁ atikkāmentiyā āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she crosses the boundary of an enclosed lodging without first relinquishing it to a nun, a trainee nun, or a novice nun, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.1.9
Pali: Aparikkhittassa āvasathassa upacāraṁ atikkāmentiyā āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she goes beyond the vicinity of an unenclosed lodging, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.2.1
Pali: Anissajjite anissajjitasaññā pakkamati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been relinquished, and she perceives that it has not, and she goes, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.2.2
Pali: Anissajjite vematikā pakkamati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been relinquished, but she is unsure of it, and she goes, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.2.3
Pali: Anissajjite nissajjitasaññā pakkamati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has not been relinquished, but she perceives that it has, and she goes, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.2.4
Pali: Akavāṭabaddhaṁ anissajjitvā pakkamati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she does not relinquish a lodging without a door, and then goes, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.2.5
Pali: Nissajjite anissajjitasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has been relinquished, but she perceives that it has not, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.2.6
Pali: Nissajjite vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has been relinquished, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.2.7
Pali: Nissajjite nissajjitasaññā, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If it has been relinquished, and she perceives that it has, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.3.2
Pali: nissajjitvā pakkamati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she relinquishes it and then goes; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.3.3
Pali: sati antarāye, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an obstacle; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.3.4
Pali: pariyesitvā na labhati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she searches, but is unable to find anyone to relinquish it to; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.3.5
Pali: gilānāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is sick; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.3.6
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.3.7
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.3.8
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc48:2.3.9
Pali: Aṭṭhamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The eighth training rule is finished.

Pali: 49. Tiracchānavijjāpariyāpuṇanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 49. Studying worldly subjects 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo tiracchānavijjaṁ pariyāpuṇanti.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:1.3
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:1.4

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:1.5
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:1.6
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:1.7
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo tiracchānavijjaṁ pariyāpuṇissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the nuns from the group of six do this?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:1.8
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo tiracchānavijjaṁ pariyāpuṇantī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that those nuns do this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:1.9
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:1.10
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:1.11

English (brahmali, published): “How can the nuns from the group of six do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:1.12
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:1.13
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:1.14.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī tiracchānavijjaṁ pariyāpuṇeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun studies worldly subjects, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:2.1.5
Pali: Tiracchānavijjā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Worldly subjects: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:2.1.6
Pali: yaṁ kiñci bāhirakaṁ anatthasaṁhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): whatever is external to the Buddha’s Teaching, not connected with the goal. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:2.1.7
Pali: Pariyāpuṇeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Learns: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:2.1.8
Pali: padena pariyāpuṇāti, pade pade āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she learns by the line, then for every line she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:2.1.9
Pali: Akkharāya pariyāpuṇāti, akkharakkharāya āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she learns by the syllable, then for every syllable she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:2.2.2
Pali: lekhaṁ pariyāpuṇāti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she learns writing; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:2.2.3
Pali: dhāraṇaṁ pariyāpuṇāti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she learns protective verses; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:2.2.4
Pali: guttatthāya parittaṁ pariyāpuṇāti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she learns verses for the purpose of protection; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:2.2.5
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:2.2.6
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc49:2.2.7
Pali: Navamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The ninth training rule is finished.
