pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:0.4
Pali: Ārāmavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on monasteries 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:0.5
Pali: 51. Ārāmapavisanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 51. Entering a monks’ monastery 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhū gāmakāvāse ekacīvarā cīvarakammaṁ karonti. 
English (brahmali, published): a number of monks dressed only in sarongs were making robes in a certain village monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:1.3
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo anāpucchā ārāmaṁ pavisitvā yena te bhikkhū tenupasaṅkamiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Nuns entered that monastery without asking permission and approached those monks. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:1.4
Pali: Bhikkhū ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The monks complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:1.5
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo anāpucchā ārāmaṁ pavisissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns enter a monastery without asking permission?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:1.6
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo anāpucchā ārāmaṁ pavisantī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that nuns did that?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:1.7
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:1.8
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:1.9
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo anāpucchā ārāmaṁ pavisissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns do that? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:1.10
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:1.11
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:1.12.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī anāpucchā ārāmaṁ paviseyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun enters a monastery without asking permission, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:1.13
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhunīnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the nuns. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena te bhikkhū tamhā āvāsā pakkamiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards those monks left that monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:2.2
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo—“ayyā pakkantā”ti ārāmaṁ nāgamaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): But even though the nuns had heard that the monks had left, they did not go there. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:2.3
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū punadeva taṁ āvāsaṁ paccāgacchiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon the monks returned. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:2.4
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo—“ayyā āgatā”ti āpucchā ārāmaṁ pavisitvā yena te bhikkhū tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; 
English (brahmali, published): When the nuns heard that this was the case, they asked permission and then entered that monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:2.5
Pali: upasaṅkamitvā te bhikkhū abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): They approached the monks and bowed down to them. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:2.6
Pali: Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho tā bhikkhuniyo te bhikkhū etadavocuṁ—“kissa tumhe, bhaginiyo, ārāmaṁ neva sammajjittha na pānīyaṁ paribhojanīyaṁ upaṭṭhāpitthā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): The monks then said to them, “Sisters, why didn’t you sweep the monastery, or put out water for drinking or water for washing?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:2.7
Pali: “Bhagavatā, ayyā, sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ—‘na anāpucchā ārāmo pavisitabbo’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “The Buddha has laid down a training rule that we may not enter a monastery without asking permission. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:2.8
Pali: Tena mayaṁ na āgamimhā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): That’s why we didn’t do it.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:2.9
Pali: Bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. Soon afterwards the Buddha had the Sangha gathered and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:2.10
Pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, santaṁ bhikkhuṁ āpucchā ārāmaṁ pavisituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, when a monk is available, a nun should enter a monastery only after asking permission. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:2.11
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:2.12.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī santaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā ārāmaṁ paviseyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun, when a monk is available, enters a monastery without asking permission, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:2.13
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhunīnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the nuns. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:3.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena te bhikkhū tamhā āvāsā pakkamitvā punadeva taṁ āvāsaṁ paccāgacchiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): After leaving that monastery once more, the monks returned yet again. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:3.2
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo—“ayyā pakkantā”ti anāpucchā ārāmaṁ pavisiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Thinking that the monks were still away, the nuns entered the monastery without asking permission. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:3.3
Pali: Tāsaṁ kukkuccaṁ ahosi—“bhagavatā sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ—‘na santaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā ārāmo pavisitabbo’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns became anxious, thinking, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:3.4
Pali: Mayañcamhā santaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā ārāmaṁ pavisimhā. 
English (brahmali, published): “We have entered a monastery without asking permission, even though a monk was available. And the Buddha has laid down a training rule against this. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:3.5
Pali: Kacci nu kho mayaṁ pācittiyaṁ āpattiṁ āpannā”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): Could it be that we have committed an offense entailing confession?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:3.6
Pali: bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:3.7
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards he gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:3.8
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And so, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:3.9.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī jānaṁ sabhikkhukaṁ ārāmaṁ anāpucchā paviseyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun, knowing that there are monks in a monastery, enters it without asking permission, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.1.5
Pali: Jānāti nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Knowing: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.1.6
Pali: sāmaṁ vā jānāti, aññe vā tassā ārocenti, te vā ārocenti. 
English (brahmali, published): she knows it by herself or others have told her or they have told her. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.1.7
Pali: Sabhikkhuko nāma ārāmo 
English (brahmali, published): There are monks in a monastery: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.1.8
Pali: yattha bhikkhū rukkhamūlepi vasanti. 
English (brahmali, published): even where monks stay at the foot of a tree. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.1.9
Pali: Anāpucchā ārāmaṁ paviseyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Enters it without asking permission: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.1.10
Pali: bhikkhuṁ vā sāmaṇeraṁ vā ārāmikaṁ vā anāpucchā parikkhittassa ārāmassa parikkhepaṁ atikkāmentiyā āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she crosses the boundary of an enclosed monastery without asking permission of a monk or a novice monk or a monastery worker, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.1.11
Pali: Aparikkhittassa ārāmassa upacāraṁ okkamantiyā āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she enters the vicinity of an unenclosed monastery, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.2.1
Pali: Sabhikkhuke sabhikkhukasaññā santaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā ārāmaṁ pavisati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If there are monks in a monastery, and she perceives that there are, and she enters it without asking permission of an available monk, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.2.2
Pali: Sabhikkhuke vematikā santaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā ārāmaṁ pavisati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If there are monks in a monastery, but she is unsure of it, and she enters it without asking permission of an available monk, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.2.3
Pali: Sabhikkhuke abhikkhukasaññā santaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā ārāmaṁ pavisati, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If there are monks in a monastery, but she does not perceive that there are, and she enters it without asking permission of an available monk, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.2.4
Pali: Abhikkhuke sabhikkhukasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If there are no monks in a monastery, but she perceives that there are, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.2.5
Pali: Abhikkhuke vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If there are no monks in a monastery, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.2.6
Pali: Abhikkhuke abhikkhukasaññā, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If there are no monks in a monastery, and she does not perceive that there are, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.3.2
Pali: santaṁ bhikkhuṁ āpucchā pavisati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she enters after asking permission of an available monk; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.3.3
Pali: asantaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā pavisati, 
English (brahmali, published): if, when there is no available monk, she enters without asking permission; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.3.4
Pali: sīsānulokikā gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she goes while looking at the head of a nun in front of her; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.3.5
Pali: yattha bhikkhuniyo sannipatitā honti tattha gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is going to where the nuns have gathered; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.3.6
Pali: ārāmena maggo hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if a path goes through the monastery; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.3.7
Pali: gilānāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is sick; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.3.8
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.3.9
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.3.10
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc51:4.3.11
Pali: Paṭhamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The first training rule is finished.

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on monasteries 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:0.5
Pali: 52. Bhikkhuakkosanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 52. Abusing a monk 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying  in the house with the peaked roof in the Great Wood near Vesālī, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmato upālissa upajjhāyo āyasmā kappitako susāne viharati. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Upāli’s preceptor, Venerable Kappita, was staying in a charnel ground. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.3
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyānaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ mahattarā bhikkhunī kālaṅkatā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the leader of the nuns from the group of six had just died. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.4
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo taṁ bhikkhuniṁ nīharitvā āyasmato kappitakassa vihārassa avidūre jhāpetvā thūpaṁ katvā gantvā tasmiṁ thūpe rodanti. 
English (brahmali, published): They took her to that charnel ground, cremated her near Venerable Kappita’s dwelling, and made a stupa. They then went there to cry. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.5
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā kappitako tena saddena ubbāḷho taṁ thūpaṁ bhinditvā pakiresi. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Kappita was disturbed by the noise, and so he demolished the stupa and scattered the rubble. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.6
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns from the group of six thought, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.7
Pali: “iminā kappitakena amhākaṁ ayyāya thūpo bhinno, handa naṁ ghātemā”ti mantesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Kappita has demolished our venerable’s stupa; let’s kill him,” and they laid a plan. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.8
Pali: Aññatarā bhikkhunī āyasmato upālissa etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): A certain nun told Venerable Upāli what was happening, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.9
Pali: Āyasmā upāli āyasmato kappitakassa etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): and he in turn told Venerable Kappita. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.10
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā kappitako vihārā nikkhamitvā nilīno acchi. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Kappita then left his dwelling and went into hiding. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.11
Pali: Atha kho chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo yenāyasmato kappitakassa vihāro tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the nuns from the group of six went to his dwelling 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.12
Pali: upasaṅkamitvā āyasmato kappitakassa vihāraṁ pāsāṇehi ca leḍḍūhi ca ottharāpetvā, “mato kappitako”ti pakkamiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): and crushed it with rocks and lumps of earth. When they thought he was dead, they left. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.13
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā kappitako tassā rattiyā accayena pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya vesāliṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi. 
English (brahmali, published): The following morning Venerable Kappita robed up, took his bowl and robe, and entered Vesālī for almsfood. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.14
Pali: Addasaṁsu kho chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo āyasmantaṁ kappitakaṁ piṇḍāya carantaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When the nuns from the group of six saw him, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.15
Pali: Disvāna evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): they said, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.16
Pali: “ayaṁ kappitako jīvati, ko nu kho amhākaṁ mantaṁ saṁharī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Kappita is alive! Who spoiled our plan?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.17
Pali: Assosuṁ kho chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo—
English (brahmali, published): When they heard 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.18
Pali: “ayyena kira upālinā amhākaṁ manto saṁhaṭo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): it was Venerable Upāli, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.19
Pali: Tā āyasmantaṁ upāliṁ akkosiṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): they abused him, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.20
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayaṁ kāsāvaṭo malamajjano nihīnajacco amhākaṁ mantaṁ saṁharissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could this barber, this dirt remover of inferior caste, spoil our plan?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.21
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.22
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo ayyaṁ upāliṁ akkosissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nuns from the group of six abuse Venerable Upāli?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.23
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo upāliṁ akkosantī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that those nuns did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.24
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.25
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.26
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo upāliṁ akkosissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nuns from the group of six do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.27
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.28
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:1.29.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhuṁ akkoseyya vā paribhāseyya vā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun abuses or reviles a monk, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:2.1.5
Pali: Bhikkhunti 
English (brahmali, published): A monk: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:2.1.6
Pali: upasampannaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): one who is fully ordained. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:2.1.7
Pali: Akkoseyya vāti 
English (brahmali, published): Abuses: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:2.1.8
Pali: dasahi vā akkosavatthūhi akkosati etesaṁ vā aññatarena, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she abuses with the ten kinds of abuse or with any one of them, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:2.1.9
Pali: Paribhāseyya vāti 
English (brahmali, published): Reviles: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:2.1.10
Pali: bhayaṁ upadaṁseti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she induces fear, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:2.2.1
Pali: Upasampanne upasampannasaññā akkosati vā paribhāsati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is fully ordained, and she perceives him as such, and she abuses or reviles him, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:2.2.2
Pali: Upasampanne vematikā akkosati vā paribhāsati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, and she abuses or reviles him, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:2.2.3
Pali: Upasampanne anupasampannasaññā akkosati vā paribhāsati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is fully ordained, but she does not perceive him as such, and she abuses or reviles him, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:2.2.4
Pali: Anupasampannaṁ akkosati vā paribhāsati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she abuses or reviles someone who is not fully ordained, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:2.2.5
Pali: Anupasampanne upasampannasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is not fully ordained, but she perceives him as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:2.2.6
Pali: Anupasampanne vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is not fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:2.2.7
Pali: Anupasampanne anupasampannasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is not fully ordained, and she does not perceive him as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:2.3.2
Pali: atthapurekkhārāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is aiming at something beneficial; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:2.3.3
Pali: dhammapurekkhārāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is aiming at giving a teaching; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:2.3.4
Pali: anusāsanipurekkhārāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is aiming at giving an instruction; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:2.3.5
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:2.3.6
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc52:2.3.7
Pali: Dutiyasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The second training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53
Root: root/pli/ms/vinaya/pli-tv-bi-vb/pli-tv-bi-vb-pc/pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53_root-pli-ms.json

English translation sources:
  - brahmali [published]: translation/en/brahmali/vinaya/pli-tv-bi-vb/pli-tv-bi-vb-pc/pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53_translation-en-brahmali.json

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:0.2
Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:0.4
Pali: Ārāmavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on monasteries 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:0.5
Pali: 53. Gaṇaparibhāsanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 53. Reviling the community 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī bhaṇḍanakārikā hoti kalahakārikā vivādakārikā bhassakārikā saṅghe adhikaraṇakārikā. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the nun Caṇḍakāḷī was quarrelsome and argumentative, and she created legal issues in the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.3
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī tassā kamme karīyamāne paṭikkosati. 
English (brahmali, published): But when a legal procedure was being done against her, the nun Thullanandā objected. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.4
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī gāmakaṁ agamāsi kenacideva karaṇīyena. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards when Thullanandā went to a village on some business, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.5
Pali: Atha kho bhikkhunisaṅgho—
English (brahmali, published): the Sangha of nuns 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.6
Pali: “thullanandā bhikkhunī pakkantā”ti caṇḍakāḷiṁ bhikkhuniṁ āpattiyā adassane ukkhipi. 
English (brahmali, published): took the opportunity to eject Caṇḍakāḷī for not recognizing an offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.7
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī gāmake taṁ karaṇīyaṁ tīretvā punadeva sāvatthiṁ paccāgacchi. 
English (brahmali, published): When Thullanandā had finished her business, she returned to Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.8
Pali: Caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā āgacchantiyā neva āsanaṁ paññapesi na pādodakaṁ pādapīṭhaṁ pādakaṭhalikaṁ upanikkhipi; 
English (brahmali, published): As she was approaching, Caṇḍakāḷī neither prepared a seat for her, nor set out a foot stool, a foot scraper, or water for washing the feet; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.9
Pali: na paccuggantvā pattacīvaraṁ paṭiggahesi na pānīyena āpucchi. 
English (brahmali, published): and she did not go out to meet her to receive her bowl and robe or to ask whether she wanted water to drink. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.10
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī caṇḍakāḷiṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Thullanandā asked her 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.11
Pali: “kissa tvaṁ, ayye, mayi āgacchantiyā neva āsanaṁ paññapesi na pādodakaṁ pādapīṭhaṁ pādakaṭhalikaṁ upanikkhipi; 
English (brahmali, published): why she was acting like this. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.12
Pali: na paccuggantvā pattacīvaraṁ paṭiggahesi na pānīyena āpucchī”ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.13
Pali: “Evañhetaṁ, ayye, hoti yathā taṁ anāthāyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): She replied, “That’s how it is, venerable, when one doesn’t have a protector.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.14
Pali: “Kissa pana tvaṁ, ayye, anāthā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “But how is it, venerable, that you don’t have a protector?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.15
Pali: “Imā maṁ, ayye, bhikkhuniyo—
English (brahmali, published): “When these nuns knew 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.16
Pali: ‘ayaṁ anāthā appaññātā, natthi imissā kāci pativattā’ti, āpattiyā adassane ukkhipiṁsū”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): that no-one would speak up for me because I am not esteemed by them and I didn’t have a protector, they ejected me for not recognizing an offense.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.17
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī—
English: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.18
Pali: “bālā etā abyattā etā netā jānanti kammaṁ vā kammadosaṁ vā kammavipattiṁ vā kammasampattiṁ vā”ti, caṇḍīkatā gaṇaṁ paribhāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): “They are incompetent fools! They don’t understand legal procedures, nor what makes them fail or succeed.” And being furious, she reviled the community. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.19
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.20
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā caṇḍīkatā gaṇaṁ paribhāsissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Thullanandā revile the community because she is furious?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.21
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī caṇḍīkatā gaṇaṁ paribhāsatī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.22
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.23
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.24
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī caṇḍīkatā gaṇaṁ paribhāsissati. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nun Thullanandā do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.25
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.26
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:1.27.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī caṇḍīkatā gaṇaṁ paribhāseyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a furious nun reviles the community, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:2.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:2.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:2.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:2.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:2.5
Pali: Caṇḍīkatā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Furious: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:2.6
Pali: kodhanā vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): angry is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:2.7
Pali: Gaṇo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The community: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:2.8
Pali: bhikkhunisaṅgho vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): the Sangha of nuns is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:2.9
Pali: Paribhāseyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Reviles: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:2.10
Pali: “bālā etā abyattā etā netā jānanti kammaṁ vā kammadosaṁ vā kammavipattiṁ vā kammasampattiṁ vā”ti paribhāsati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she reviles them, saying, “They are incompetent fools. They don’t understand legal procedures, nor what makes them fail or succeed,” she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:2.11
Pali: Sambahulā bhikkhuniyo vā ekaṁ bhikkhuniṁ vā anupasampannaṁ vā paribhāsati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she reviles several nuns, a single nun, or someone who is not fully ordained, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:2.12.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:2.13
Pali: atthapurekkhārāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is aiming at something beneficial; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:2.14
Pali: dhammapurekkhārāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is aiming at giving a teaching; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:2.15
Pali: anusāsanipurekkhārāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is aiming at giving an instruction; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:2.16
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:2.17
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc53:2.18
Pali: Tatiyasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The third training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:0.2
Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:0.4
Pali: Ārāmavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on monasteries 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:0.5
Pali: 54. Pavāritasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 54. Eating more after refusing an offer 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro brāhmaṇo bhikkhuniyo nimantetvā bhojesi. 
English (brahmali, published): a certain brahmin had invited the nuns for a meal. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:1.3
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo bhuttāvī pavāritā ñātikulāni gantvā ekaccā bhuñjiṁsu ekaccā piṇḍapātaṁ ādāya agamaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): When the nuns had finished and refused an offer to eat more, they went to their respective families, where some ate and others got almsfood and left. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:1.4
Pali: Atha kho so brāhmaṇo paṭivissake etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards that brahmin said this to his neighbors, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:1.5
Pali: “bhikkhuniyo mayā, ayyā, santappitā, etha tumhepi santappessāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I have satisfied the nuns. Come, and I’ll satisfy you, too.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:1.6
Pali: Te evamāhaṁsu—
English: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:1.7
Pali: “kiṁ tvaṁ, ayyo, amhe santappessasi. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could you satisfy us? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:1.8
Pali: Yāpi tayā nimantitā tāpi amhākaṁ gharāni āgantvā ekaccā bhuñjiṁsu ekaccā piṇḍapātaṁ ādāya agamaṁsū”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Those nuns who were invited by you came to our houses, where some ate and others got almsfood and left.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:1.9
Pali: Atha kho so brāhmaṇo ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—
English (brahmali, published): That brahmin complained and criticized those nuns, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:1.10
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo amhākaṁ ghare bhuñjitvā aññatra bhuñjissanti, na cāhaṁ paṭibalo yāvadatthaṁ dātun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could they eat in our house and afterwards eat elsewhere? Am I not able to give them as much as they want?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:1.11
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tassa brāhmaṇassa ujjhāyantassa khiyyantassa vipācentassa. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns heard the complaints of that brahmin, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:1.12
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:1.13
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo bhuttāvī pavāritā aññatra bhuñjissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could nuns finish their meal, refuse an offer to eat more, and then eat elsewhere?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:1.14
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo bhuttāvī pavāritā aññatra bhuñjantī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that nuns did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:1.15
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:1.16
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:1.17
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo bhuttāvī pavāritā aññatra bhuñjissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could nuns act in this way? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:1.18
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:1.19
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:1.20.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī nimantitā vā pavāritā vā khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā khādeyya vā bhuñjeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun, who has been invited to a meal, refuses an offer to eat more, and then eats fresh or cooked food, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.1.5
Pali: Nimantitā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Who has been invited: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.1.6
Pali: pañcannaṁ bhojanānaṁ aññatarena bhojanena nimantitā. 
English (brahmali, published): who has been invited to eat any of the five cooked foods. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.1.7
Pali: Pavāritā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Refuses an offer to eat more: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.1.8
Pali: asanaṁ paññāyati, bhojanaṁ paññāyati, hatthapāse ṭhitā abhiharati, paṭikkhepo paññāyati. 
English (brahmali, published): there is eating; there is cooked food; they stand within arm’s reach; there is an offering; there is a refusal. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.1.9
Pali: Khādanīyaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Fresh food: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.1.10
Pali: pañca bhojanāni yāguṁ yāmakālikaṁ sattāhakālikaṁ yāvajīvikaṁ ṭhapetvā 
English (brahmali, published): apart from the five cooked foods, congee, the post-midday tonics, the seven-day tonics, and the lifetime tonics, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.1.11
Pali: avasesaṁ khādanīyaṁ nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): the rest is called “fresh food”. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.1.12
Pali: Bhojanīyaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Cooked food: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.1.13
Pali: pañca bhojanāni—odano, kummāso, sattu, maccho, maṁsaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there are five kinds of cooked food: cooked grain, porridge, flour products, fish, and meat. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.1.14
Pali: “Khādissāmi bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she receives it with the intention of eating it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.1.15
Pali: Ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For every mouthful swallowed, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.2.1
Pali: Nimantite nimantitasaññā khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā khādati vā bhuñjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she has been invited, and she perceives that she has, and she eats fresh or cooked food, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.2.2
Pali: Nimantite vematikā khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā khādati vā bhuñjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she has been invited, but she is unsure of it, and she eats fresh or cooked food, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.2.3
Pali: Nimantite animantitasaññā khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā khādati vā bhuñjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she has has been invited, but she does not perceive that she has, and she eats fresh or cooked food, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.2.4
Pali: Yāmakālikaṁ sattāhakālikaṁ yāvajīvikaṁ āhāratthāya paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she receives post-midday tonics, seven-day tonics, or lifetime tonics for the purpose of food, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.2.5
Pali: Ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti dukkaṭassa …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): For every mouthful swallowed, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.2.6.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.2.7
Pali: nimantitā appavāritā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she has been invited, but has not refused an offer to eat more; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.2.8
Pali: yāguṁ pivati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she drinks congee; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.2.9
Pali: sāmike apaloketvā bhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she eats more after getting permission from the person who invited her; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.2.10
Pali: yāmakālikaṁ sattāhakālikaṁ yāvajīvikaṁ sati paccaye paribhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if, when there is a reason, she uses post-midday tonics, seven-day tonics, or lifetime tonics; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.2.11
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.2.12
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc54:2.2.13
Pali: Catutthasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The fourth training rule is finished.

Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:0.4
Pali: Ārāmavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on monasteries 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:0.5
Pali: 55. Kulamaccharinīsikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 55. Being stingy with families 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarā bhikkhunī sāvatthiyaṁ aññatarissā visikhāya piṇḍāya caramānā yena aññataraṁ kulaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): a certain nun there was walking for almsfood along a street. She then went to a certain family where she sat down on the prepared seat. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:1.3
Pali: Atha kho te manussā taṁ bhikkhuniṁ bhojetvā etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The people there gave her a meal and said, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:1.4
Pali: “aññāpi, ayye, bhikkhuniyo āgacchantū”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, other nuns may come too.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:1.5
Pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī—
English (brahmali, published): Then that nun thought, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:1.6
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo nāgaccheyyun”ti, bhikkhuniyo upasaṅkamitvā etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): “What can I do so that other nuns don’t come?” And she went up to other nuns and said, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:1.7
Pali: “amukasmiṁ, ayye, okāse vāḷā sunakhā caṇḍo balibaddo cikkhallo okāso. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, in such-and-such a place there are malicious dogs, a fierce ox, and it’s muddy. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:1.8
Pali: Mā kho tattha agamitthā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Don’t go there.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:1.9
Pali: Aññatarāpi bhikkhunī tassā visikhāya piṇḍāya caramānā yena taṁ kulaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): Another nun, too, went to that family while walking for almsfood along that street. She sat down on the prepared seat 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:1.10
Pali: Atha kho te manussā taṁ bhikkhuniṁ bhojetvā etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): and the people there gave her a meal. They then asked her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:1.11
Pali: “kissa, ayye, bhikkhuniyo na āgacchantī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, why don’t the nuns come here?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:1.12
Pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī tesaṁ manussānaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): And she told them what had happened. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:1.13
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:1.14
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhunī kulaṁ maccharāyissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can a nun keep a family to herself?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:1.15
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī kulaṁ maccharāyatī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that a nun acted like this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:1.16
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:1.17
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:1.18
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī kulaṁ maccharāyissati. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could a nun act like this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:1.19
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:1.20
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:1.21.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī kulamaccharinī assa, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun keeps a family to herself, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:2.1.5
Pali: Kulaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A family: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:2.1.6
Pali: cattāri kulāni— khattiyakulaṁ, brāhmaṇakulaṁ, vessakulaṁ, suddakulaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there are four kinds of families: the aristocratic family, the brahmin family, the merchant family, the worker family. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:2.1.7
Pali: Maccharinī assāti 
English (brahmali, published): Keeps for herself: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:2.1.8
Pali: “kathaṁ bhikkhuniyo nāgaccheyyun”ti bhikkhunīnaṁ santike kulassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she thinks, “What can I do so that other nuns don’t come?” and she then speaks disparagingly about that family to the nuns, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:2.1.9
Pali: Kulassa vā santike bhikkhunīnaṁ avaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she speaks disparagingly about that family to the nuns, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:2.2.2
Pali: kulaṁ na maccharāyantī santaṁyeva ādīnavaṁ ācikkhati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is not keeping a family to herself, but describes the drawbacks as they are; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:2.2.3
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:2.2.4
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc55:2.2.5
Pali: Pañcamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The fifth training rule is finished.

English (brahmali, published): 56. Spending the rainy season apart from monks 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc56:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc56:1.2

English (brahmali, published): At that time a number of nuns who had completed the rainy-season residence in a village monastery went to Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc56:1.3

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc56:1.5

English (brahmali, published): We hope the instruction was effective?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc56:1.6
Pali: “Natthayye, tattha bhikkhū; 
English (brahmali, published): “There were no monks there. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc56:1.7
Pali: kuto ovādo iddho bhavissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): So how could the instruction be effective?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc56:1.8

English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc56:1.16.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī abhikkhuke āvāse vassaṁ vaseyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun spends the rainy-season residence in a monastery without monks, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc56:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc56:2.1.2

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc56:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc56:2.1.5
Pali: Abhikkhuko nāma āvāso 
English (brahmali, published): A monastery without monks: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc56:2.1.6
Pali: na sakkā hoti ovādāya vā saṁvāsāya vā gantuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): she is not able to go to the instruction or to a formal meeting of the community. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc56:2.1.7
Pali: “Vassaṁ vasissāmī”ti senāsanaṁ paññapeti pānīyaṁ paribhojanīyaṁ upaṭṭhapeti pariveṇaṁ sammajjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she thinks, “I’ll stay here for the rainy-season residence,” and she prepares a dwelling, sets out water for drinking and water for washing, and sweeps the yard, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc56:2.1.8
Pali: Saha aruṇuggamanā āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): At dawn, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc56:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc56:2.2.2
Pali: vassupagatā bhikkhū pakkantā vā honti vibbhantā vā kālaṅkatā vā pakkhasaṅkantā vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if the monks who entered the rainy-season residence there depart or disrobe or die or join another religion or sect; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc56:2.2.3
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc56:2.2.4
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc56:2.2.5
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc56:2.2.6
Pali: Chaṭṭhasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The sixth training rule is finished.

Pali: 57. Apavāraṇāsikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 57. Not doing the invitation ceremony 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc57:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

English (brahmali, published): The nuns there asked them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc57:1.4

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc57:1.5
Pali: kattha bhikkhusaṅgho pavārito”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): Where did you invite the Sangha of monks for correction?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc57:1.6
Pali: “Na mayaṁ, ayye, bhikkhusaṅghaṁ pavāremā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “We didn’t invite the Sangha of monks for correction.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc57:1.7

English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

English (brahmali, published): “How could nuns who have completed the rains residence not invite the Sangha of monks for correction?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc57:1.9
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo vassaṁvuṭṭhā bhikkhusaṅghaṁ na pavārentī”ti?

Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc57:1.14

English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc57:1.15.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī vassaṁvuṭṭhā ubhatosaṅghe tīhi ṭhānehi na pavāreyya diṭṭhena vā sutena vā parisaṅkāya vā, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun who has completed the rainy-season residence does not invite correction from both Sanghas in regard to three things—what has been seen, heard, or suspected—she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc57:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc57:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc57:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc57:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc57:2.1.5
Pali: Vassaṁvuṭṭhā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Who has completed the rainy-season residence: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc57:2.1.6
Pali: purimaṁ vā temāsaṁ pacchimaṁ vā temāsaṁ vuṭṭhā. 
English (brahmali, published): who has completed the first three or the last three months of the rainy-season residence. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc57:2.1.7
Pali: Ubhatosaṅghe tīhi ṭhānehi na pavāressāmi diṭṭhena vā sutena vā parisaṅkāya vā”ti dhuraṁ nikkhittamatte āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she thinks, “I won’t invite correction from both Sanghas in regard to three things—what has been seen, heard, or suspected,” then by the mere fact of abandoning her duty, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc57:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc57:2.2.2
Pali: sati antarāye, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an obstacle; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc57:2.2.3
Pali: pariyesitvā na labhati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she searches, but does not find anyone to invite for correction; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc57:2.2.4
Pali: gilānāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is sick; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc57:2.2.5
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc57:2.2.6
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc57:2.2.7
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc57:2.2.8
Pali: Sattamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The seventh training rule is finished.

Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc58:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc58:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc58:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc58:2.1.5
Pali: Ovādo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The instruction: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc58:2.1.6
Pali: aṭṭha garudhammā. 
English (brahmali, published): the eight important principles. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc58:2.1.7
Pali: Saṁvāso nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The community: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc58:2.1.8
Pali: ekakammaṁ ekuddeso samasikkhatā. 
English (brahmali, published): joint legal procedures, a joint recitation, the same training. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc58:2.1.9
Pali: Ovādāya vā saṁvāsāya vā na gacchissāmīti dhuraṁ nikkhittamatte āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she thinks, “I won’t go to the instruction or to formal meetings of the community,” then by the mere fact of abandoning her duty, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc58:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc58:2.2.2
Pali: sati antarāye, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an obstacle; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc58:2.2.3
Pali: pariyesitvā dutiyikaṁ bhikkhuniṁ na labhati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she searches for a companion nun, but does not find one; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc58:2.2.4
Pali: gilānāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is sick; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc58:2.2.5
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc58:2.2.6
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc58:2.2.7
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc58:2.2.8
Pali: Aṭṭhamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The eighth training rule is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc59:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc59:1.6

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc59:1.10

English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc59:1.11.1
Pali: “Anvaddhamāsaṁ bhikkhuniyā bhikkhusaṅghato dve dhammā paccāsīsitabbā—uposathapucchakañca ovādūpasaṅkamanañca. Taṁ atikkāmentiyā pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Every half-month a nun should seek two things from the Sangha of monks: asking it about the observance day and going to it for the instruction. If she lets the half-month pass, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc59:2.5

English (brahmali, published): The instruction: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc59:2.6
Pali: aṭṭha garudhammā.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc59:2.7

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc59:2.8.1

English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc59:2.9

English (brahmali, published): if there is an obstacle; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc59:2.10

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc60:1.16.1

English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun, alone with a man, without getting permission from the Sangha or a group, has an abscess or a wound situated on the lower part of her body ruptured by him, or split open, washed, anointed, bandaged, or unwrapped by him, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

English (brahmali, published): If she tells him, “Rupture it,” she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc60:2.1.26
Pali: Bhinne āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When it has been ruptured, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc60:2.1.27

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc60:2.1.30
Pali: Dhovite āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When it has been washed, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc60:2.1.31

English (brahmali, published): If she tells him, “Bandage it,” she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc60:2.1.34
Pali: Baddhe āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When it has been bandaged, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc60:2.1.35
Pali: “Mocehī”ti āṇāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she tells him, “Unwrap it,” she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc60:2.1.36
Pali: Mutte āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When it has been unwrapped, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc60:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc60:2.2.2
Pali: apaloketvā bhedāpeti vā phālāpeti vā dhovāpeti vā ālimpāpeti vā bandhāpeti vā mocāpeti vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she gets permission and then has it ruptured, split open, washed, anointed, bandaged, or unwrapped; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc60:2.2.3
Pali: yā kāci viññū dutiyikā hoti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she has a female companion who understands; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc60:2.2.4
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc60:2.2.5
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc60:2.2.6
Pali: Dasamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The tenth training rule is finished.

Pali: Ārāmavaggo chaṭṭho. 
English (brahmali, published): The sixth subchapter on monasteries is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:1.6
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:1.7
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo gabbhiniṁ vuṭṭhāpessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nuns give the full admission to a pregnant woman?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:1.8
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns heard the complaints of those people 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:1.9
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:1.10
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo gabbhiniṁ vuṭṭhāpessantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could nuns do this?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:1.11
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo gabbhiniṁ vuṭṭhāpentī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that nuns did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:1.12
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:1.13
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:1.14
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo gabbhiniṁ vuṭṭhāpessanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could nuns do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:1.15
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:1.16
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:1.17.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī gabbhiniṁ vuṭṭhāpeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun gives the full admission to a pregnant woman, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.1.5
Pali: Gabbhinī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A pregnant woman: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.1.6
Pali: āpannasattā vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): a woman with child is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.1.7
Pali: Vuṭṭhāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Gives the full admission: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.1.8
Pali: upasampādeyya. 
English (brahmali, published): gives the full ordination. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.1.9
Pali: “Vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti gaṇaṁ vā ācariniṁ vā pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā pariyesati, sīmaṁ vā sammannati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to give the full admission, she searches for a group, a teacher, a bowl, or a robe, or she establishes a monastery zone, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.1.10
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.1.11
Pali: Dvīhi kammavācāhi dukkaṭā. 
English (brahmali, published): After each of the first two announcements, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.1.12
Pali: Kammavācāpariyosāne upajjhāyāya āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When the last announcement is finished, the preceptor commits an offense entailing confession, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.1.13
Pali: Gaṇassa ca ācariniyā ca āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): and the group and the teacher commit an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.2.1
Pali: Gabbhiniyā gabbhinisaññā vuṭṭhāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the woman is pregnant, and the nun perceives her as such, yet she gives her the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.2.2
Pali: Gabbhiniyā vematikā vuṭṭhāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the woman is pregnant, but the nun is unsure of it, yet she gives her the full admission, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.2.3
Pali: Gabbhiniyā agabbhinisaññā vuṭṭhāpeti, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If the woman is pregnant, but the nun does not perceive her as such, and she gives her the full admission, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.2.4
Pali: Agabbhiniyā gabbhinisaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the woman is not pregnant, but the nun perceives her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.2.5
Pali: Agabbhiniyā vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If the woman is not pregnant, but the nun is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.2.6
Pali: Agabbhiniyā agabbhinisaññā, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If the woman is not pregnant, and the nun does not perceive her as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.3.2
Pali: gabbhiniṁ agabbhinisaññā vuṭṭhāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she gives the full admission to a woman who is pregnant, but she  does not perceive her as such; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.3.3
Pali: agabbhiniṁ agabbhinisaññā vuṭṭhāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she gives the full admission to a woman who is not pregnant, and she does not perceive her as such; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.3.4
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.3.5
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc61:2.3.6
Pali: Paṭhamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The first training rule is finished.

Root: root/pli/ms/vinaya/pli-tv-bi-vb/pli-tv-bi-vb-pc/pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63_root-pli-ms.json

Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:0.3
Pali: Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:0.4
Pali: Gabbhinivagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on pregnant women 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:0.5
Pali: 63. Sikkhamānasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 63. Ordaining an untrained candidate 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo dve vassāni chasu dhammesu asikkhitasikkhaṁ sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpenti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the nuns were giving the full admission to trainee nuns who had not trained for two years in the six rules. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.3
Pali: Tā bālā honti abyattā na jānanti kappiyaṁ vā akappiyaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): They were ignorant and incompetent, and did not know what was allowable and what was not. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.4
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.5
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo dve vassāni chasu dhammesu asikkhitasikkhaṁ sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpessantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns give the full admission to trainee nuns who haven’t trained for two years in the six rules?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.6
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo dve vassāni chasu dhammesu asikkhitasikkhaṁ sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpentī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.7
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.8
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.9
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo dve vassāni chasu dhammesu asikkhitasikkhaṁ sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpessanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.10
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.11
Pali: vigarahitvā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking them … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.12
Pali: dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): he gave a teaching and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.13
Pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, sikkhamānāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiṁ dātuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, approval is required for a trainee nun to train for two years in the six rules. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.14
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, dātabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): And it should be given like this. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.15
Pali: Tāya sikkhamānāya saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā bhikkhunīnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): After approaching the Sangha of nuns, that trainee nun should arrange her upper robe over one shoulder and pay respect at the feet of the nuns. She should then squat on her heels, raise her joined palms, and say: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.16
Pali: ‘ahaṁ, ayye, itthannāmā itthannāmāya ayyāya sikkhamānā. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Venerables, I, so-and-so, am a trainee nun under venerable so-and-so. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.17
Pali: Saṅghaṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiṁ yācāmī’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I ask the Sangha for approval to train for two years in the six rules.’ 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.18
Pali: Dutiyampi yācitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): And she should ask a second 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.19
Pali: Tatiyampi yācitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): and a third time. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.20
Pali: Byattāya bhikkhuniyā paṭibalāya saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): A competent and capable nun should then inform the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.21
Pali: ‘Suṇātu me, ayye, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.22
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā itthannāmāya ayyāya sikkhamānā saṅghaṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiṁ yācati. 
English (brahmali, published): The trainee nun so-and-so is asking the Sangha for approval to train for two years in the six rules under venerable so-and-so. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.23
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ saṅgho itthannāmāya sikkhamānāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiṁ dadeyya. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should give approval to the trainee nun so-and-so to train for two years in the six rules. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.24
Pali: Esā ñatti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the motion. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.25
Pali: Suṇātu me, ayye, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.26
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā itthannāmāya ayyāya sikkhamānā saṅghaṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiṁ yācati. 
English (brahmali, published): The trainee nun so-and-so is asking the Sangha for approval to train for two years in the six rules under venerable so-and-so. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.27
Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmāya sikkhamānāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiṁ deti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha gives approval to the trainee nun so-and-so to train for two years in the six rules. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.28
Pali: Yassā ayyāya khamati itthannāmāya sikkhamānāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiyā dānaṁ, sā tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any nun who approves of giving approval to the trainee nun so-and-so to train for two years in the six rules should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.29
Pali: yassā nakkhamati, sā bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any nun who doesn’t approve should speak up. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.30
Pali: Dinnā saṅghena itthannāmāya sikkhamānāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammuti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has given approval to the trainee nun so-and-so to train for two years in the six rules. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.31
Pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’ 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.32
Pali: Sā sikkhamānā “evaṁ vadehī”ti vattabbā—
English (brahmali, published): That trainee nun should then be told to say this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.33
Pali: “pāṇātipātā veramaṇiṁ dve vassāni avītikkamma samādānaṁ samādiyāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘I undertake to abstain from killing living beings for two years without transgression. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.34
Pali: Adinnādānā veramaṇiṁ dve vassāni avītikkamma samādānaṁ samādiyāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): I undertake to abstain from stealing for two years without transgression. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.35
Pali: Abrahmacariyā veramaṇiṁ dve vassāni avītikkamma samādānaṁ samādiyāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): I undertake to abstain from sexual activity for two years without transgression. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.36
Pali: Musāvādā veramaṇiṁ dve vassāni avītikkamma samādānaṁ samādiyāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): I undertake to abstain from lying for two years without transgression. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.37
Pali: Surāmerayamajjappamādaṭṭhānā veramaṇiṁ dve vassāni avītikkamma samādānaṁ samādiyāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): I undertake to abstain from alcohol, which causes heedlessness, for two years without transgression. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.38
Pali: Vikālabhojanā veramaṇiṁ dve vassāni avītikkamma samādānaṁ samādiyāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I undertake to abstain from eating at the wrong time for two years without transgression.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.39
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā tā bhikkhuniyo anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā dubbharatāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): Then, after rebuking those nuns in many ways, the Buddha spoke in dispraise of being difficult to support … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.40
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:1.41.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī dve vassāni chasu dhammesu asikkhitasikkhaṁ sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpeyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun gives the full admission to a trainee nun who has not trained for two years in the six rules, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.1.5
Pali: Dve vassānīti 
English (brahmali, published): Two years: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.1.6
Pali: dve saṁvaccharāni. 
English (brahmali, published): two twelve-month periods. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.1.7
Pali: Asikkhitasikkhā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Who has not trained: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.1.8
Pali: sikkhā vā na dinnā hoti, dinnā vā sikkhā kupitā. 
English (brahmali, published): the training has not been given to her, or the training has been given to her, but she has failed in it. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.1.9
Pali: Vuṭṭhāpeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Gives the full admission: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.1.10
Pali: upasampādeyya. 
English (brahmali, published): gives the full ordination. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.1.11
Pali: “Vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti gaṇaṁ vā ācariniṁ vā pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā pariyesati, sīmaṁ vā sammannati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to give the full admission, she searches for a group, a teacher, a bowl, or a robe, or she establishes a monastery zone, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.1.12
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.1.13
Pali: Dvīhi kammavācāhi dukkaṭā. 
English (brahmali, published): After each of the first two announcements, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.1.14
Pali: Kammavācāpariyosāne upajjhāyāya āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When the last announcement is finished, the preceptor commits an offense entailing confession, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.1.15
Pali: Gaṇassa ca ācariniyā ca āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): and the group and the teacher commit an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.2.1
Pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññā vuṭṭhāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.2.2
Pali: Dhammakamme vematikā vuṭṭhāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.2.3
Pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññā vuṭṭhāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as illegitimate, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.2.4
Pali: Adhammakamme dhammakammasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as legitimate, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.2.5
Pali: Adhammakamme vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.2.6
Pali: Adhammakamme adhammakammasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.3.2
Pali: dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhaṁ sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she gives the full admission to a trainee nun who has trained for two years in the six rules; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.3.3
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.3.4
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc63:2.3.5
Pali: Tatiyasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The third training rule is finished.
