English (brahmali, published): “How could a nun do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:1.14
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:1.15
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:1.16.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī asaṅkaccikā gāmaṁ paviseyya, pācittiyan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun enters an inhabited area without wearing her chest wrap, she commits an offense entailing confession.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.1.5
Pali: Asaṅkaccikāti 
English (brahmali, published): Without wearing her chest wrap: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.1.6
Pali: vinā saṅkaccikaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): not wearing her chest wrap. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.1.7
Pali: Saṅkaccikaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Chest wrap: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.1.8
Pali: adhakkhakaṁ ubbhanābhi, tassa paṭicchādanatthāya. 
English (brahmali, published): it is for the purpose of concealing the body below the collar bone and above the navel. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.1.9
Pali: Gāmaṁ paviseyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Enters an inhabited area: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.1.10
Pali: parikkhittassa gāmassa parikkhepaṁ atikkāmentiyā āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she crosses the boundary of an enclosed inhabited area, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.1.11
Pali: Aparikkhittassa gāmassa upacāraṁ okkamantiyā āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she enters the vicinity of an unenclosed inhabited area, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.2.2
Pali: acchinnacīvarikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if her robe is stolen; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.2.3
Pali: naṭṭhacīvarikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if her robe is lost; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.2.4
Pali: gilānāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is sick; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.2.5
Pali: assatiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is not mindful; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.2.6
Pali: ajānantiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she does not know; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.2.7
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.2.8
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.2.9
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.2.10
Pali: Terasamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The thirteenth training rule is finished. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.2.11
Pali: Chattupāhanavaggo navamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The ninth subchapter on sunshades and sandals is finished. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.2.12.0
Pali: Dhammikavagga 
English: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.2.13.0
Pali: Nandasikkhāpada 
English: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.2.14
Pali: Uddiṭṭhā kho, ayyāyo, chasaṭṭhisatā pācittiyā dhammā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, the one hundred and sixty-six rules on confession have been recited. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.2.15
Pali: Tatthāyyāyo pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): In regard to this I ask you, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.2.16
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.2.17
Pali: Dutiyampi pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): A second time I ask, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.2.18
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.2.19
Pali: Tatiyampi pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): A third time I ask, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.2.20
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.2.21
Pali: Parisuddhetthāyyāyo, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmīti. 
English (brahmali, published): You are pure in this and therefore silent. I will remember it thus.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.2.22
Pali: Khuddakaṁ samattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The section on minor rules has been completed. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pc96:2.2.23
Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅge pācittiyakaṇḍaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing confession in the Nuns’ Analysis is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:0.3
Pali: Pāṭidesanīyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing acknowledgment 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:0.4
Pali: 1. Sappiviññāpanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 1. The training rule on asking for ghee 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:0.5
Pali: Ime kho panāyyāyo aṭṭha pāṭidesanīyā dhammā uddesaṁ āgacchanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerables, these eight rules on acknowledgment come up for recitation. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo sappiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjanti. 
English (brahmali, published): the nuns from the group of six were eating ghee that they had asked for. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.1.3
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.1.4
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo sappiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns eat ghee that they have asked for? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.1.5
Pali: Kassa sampannaṁ na manāpaṁ, kassa sāduṁ na ruccatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Who doesn’t like nice food? Who doesn’t prefer tasty food?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.1.6
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns heard the complaints of those people. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.1.7
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.1.8
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo sappiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjissantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the nuns from the group of six eat ghee that they have asked for?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.1.9
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā, bhikkhuniyo sappiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjantī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that those nuns do this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.1.10
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.1.11
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.1.12
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo sappiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjissanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the nuns from the group of six do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.1.13
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.1.14
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.1.15.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī sappiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjeyya, paṭidesetabbaṁ tāya bhikkhuniyā—
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun asks for ghee and then eats it, she must acknowledge it: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.1.16
Pali: ‘gārayhaṁ, ayye, dhammaṁ āpajjiṁ asappāyaṁ pāṭidesanīyaṁ, taṁ paṭidesemī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I have done a blameworthy and unsuitable thing that is to be acknowledged. I acknowledge it.”’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.1.17
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhunīnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the nuns. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo gilānā honti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards a number of nuns were sick. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.2.2
Pali: Gilānapucchikā bhikkhuniyo gilānā bhikkhuniyo etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns who were looking after them asked, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.2.3
Pali: “kacci, ayye, khamanīyaṁ, kacci yāpanīyan”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “I hope you’re bearing up? I hope you’re getting better?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.2.4
Pali: “Pubbe mayaṁ, ayye, sappiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjāma, tena no phāsu hoti; 
English (brahmali, published): “Previously we ate ghee that we had asked for, and then we were comfortable. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.2.5
Pali: idāni pana ‘bhagavatā paṭikkhittan’ti kukkuccāyantā na viññāpema, tena no na phāsu hotī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): But now that the Buddha has prohibited this, we don’t ask because we’re afraid of wrongdoing. And because of that we’re not comfortable.” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.2.6
Pali: bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. Soon afterwards the Buddha had the Sangha gathered and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.2.7
Pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, gilānāya bhikkhuniyā sappiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow a sick nun to eat ghee that she has asked for. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.2.8
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.2.9.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī agilānā sappiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjeyya, paṭidesetabbaṁ tāya bhikkhuniyā—
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun who is not sick asks for ghee and then eats it, she must acknowledge it: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:1.2.10
Pali: ‘gārayhaṁ, ayye, dhammaṁ āpajjiṁ asappāyaṁ pāṭidesanīyaṁ taṁ paṭidesemī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I have done a blameworthy and unsuitable thing that is to be acknowledged. I acknowledge it.”’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.1.5
Pali: Agilānā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Who is not sick: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.1.6
Pali: yassā vinā sappinā phāsu hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): who is comfortable without ghee. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.1.7
Pali: Gilānā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Who is sick: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.1.8
Pali: yassā vinā sappinā na phāsu hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): who is not comfortable without ghee. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.1.9
Pali: Sappi nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Ghee: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.1.10
Pali: gosappi vā ajikāsappi vā mahiṁsasappi vā. Yesaṁ maṁsaṁ kappati tesaṁ sappi. 
English (brahmali, published): ghee from cows, ghee from goats, ghee from buffaloes, or ghee from whatever animal whose meat is allowable. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.1.11
Pali: Agilānā attano atthāya viññāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is not sick and she asks for herself, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.1.12
Pali: Paṭilābhena “bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When she receives it with the intention of eating it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.1.13
Pali: Ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti pāṭidesanīyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For every mouthful swallowed, she commits an offense entailing acknowledgment. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.2.1
Pali: Agilānā agilānasaññā sappiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjati, āpatti pāṭidesanīyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is not sick, and she does not perceive herself as sick, and she eats ghee that she has asked for, she commits an offense entailing acknowledgment. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.2.2
Pali: Agilānā vematikā sappiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjati, āpatti pāṭidesanīyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is not sick, but she is unsure of it, and she eats ghee that she has asked for, she commits an offense entailing acknowledgment. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.2.3
Pali: Agilānā gilānasaññā sappiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjati, āpatti pāṭidesanīyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is not sick, but she perceives herself as sick, and she eats ghee that she has asked for, she commits an offense entailing acknowledgment. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.2.4
Pali: Gilānā agilānasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is sick, but she does not perceive herself as sick, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.2.5
Pali: Gilānā vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is sick, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.2.6
Pali: Gilānā gilānasaññā, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is sick, and she perceives herself as sick, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.3.2
Pali: gilānāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is sick; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.3.3
Pali: gilānā hutvā viññāpetvā agilānā bhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she asked for it when she was sick, but eats it when she is no longer sick; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.3.4
Pali: gilānāya sesakaṁ bhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she eats the leftovers from one who is sick; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.3.5
Pali: ñātakānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from relatives; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.3.6
Pali: pavāritānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from those who have given an invitation; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.3.7
Pali: aññassatthāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is for the benefit of someone else; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.3.8
Pali: attano dhanena, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is by means of her own property; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.3.9
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.3.10
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd1:2.3.11
Pali: Paṭhamapāṭidesanīyasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The first training rule on acknowledgment is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd2:1.2.5
Pali: idāni pana ‘bhagavatā paṭikkhittan’ti kukkuccāyantā na viññāpema, tena no na phāsu hotī”ti … pe … 
English (brahmali, published): But now that the Buddha has prohibited this, we don’t ask because we’re afraid of wrongdoing. And because of that we’re not comfortable.” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd2:1.2.6
Pali: bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ … pe … 
English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. Soon afterwards the Buddha had the Sangha gathered and addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd2:1.2.7
Pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, gilānāya bhikkhuniyā dadhiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow a sick nun to eat curd that she has asked for. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd2:1.2.8
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd2:1.2.9.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī agilānā (telaṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun who is not sick asks for (oil … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd3:1.1.1
Pali: madhuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): honey … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd4:1.1.1
Pali: phāṇitaṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): syrup … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd5:1.1.1
Pali: macchaṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): fish … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd6:1.1.1
Pali: maṁsaṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): meat … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd7:1.1.1
Pali: khīraṁ …pe…) 
English (brahmali, published): milk … ) 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:1.1.1
Pali: dadhiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjeyya, paṭidesetabbaṁ tāya bhikkhuniyā—
English (brahmali, published): curd and then eats it, she must acknowledge it: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:1.1.2
Pali: ‘gārayhaṁ, ayye, dhammaṁ āpajjiṁ asappāyaṁ pāṭidesanīyaṁ, taṁ paṭidesemī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I have done a blameworthy and unsuitable thing that is to be acknowledged. I acknowledge it.”’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā … pe … 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.4
Pali: … pe … ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.5
Pali: Agilānā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Who is not sick: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.6
Pali: yassā vinā dadhinā phāsu hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): who is comfortable without curd. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.7
Pali: Gilānā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Who is sick: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.8
Pali: yassā vinā dadhinā na phāsu hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): who is not comfortable without curd. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.9
Pali: Telaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Oil: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.10
Pali: tilatelaṁ sāsapatelaṁ madhukatelaṁ eraṇḍatelaṁ vasātelaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): sesame oil, mustard oil, honey tree oil, castor oil, oil from tallow. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.11
Pali: Madhu nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Honey: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.12
Pali: makkhikāmadhu. 
English (brahmali, published): honey from bees. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.13
Pali: Phāṇitaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Syrup: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.14
Pali: ucchumhā nibbattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): from sugarcane. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.15
Pali: Maccho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Fish: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.16
Pali: odako vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): what lives in water is what is meant. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.17
Pali: Maṁsaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Meat: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.18
Pali: yesaṁ maṁsaṁ kappati tesaṁ maṁsaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): the meat of those animals whose meat is allowable. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.19
Pali: Khīraṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Milk: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.20
Pali: gokhīraṁ vā ajikākhīraṁ vā mahiṁsakhīraṁ vā yesaṁ maṁsaṁ kappati tesaṁ khīraṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): milk from cows, milk from goats, milk from buffaloes, or milk from whatever animal whose meat is allowable. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.21
Pali: Dadhi nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Curd: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.22
Pali: tesaññeva dadhi. 
English (brahmali, published): curd from those same animals. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.23
Pali: Agilānā attano atthāya viññāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is not sick and she asks for herself, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.24
Pali: Paṭilābhena “bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When she receives it with the intention of eating it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.1.25
Pali: Ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti pāṭidesanīyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For every mouthful swallowed, she commits an offense entailing acknowledgment. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.2.1
Pali: Agilānā agilānasaññā dadhiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjati, āpatti pāṭidesanīyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is not sick, and she does not perceive herself as sick, and she eats curd that she has asked for, she commits an offense entailing acknowledgment. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.2.2
Pali: Agilānā vematikā dadhiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjati, āpatti pāṭidesanīyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is not sick, but she is unsure of it, and she eats curd that she has asked for, she commits an offense entailing acknowledgment. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.2.3
Pali: Agilānā gilānasaññā dadhiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjati, āpatti pāṭidesanīyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is not sick, but she perceives herself as sick, and she eats curd that she has asked for, she commits an offense entailing acknowledgment. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.2.4
Pali: Gilānā agilānasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is sick, but she does not perceive herself as sick, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.2.5
Pali: Gilānā vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is sick, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.2.6
Pali: Gilānā gilānasaññā anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is sick, and she perceives herself as sick, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.3.2
Pali: gilānāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is sick; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.3.3
Pali: gilānā hutvā viññāpetvā agilānā bhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she asked for it when she was sick, but eats it when she is no longer sick; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.3.4
Pali: gilānāya sesakaṁ bhuñjati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she eats the leftovers from one who is sick; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.3.5
Pali: ñātakānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from relatives; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.3.6
Pali: pavāritānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is from those who have given an invitation; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.3.7
Pali: aññassatthāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is for the benefit of someone else; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.3.8
Pali: attano dhanena, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is by means of her own property; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.3.9
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.3.10
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.3.11
Pali: Aṭṭhamapāṭidesanīyasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The eighth training rule entailing acknowledgment is finished. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.3.12
Pali: Uddiṭṭhā kho, ayyāyo, aṭṭha pāṭidesanīyā dhammā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, the eight rules on acknowledgment have been recited. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.3.13
Pali: Tatthāyyāyo pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): In regard to this I ask you, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.3.14
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.3.15
Pali: Dutiyampi pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): A second time I ask, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.3.16
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.3.17
Pali: Tatiyampi pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): A third time I ask, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.3.18
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.3.19
Pali: Parisuddhetthāyyāyo, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmīti. 
English (brahmali, published): You are pure in this and therefore silent. I will remember it thus.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pd8:2.3.20
Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅge pāṭidesanīyakaṇḍaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing acknowledgment in the Nuns’ Analysis is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5
Root: root/pli/ms/vinaya/pli-tv-bi-vb/pli-tv-bi-vb-pj/pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5_root-pli-ms.json

English translation sources:
  - brahmali [published]: translation/en/brahmali/vinaya/pli-tv-bi-vb/pli-tv-bi-vb-pj/pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5_translation-en-brahmali.json

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:0.2
Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:0.3
Pali: Pārājikakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing expulsion 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:0.4
Pali: 5. Ubbhajāṇumaṇḍalikāsikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 5. The training rule on physical contact with a man 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.0.2
Pali: Namo tassa Bhagavato Arahato Sammāsambuddhassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Homage to the Buddha, the Perfected One, the fully Awakened One 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sāḷho migāranattā bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihāraṁ kattukāmo hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Migāra’s grandson Sāḷha wanted to build a dwelling for the Sangha of nuns. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.3
Pali: Atha kho sāḷho migāranattā bhikkhuniyo upasaṅkamitvā etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): He went to the nuns and said, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.4
Pali: “icchāmahaṁ, ayye, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihāraṁ kātuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, I wish to build a dwelling for the Sangha of nuns. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.5
Pali: Detha me navakammikaṁ bhikkhunin”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Please get me the nun in charge of building work.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.6
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena catasso bhaginiyo bhikkhunīsu pabbajitā honti—
English (brahmali, published): At that time four sisters had gone forth as nuns: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.7
Pali: nandā, nandavatī, sundarīnandā, thullanandāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Nandā, Nandavatī, Sundarīnandā, and Thullanandā. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.8
Pali: Tāsu sundarīnandā bhikkhunī taruṇapabbajitā abhirūpā hoti dassanīyā pāsādikā paṇḍitā byattā medhāvinī dakkhā analasā, tatrupāyāya vīmaṁsāya samannāgatā, alaṁ kātuṁ alaṁ saṁvidhātuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Of these, the nun Sundarīnandā had gone forth when she was young, and she was beautiful, intelligent, skilled, and diligent, and she possessed good judgment in regard to doing and arranging things well. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.9
Pali: Atha kho bhikkhunisaṅgho sundarīnandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ sammannitvā sāḷhassa migāranattuno navakammikaṁ adāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): After appointing Sundarīnandā to be in charge of building work, the Sangha made her work with Sāḷha. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.10
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sundarīnandā bhikkhunī sāḷhassa migāranattuno nivesanaṁ abhikkhaṇaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): As a consequence of this, Sundarīnandā would often go to Sāḷha’s house to ask for tools, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.11
Pali: “vāsiṁ detha, parasuṁ detha, kuṭhāriṁ detha, kudālaṁ detha, nikhādanaṁ dethā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): whether a machete, a hatchet, an ax, a spade, or a chisel. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.12
Pali: Sāḷhopi migāranattā bhikkhunupassayaṁ abhikkhaṇaṁ gacchati katākataṁ jānituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): And Sāḷha would often go to the nunnery to find out about the progress of the building work. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.13
Pali: Te abhiṇhadassanena paṭibaddhacittā ahesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): And because they saw each other frequently, they fell in love. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.14
Pali: Atha kho sāḷho migāranattā sundarīnandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ dūsetuṁ okāsaṁ alabhamāno etadevaatthāya bhikkhunisaṅghassa bhattaṁ akāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): But because Sāḷha could not find any opportunity to be intimate with Sundarīnandā, he invited the Sangha of nuns for a meal. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.15
Pali: Atha kho sāḷho migāranattā bhattagge āsanaṁ paññapento “ettakā bhikkhuniyo ayyāya sundarīnandāya vuḍḍhatarā”ti—ekamantaṁ āsanaṁ paññapesi “ettakā navakatarā”ti—ekamantaṁ āsanaṁ paññapesi. 
English (brahmali, published): When preparing the seats in the dining hall, he counted the number of nuns senior to Venerable Sundarīnandā and placed their seats to one side, and he counted the number of nuns junior to her and placed their seats on the other side. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.16
Pali: Paṭicchanne okāse nikūṭe sundarīnandāya bhikkhuniyā āsanaṁ paññapesi, yathā therā bhikkhuniyo jāneyyuṁ—“navakānaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ santike nisinnā”ti; navakāpi bhikkhuniyo jāneyyuṁ—“therānaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ santike nisinnā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): He then placed Sundarīnandā’s seat in a concealed spot in a corner. In this way the senior nuns would think she was seated close to the junior ones and the junior nuns would think she was seated close to the senior ones. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.17
Pali: Atha kho sāḷho migāranattā bhikkhunisaṅghassa kālaṁ ārocāpesi—“kālo, ayye, niṭṭhitaṁ bhattan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards he informed the Sangha that the meal was ready. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.18
Pali: Sundarīnandā bhikkhunī sallakkhetvā—“na bahukato sāḷho migāranattā bhikkhunisaṅghassa bhattaṁ akāsi; maṁ so dūsetukāmo. 
English (brahmali, published): Sundarīnandā thought, “Sāḷha hasn’t prepared a meal for the Sangha as a service, but because he wants to be intimate with me. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.19
Pali: Sacāhaṁ gamissāmi vissaro me bhavissatī”ti, antevāsiniṁ bhikkhuniṁ āṇāpesi—“gaccha me piṇḍapātaṁ nīhara. 
English (brahmali, published): If I go, I will get into trouble.” She then told a nun who was her pupil, “Go and bring back almsfood for me. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.20
Pali: Yo ce maṁ pucchati, ‘gilānā’ti paṭivedehī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If anyone asks about me, tell them I’m sick.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.21
Pali: “Evaṁ, ayye”ti kho sā bhikkhunī sundarīnandāya bhikkhuniyā paccassosi. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes, venerable,” she replied. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.22
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sāḷho migāranattā bahidvārakoṭṭhake ṭhito hoti sundarīnandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ paṭipucchanto—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards Sāḷha was standing outside his gatehouse repeatedly inquiring after Sundarīnandā: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.23
Pali: “kahaṁ, ayye, ayyā sundarīnandā? Kahaṁ, ayye, ayyā sundarīnandā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, where’s Venerable Sundarīnandā?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.24
Pali: Evaṁ vutte, sundarīnandāya bhikkhuniyā antevāsinī bhikkhunī sāḷhaṁ migāranattāraṁ etadavoca—“gilānāvuso; piṇḍapātaṁ nīharissāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The nun who was Sundarīnandā’s pupil told him: “She’s sick. I’m bringing her almsfood.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.25
Pali: Atha kho sāḷho migāranattā—“yampāhaṁ atthāya bhikkhunisaṅghassa bhattaṁ akāsiṁ ayyāya sundarīnandāya kāraṇā”ti manusse āṇāpetvā—
English (brahmali, published): Sāḷha thought, “I invited the Sangha of nuns because of Sundarīnandā,” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.26
Pali: “bhikkhunisaṅghaṁ bhattena parivisathā”ti vatvā yena bhikkhunupassayo tenupasaṅkami. 
English (brahmali, published): and after telling the people there to serve the meal to the Sangha of nuns, he left for the nunnery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.27
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sundarīnandā bhikkhunī bahārāmakoṭṭhake ṭhitā hoti sāḷhaṁ migāranattāraṁ patimānentī. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then Sundarīnandā was standing outside the monastery gatehouse longing for Sāḷha. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.28
Pali: Addasā kho sundarīnandā bhikkhunī sāḷhaṁ migāranattāraṁ dūratova āgacchantaṁ. Disvāna upassayaṁ pavisitvā sasīsaṁ pārupitvā mañcake nipajji. 
English (brahmali, published): When she saw him coming, she entered the nunnery, put on her upper robe so that it covered her head, and lay down on her bed. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.29
Pali: Atha kho sāḷho migāranattā yena sundarīnandā bhikkhunī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā sundarīnandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavoca—“kiṁ te, ayye, aphāsu, kissa nipannāsī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): Sāḷha approached her and said, “Venerable, what’s wrong with you? Why are you lying down?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.30
Pali: “Evañhetaṁ, āvuso, hoti yā anicchantaṁ icchatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “That’s what happens when you desire someone who doesn’t desire you in return.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.31
Pali: “Kyāhaṁ taṁ, ayye, na icchissāmi? 
English (brahmali, published): “What do you mean I don’t desire you? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.32
Pali: Api cāhaṁ okāsaṁ na labhāmi taṁ dūsetun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): I just couldn’t find an opportunity to be intimate with you.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.33
Pali: Avassuto avassutāya sundarīnandāya bhikkhuniyā kāyasaṁsaggaṁ samāpajji. 
English (brahmali, published): And both having lust, he made physical contact with Sundarīnandā. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.34
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarā bhikkhunī jarādubbalā caraṇagilānā sundarīnandāya bhikkhuniyā avidūre nipannā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then a nun who was weak from old age and had problems with her feet was lying down not far from Sundarīnandā. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.35
Pali: Addasā kho sā bhikkhunī sāḷhaṁ migāranattāraṁ avassutaṁ avassutāya sundarīnandāya bhikkhuniyā kāyasaṁsaggaṁ samāpajjantaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): She saw how Sāḷha made physical contact with Sundarīnandā while both of them had lust. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.36
Pali: Disvāna ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—“kathañhi nāma ayyā sundarīnandā avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): She complained and criticized her, “How could Venerable Sundarīnandā consent to a man making physical contact with her, when they both had lust?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.37
Pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): That nun then told the nuns what had happened. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.38
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā santuṭṭhā lajjiniyo kukkuccikā sikkhākāmā tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires, who had a sense of conscience, and who were contented, afraid of wrongdoing, and fond of the training, complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.39
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā sundarīnandā avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Sundarīnandā consent to a man making physical contact with her, when they both had lust?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.40
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. Ye te bhikkhū appicchā santuṭṭhā lajjino kukkuccakā sikkhākāmā te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—“kathañhi nāma sundarīnandā bhikkhunī avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Those nuns then told the monks. And the monks of few desires, who had a sense of conscience, and who were contented, afraid of wrongdoing, and fond of the training, criticized her in the same way. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.41
Pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū sundarīnandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): After rebuking the nun Sundarīnandā in many ways, they told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.42
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe bhikkhusaṅghaṁ sannipātāpetvā bhikkhū paṭipucchi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.43
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, sundarīnandā bhikkhunī avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyatī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the nun Sundarīnandā acted like this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.44
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.45
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā—
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.46
Pali: “ananucchavikaṁ, bhikkhave, sundarīnandāya bhikkhuniyā ananulomikaṁ appatirūpaṁ assāmaṇakaṁ akappiyaṁ akaraṇīyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s not suitable, monks, it’s not proper for the nun Sundarīnandā, it’s not worthy of a monastic, it’s not allowable, it’s not to be done. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.47
Pali: Kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, sundarīnandā bhikkhunī avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyissati. 
English (brahmali, published): How could Sundarīnandā consent to a man making physical contact with her, when they both had lust? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.48
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya pasannānaṁ vā bhiyyobhāvāya. 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.49
Pali: Atha khvetaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānañceva appasādāya pasannānañca ekaccānaṁ aññathattāyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and cause some to lose it.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.50
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā sundarīnandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā dubbharatāya dupposatāya mahicchatāya asantuṭṭhitāya saṅgaṇikāya kosajjassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsitvā, anekapariyāyena subharatāya suposatāya appicchassa santuṭṭhassa sallekhassa dhutassa pāsādikassa apacayassa vīriyārambhassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsitvā, bhikkhūnaṁ tadanucchavikaṁ tadanulomikaṁ dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
English (brahmali, published): And the Buddha spoke in many ways in dispraise of being difficult to support and maintain, in dispraise of great desires, discontent, socializing, and laziness; but he spoke in many ways in praise of being easy to support and maintain, of fewness of wishes, contentment, self-effacement, ascetic practices, serenity, reduction in things, and being energetic. After giving a teaching on what is right and proper, he addressed the monks: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.51
Pali: “tena hi, bhikkhave, bhikkhunīnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññapessāmi dasa atthavase paṭicca—
English (brahmali, published): “Well then, monks, I will lay down a training rule for the following ten reasons: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.52
Pali: saṅghasuṭṭhutāya, saṅghaphāsutāya, dummaṅkūnaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ niggahāya, pesalānaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ phāsuvihārāya, diṭṭhadhammikānaṁ āsavānaṁ saṁvarāya, samparāyikānaṁ āsavānaṁ paṭighātāya, appasannānaṁ pasādāya, pasannānaṁ bhiyyobhāvāya, saddhammaṭṭhitiyā vinayānuggahāya. 
English (brahmali, published): for the well-being of the Sangha, for the comfort of the Sangha, for the restraint of bad nuns, for the ease of good nuns, for the restraint of the corruptions relating to the present life, for the restraint of the corruptions relating to future lives, to give rise to confidence in those without it, to increase the confidence of those who have it, for the longevity of the true Teaching, and for supporting the training. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.53
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:1.54.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa adhakkhakaṁ ubbhajāṇumaṇḍalaṁ āmasanaṁ vā parāmasanaṁ vā gahaṇaṁ vā chupanaṁ vā paṭipīḷanaṁ vā sādiyeyya, ayampi pārājikā hoti asaṁvāsā ubbhajāṇumaṇḍalikā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a lustful nun consents to a lustful man making physical contact with her, to touching her, to taking hold of her, to contacting her, or to squeezing her, anywhere below the collar bone but above the knees, she too is expelled and excluded from the community. The training rule on above the knees.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā yathāyuttā yathājaccā yathānāmā yathāgottā yathāsīlā yathāvihārinī yathāgocarā therā vā navā vā majjhimā vā, esā vuccati yā panāti. 
English (brahmali, published): whoever, of such a kind, of such activity, of such caste, of such name, of such family, of such conduct, of such behavior, of such association, who is senior, who is junior, or who is of middle standing—this is called “a”.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:2.1.14

English (brahmali, published): a fully trained nun; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:2.1.15

English (brahmali, published): a nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:2.1.16

English (brahmali, published): The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:2.1.17
Pali: Avassutā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Lustful:

English (brahmali, published): a human male, not a male spirit, not a male ghost, not a male animal. He understands and is capable of making physical contact.

Pali: Ayampīti 
English (brahmali, published): She too:

English (brahmali, published): this is said with reference to the preceding offenses entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:2.1.39
Pali: Pārājikā hotīti 
English (brahmali, published): Is expelled: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:2.1.40
Pali: seyyathāpi nāma puriso sīsacchinno abhabbo tena sarīrabandhanena jīvituṁ; evameva bhikkhunī avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa adhakkhakaṁ ubbhajāṇumaṇḍalaṁ āmasanaṁ vā parāmasanaṁ vā gahaṇaṁ vā chupanaṁ vā paṭipīḷanaṁ vā sādiyantī assamaṇī hoti asakyadhītā. Tena vuccati pārājikā hotīti. 
English (brahmali, published): just as a man with his head cut off is unable to continue living by reconnecting it to the body, so is a lustful nun who consents to a lustful man making physical contact with her, to touching her, to taking hold of her, to contacting her, or to squeezing her, anywhere below the collar bone but above the knees, not a monastic, not a daughter of the Sakyan. Therefore it is said “she is expelled.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:2.1.41
Pali: Asaṁvāsāti 
English (brahmali, published): Excluded from the community:

English (brahmali, published): Community: joint legal procedures, a joint recitation, the same training—this is called “community”. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:2.1.43
Pali: So tāya saddhiṁ natthi, tena vuccati asaṁvāsāti. 
English (brahmali, published): She does not take part in this—therefore it is called “excluded from the community”. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj5:2.2.1
Pali: Ubhatoavassute adhakkhakaṁ ubbhajāṇumaṇḍalaṁ kāyena kāyaṁ āmasati, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Physical contact below the collar bone but above the knees, both having lust: (1) If he, with his own body,  makes physical contact with her body, she commits an offense entailing expulsion.

English (brahmali, published): (2) If he, with his own body, makes physical contact with something connected to her body, she commits a serious offense.

Pali: Kāyapaṭibaddhena kāyaṁ āmasati, āpatti thullaccayassa.

Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing expulsion 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:0.4
Pali: 6. Vajjappaṭicchādikāsikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 6. The training rule on concealing an expulsion offense 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sundarīnandā bhikkhunī sāḷhena migāranattunā gabbhinī hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the nun Sundarīnandā was pregnant by Migāra’s grandson Sāḷha. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:1.3
Pali: Yāva gabbho taruṇo ahosi tāva chādesi. 
English (brahmali, published): When the fetus got large, she concealed her condition. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:1.4
Pali: Paripakke gabbhe vibbhamitvā vijāyi. 
English (brahmali, published): And when the fetus was fully grown, she disrobed and gave birth. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:1.5
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavocuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns said to the nun Thullanandā, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:1.6
Pali: “sundarīnandā kho, ayye, aciravibbhantā vijātā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, Sundarīnandā gave birth shortly after disrobing.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:1.7
Pali: Kacci no sā bhikkhunīyeva samānā gabbhinī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Could it be that she was pregnant while she was still a nun?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:1.8
Pali: “Evaṁ, ayye”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yes, venerables.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:1.9
Pali: “Kissa pana tvaṁ, ayye, jānaṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpannaṁ bhikkhuniṁ nevattanā paṭicodesi na gaṇassa ārocesī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “But, venerable, when you knew that a nun had committed an offense entailing expulsion, why didn’t you either confront her yourself or tell the community?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:1.10
Pali: “Yo etissā avaṇṇo mayheso avaṇṇo, yā etissā akitti mayhesā akitti, yo etissā ayaso mayheso ayaso, yo etissā alābho mayheso alābho. 
English (brahmali, published): “Her disrepute is my disrepute, her infamy is my infamy, her notoriety is my notoriety, her loss is my loss. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:1.11
Pali: Kyāhaṁ, ayye, attano avaṇṇaṁ attano akittiṁ attano ayasaṁ attano alābhaṁ paresaṁ ārocessāmī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): Why would I tell others of my own disrepute, infamy, notoriety, and loss?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:1.12
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:1.13
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā jānaṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpannaṁ bhikkhuniṁ nevattanā paṭicodessati na gaṇassa ārocessatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Thullanandā, knowing that a nun had committed an offense entailing expulsion, neither confront her herself nor tell the community?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:1.14
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Then those nuns told the monks what had happened, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:1.15
Pali: Bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): and the monks in turn told the Buddha. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:1.16
Pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe bhikkhusaṅghaṁ sannipātāpetvā bhikkhū paṭipucchi—
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards the Buddha had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:1.17
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī jānaṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpannaṁ bhikkhuniṁ nevattanā paṭicodeti na gaṇassa ārocetī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā acted like this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:1.18
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:1.19
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:1.20
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī jānaṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpannaṁ bhikkhuniṁ nevattanā paṭicodessati na gaṇassa ārocessati. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nun Thullanandā, knowing that a nun had committed an offense entailing expulsion, neither confront her herself nor tell the community? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:1.21
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:1.22
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:1.23.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī jānaṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpannaṁ bhikkhuniṁ nevattanā paṭicodeyya na gaṇassa āroceyya, yadā ca sā ṭhitā vā assa cutā vā nāsitā vā avassaṭā vā, sā pacchā evaṁ vadeyya—‘pubbevāhaṁ, ayye, aññāsiṁ etaṁ bhikkhuniṁ evarūpā ca evarūpā ca sā bhaginīti, no ca kho attanā paṭicodessaṁ na gaṇassa ārocessan’ti, ayampi pārājikā hoti asaṁvāsā vajjappaṭicchādikā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun knows that a nun has committed an offense entailing expulsion, but she neither confronts her herself nor tells the community, and afterward—whether that nun remains or has died or has been expelled or has converted—she says, “Venerables, although I previously knew that this nun was like this, I thought, ‘I will neither confront her myself nor tell the community,’” she too is expelled and excluded from the community. The training rule on those who conceal offenses.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.5
Pali: Jānāti nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Knows: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.6
Pali: sāmaṁ vā jānāti, aññe vā tassā ārocenti, sā vā āroceti. 
English (brahmali, published): she knows by herself or others have told her or she has told her. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.7
Pali: Pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpannanti 
English (brahmali, published): Has committed an offense entailing expulsion: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.8
Pali: aṭṭhannaṁ pārājikānaṁ aññataraṁ pārājikaṁ ajjhāpannaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): she has committed any one of the eight offenses entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.9
Pali: Nevattanā paṭicodeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): She neither confronts her herself: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.10
Pali: na sayaṁ codeyya. 
English (brahmali, published): she does not herself accuse her. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.11
Pali: Na gaṇassa āroceyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Nor tells the community: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.12
Pali: na aññāsaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ āroceyya. 
English (brahmali, published): she does not tell other nuns. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.13
Pali: Yadā ca sā ṭhitā vā assa cutā vāti 
English (brahmali, published): Whether that nun remains or has died: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.14
Pali: ṭhitā nāma saliṅge ṭhitā vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): Remains: what is meant is that she remains as a nun. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.15
Pali: Cutā nāma kālaṅkatā vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): Has died: what is meant is that she has passed away. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.16
Pali: Nāsitā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Has been expelled: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.17
Pali: sayaṁ vā vibbhantā hoti aññehi vā nāsitā. 
English (brahmali, published): she has either disrobed herself or been expelled by others. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.18
Pali: Avassaṭā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Has converted: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.19
Pali: titthāyatanaṁ saṅkantā vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): what is meant is that she has joined another religious community. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.20
Pali: Sā pacchā evaṁ vadeyya— “pubbevāhaṁ, ayye, aññāsiṁ etaṁ bhikkhuniṁ evarūpā ca evarūpā ca sā bhaginī”ti. No ca kho attanā paṭicodessanti 
English (brahmali, published): Afterward she says, “Venerables, although I previously knew that this nun was like this, I thought, ‘I will neither confront her myself’”: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.21
Pali: sayaṁ vā na codessaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “I won’t accuse her myself.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.22
Pali: Na gaṇassa ārocessanti 
English (brahmali, published): “Nor tell the community”: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.23
Pali: na aññāsaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ ārocessaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Nor tell other nuns.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.24
Pali: Ayampīti 
English (brahmali, published): She too: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.25
Pali: purimāyo upādāya vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): this is said with reference to the preceding offenses entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.26
Pali: Pārājikā hotīti 
English (brahmali, published): Is expelled: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.27
Pali: seyyathāpi nāma paṇḍupalāso bandhanā pamutto abhabbo haritatthāya, evameva bhikkhunī jānaṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpannaṁ bhikkhuniṁ nevattanā paṭicodessāmi na gaṇassa ārocessāmīti dhuraṁ nikkhittamatte assamaṇī hoti asakyadhītā. Tena vuccati pārājikā hotīti. 
English (brahmali, published): just as a fallen, withered leaf is incapable of becoming green again, so is a nun who knows that a nun has committed an offense entailing expulsion, but who thinks, “I will neither confront her myself nor tell the community,” by the mere fact of abandoning her duty, not a monastic, not a daughter of the Sakyan. Therefore it is said “she is expelled.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.28
Pali: Asaṁvāsāti 
English (brahmali, published): Excluded from the community: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.29
Pali: saṁvāso nāma ekakammaṁ ekuddeso samasikkhatā. Eso saṁvāso nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): Community: joint legal procedures, a joint recitation, the same training—this is called “community”. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.1.30
Pali: So tāya saddhiṁ natthi. Tena vuccati asaṁvāsāti. 
English (brahmali, published): She does not take part in this—therefore it is called “excluded from the community”. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.2.2
Pali: “saṅghassa bhaṇḍanaṁ vā kalaho vā viggaho vā vivādo vā bhavissatī”ti nāroceti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she does not tell because she thinks there will be quarrels or disputes in the Sangha; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.2.3
Pali: “saṅghabhedo vā saṅgharāji vā bhavissatī”ti nāroceti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she does not tell because she thinks there will be a schism or fracture in the Sangha; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.2.4
Pali: “ayaṁ kakkhaḷā pharusā jīvitantarāyaṁ vā brahmacariyantarāyaṁ vā karissatī”ti nāroceti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she does not tell because she thinks the person she is telling about is cruel and harsh and that she might become a threat to life or the monastic life; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.2.5
Pali: aññā patirūpā bhikkhuniyo apassantī nāroceti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she does not tell because she does not see any suitable nuns; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.2.6
Pali: nacchādetukāmā nāroceti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she does not tell, but not because she wants to conceal; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.2.7
Pali: paññāyissati sakena kammenāti nāroceti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she does not tell because she thinks the other person will be known through her own actions; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.2.8
Pali: ummattikāya 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.2.9
Pali: …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): if she is deranged; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.2.10
Pali: 
English (brahmali, published): if she is overwhelmed by pain; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.2.11
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj6:2.2.12
Pali: Dutiyapārājikaṁ samattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The sixth offense entailing expulsion is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:1.1

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:1.6

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:1.7

English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:1.10
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:1.11.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī samaggena saṅghena ukkhittaṁ bhikkhuṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anādaraṁ appaṭikāraṁ akatasahāyaṁ tamanuvatteyya, sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi evamassa vacanīyā—
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun takes sides with a monk who has been ejected by a unanimous Sangha—in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction—and who is disrespectful, who has not made amends, and who has not made friends, the nuns should correct her like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:1.12
Pali: ‘eso kho, ayye, bhikkhu samaggena saṅghena ukkhitto dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anādaro appaṭikāro akatasahāyo, māyye, etaṁ bhikkhuṁ anuvattī’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, this monk has been ejected by a unanimous Sangha in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction. He’s disrespectful, hasn’t made amends, and hasn’t made friends. Venerable, don’t take sides with this monk.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:1.13
Pali: Evañca sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi vuccamānā tatheva paggaṇheyya, sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsitabbā tassa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): If that nun continues as before, the nuns should press her up to three times to make her stop. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:1.14
Pali: Yāvatatiyañce samanubhāsiyamānā taṁ paṭinissajjeyya, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If she then stops, all is well. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:1.15
Pali: No ce paṭinissajjeyya, ayampi pārājikā hoti asaṁvāsā ukkhittānuvattikā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If she does not stop, she too is expelled and excluded from the community. The training rule on taking sides with one who has been ejected.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.5
Pali: Samaggo nāma saṅgho 
English (brahmali, published): A unanimous Sangha: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.6
Pali: samānasaṁvāsako samānasīmāyaṁ ṭhito. 
English (brahmali, published): those belonging to the same Buddhist sect and staying within the same monastery zone. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.7
Pali: Ukkhitto nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Who has been ejected: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.8
Pali: āpattiyā adassane vā appaṭikamme vā appaṭinissagge vā ukkhitto. 
English (brahmali, published): who has been ejected for not recognizing an offense, for not making amends, or for not giving up a bad view. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.9
Pali: Dhammena vinayenāti 
English (brahmali, published): In accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.10

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.11

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.12
Pali: jinasāsanena buddhasāsanena. 
English (brahmali, published): in accordance with the Victor’s instruction, in accordance with the Buddha’s instruction. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.13
Pali: Anādaro nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Who is disrespectful: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.14
Pali: saṅghaṁ vā gaṇaṁ vā puggalaṁ vā kammaṁ vā nādiyati. 
English (brahmali, published): he does not heed the Sangha, groups of monks, individual monks, or legal procedures. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.15
Pali: Appaṭikāro nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Who has not made amends: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.16
Pali: ukkhitto anosārito. 
English (brahmali, published): he has been ejected and not readmitted. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.17
Pali: Akatasahāyo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Who has not made friends: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.18
Pali: samānasaṁvāsakā bhikkhū vuccanti sahāyā. 
English (brahmali, published): monks belonging to the same Buddhist sect is what is meant by “friends”. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.19
Pali: So tehi saddhiṁ natthi, tena vuccati akatasahāyoti. 
English (brahmali, published): He is not together with them—therefore it is called “who has not made friends”. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.20
Pali: Tamanuvatteyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Takes sides with: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.21
Pali: yaṁdiṭṭhiko so hoti yaṅkhantiko yaṁruciko, sāpi taṁdiṭṭhikā hoti taṅkhantikā taṁrucikā. 
English (brahmali, published): she has the same view, the same belief, the same persuasion as he does. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.22
Pali: Sā bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Her: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.23

English (brahmali, published): that nun who supports one who has been ejected.

English (brahmali, published): other nuns who see it or hear about it. They should correct her like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.26

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.27
Pali: Māyye, etaṁ bhikkhuṁ anuvattī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable, don’t take sides with this monk.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.28
Pali: Dutiyampi vattabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): And they should correct her a second 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.29

English (brahmali, published): and a third time. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.30

English (brahmali, published): If she does not stop, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.32
Pali: Sutvā na vadanti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If those who hear about it do not say anything, they commit an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.33
Pali: Sā bhikkhunī saṅghamajjhampi ākaḍḍhitvā vattabbā—
English (brahmali, published): That nun, even if she has to be pulled into the midst of the Sangha, should be corrected like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.34

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.35
Pali: Māyye, etaṁ bhikkhuṁ anuvattīti. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable, don’t take sides with this monk.”

Pali: Dutiyampi vattabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): They should correct her a second

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.42
Pali: Byattāya bhikkhuniyā paṭibalāya saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): A competent and capable nun should inform the Sangha:

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.44

English (brahmali, published): The nun so-and-so is taking sides with a monk who has been ejected by a unanimous Sangha—in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction—and who is disrespectful, who has not made amends, and who has not made friends. And she keeps on doing it. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.45
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuniṁ samanubhāseyya tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should press the nun so-and-so to make her stop. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.46
Pali: Esā ñatti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the motion. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.47
Pali: Suṇātu me, ayye, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.48
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā bhikkhunī samaggena saṅghena ukkhittaṁ bhikkhuṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anādaraṁ appaṭikāraṁ akatasahāyaṁ tamanuvattati. 
English (brahmali, published): The nun so-and-so is taking sides with a monk who has been ejected by a unanimous Sangha—in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction—and who is disrespectful, who has not made amends, and who has not made friends. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.49
Pali: Sā taṁ vatthuṁ na paṭinissajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): And she keeps on doing it. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.50
Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuniṁ samanubhāsati tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha presses the nun so-and-so to make her stop. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.51
Pali: Yassā ayyāya khamati itthannāmāya bhikkhuniyā samanubhāsanā tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya, sā tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any nun who approves of pressing the nun so-and-so to make her stop should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.52
Pali: yassā nakkhamati, sā bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any nun who doesn’t approve should speak up. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.53
Pali: Dutiyampi etamatthaṁ vadāmi …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): For the second time, I speak on this matter. … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.54

English (brahmali, published): For the third time, I speak on this matter. … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.55
Pali: Samanubhaṭṭhā saṅghena itthannāmā bhikkhunī tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has pressed the nun so-and-so to make her stop. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.56
Pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.57
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.58
Pali: dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā, 
English (brahmali, published): After each of the first two announcements, she commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.59
Pali: kammavācā pariyosāne āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When the last announcement is finished, she commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.60
Pali: Ayampīti 
English (brahmali, published): She too: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.61
Pali: purimāyo upādāya vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): this is said with reference to the preceding offenses entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.62
Pali: Pārājikā hotīti 
English (brahmali, published): Is expelled: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.63
Pali: seyyathāpi nāma puthusilā dvedhā bhinnā appaṭisandhikā hoti, evameva bhikkhunī yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjantī assamaṇī hoti asakyadhītā. Tena vuccati pārājikā hotīti. 
English (brahmali, published): just as an ordinary stone that has broken in half cannot be put together again, so is a nun who does not stop when pressed three times not a monastic, not a daughter of the Sakyan. Therefore it is said “she is expelled.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.64
Pali: Asaṁvāsāti 
English (brahmali, published): Excluded from the community: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.65
Pali: saṁvāso nāma ekakammaṁ ekuddeso samasikkhatā. Eso saṁvāso nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): Community: joint legal procedures, a joint recitation, the same training—this is called “community”. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.1.66
Pali: So tāya saddhiṁ natthi. Tena vuccati asaṁvāsāti. 
English (brahmali, published): She does not take part in this—therefore it is called “excluded from the community”. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.2.1
Pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, but she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.2.2
Pali: Dhammakamme vematikā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.2.3
Pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as illegitimate, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.2.4
Pali: Adhammakamme dhammakammasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as legitimate, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.2.5
Pali: Adhammakamme vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.2.6
Pali: Adhammakamme adhammakammasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.3.2
Pali: asamanubhāsantiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she has not been pressed; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.3.3
Pali: paṭinissajjantiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she stops; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.3.4
Pali: ummattikāya 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.3.5
Pali: …pe…, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is deranged; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.3.6
Pali: 
English (brahmali, published): if she is overwhelmed by pain; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.3.7
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj7:2.3.8
Pali: Tatiyapārājikaṁ samattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The seventh offense entailing expulsion is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa hatthaggahaṇampi sādiyanti, saṅghāṭikaṇṇaggahaṇampi sādiyanti, santiṭṭhantipi, sallapantipi, saṅketampi gacchanti, purisassapi abbhāgamanaṁ sādiyanti, channampi anupavisanti, kāyampi tadatthāya upasaṁharanti etassa asaddhammassa paṭisevanatthāya. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the nuns from the group of six, being lustful and aiming to indulge in inappropriate sexual conduct, consented to lustful men holding their hands and the edge of their robes, and they stood with them, chatted with them, went to rendezvous with them, consented to men coming to them, entered covered places with them, and disposed their bodies for them for that purpose. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:1.3
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:1.4

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:1.5

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:1.7

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:1.8

English (brahmali, published): “How can the nuns from the group of six, being lustful and aiming to indulge in inappropriate sexual conduct, consent to lustful men holding their hands and the edge of their robes, and how can they stand with them, chat with them, go to rendezvous with them, consent to men coming to them, enter covered places with them, and dispose their bodies for them for that purpose? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:1.9
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:1.10
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:1.11.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa hatthaggahaṇaṁ vā sādiyeyya, saṅghāṭikaṇṇaggahaṇaṁ vā sādiyeyya, santiṭṭheyya vā, sallapeyya vā, saṅketaṁ vā gaccheyya, purisassa vā abbhāgamanaṁ sādiyeyya, channaṁ vā anupaviseyya, kāyaṁ vā tadatthāya upasaṁhareyya etassa asaddhammassa paṭisevanatthāya, ayampi pārājikā hoti asaṁvāsā aṭṭhavatthukā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If, for the purpose of indulging in inappropriate sexual conduct, a lustful nun consents to a lustful man holding her hand and the edge of her robe, and she stands with him and chats with him and goes to a rendezvous with him and consents to him coming to her and enters a covered place with him and disposes her body for him for that purpose, she too is expelled and excluded from the community. The training rule having eight parts.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.2

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.3

English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.5
Pali: Avassutā nāma

English (brahmali, published): Lustful: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.8

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.9

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.10
Pali: manussapuriso, na yakkho na peto na tiracchānagato viññū paṭibalo kāyasaṁsaggaṁ samāpajjituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): a human male, not a male spirit, not a male ghost, not a male animal. He understands and is capable of making physical contact. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.11
Pali: Hatthaggahaṇaṁ vā sādiyeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Consents to holding her hand: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.12
Pali: hattho nāma kapparaṁ upādāya yāva agganakhā. 
English (brahmali, published): hand: from the elbow to the tip of the nails. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.13
Pali: Etassa asaddhammassa paṭisevanatthāya ubbhakkhakaṁ adhojāṇumaṇḍalaṁ gahaṇaṁ sādiyati, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, for the purpose of indulging in inappropriate sexual conduct, she consents to him holding her above the collar bone or below the knees, she commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.14
Pali: Saṅghāṭikaṇṇaggahaṇaṁ vā sādiyeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): And the edge of her robe: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.15
Pali: etassa asaddhammassa paṭisevanatthāya nivatthaṁ vā pārutaṁ vā gahaṇaṁ sādiyati, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if, for the purpose of indulging in inappropriate sexual conduct, she consents to him holding her sarong or upper robe, she commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.16
Pali: Santiṭṭheyya vāti 
English (brahmali, published): And stands with him: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.17
Pali: etassa asaddhammassa paṭisevanatthāya purisassa hatthapāse tiṭṭhati, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if, for the purpose of indulging in inappropriate sexual conduct, she stands within arm’s reach of a man, she commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.18
Pali: Sallapeyya vāti 
English (brahmali, published): And chats with him: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.19
Pali: etassa asaddhammassa paṭisevanatthāya purisassa hatthapāse ṭhitā sallapati, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if, for the purpose of indulging in inappropriate sexual conduct, she stands within arm’s reach of a man, chatting with him, she commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.20
Pali: Saṅketaṁ vā gaccheyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): And goes to a rendezvous with him: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.21
Pali: etassa asaddhammassa paṭisevanatthāya purisena— “itthannāmaṁ okāsaṁ āgacchā”ti—vuttā gacchati. Pade pade āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if, for the purpose of indulging in inappropriate sexual conduct, she goes to such-and-such a place when told by a man to do so, then for every step, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.22
Pali: Purisassa hatthapāsaṁ okkantamatte āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For entering within arm’s reach of the man, she commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.23
Pali: Purisassa vā abbhāgamanaṁ sādiyeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): And consents to him coming to her: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.24
Pali: etassa asaddhammassa paṭisevanatthāya purisassa abbhāgamanaṁ sādiyati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if, for the purpose of indulging in inappropriate sexual conduct, she consents to a man coming to her, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.25
Pali: Hatthapāsaṁ okkantamatte āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When he enters within arm’s reach, she commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.26
Pali: Channaṁ vā anupaviseyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): And enters a covered place with him: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.27
Pali: etassa asaddhammassa paṭisevanatthāya yena kenaci paṭicchannaṁ okāsaṁ paviṭṭhamatte āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if, for the purpose of indulging in inappropriate sexual conduct, she enters a concealed place with any man, she commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.28
Pali: Kāyaṁ vā tadatthāya upasaṁhareyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): And disposes her body for him for that purpose: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.29
Pali: etassa asaddhammassa paṭisevanatthāya purisassa hatthapāse ṭhitā kāyaṁ upasaṁharati, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if, for the purpose of indulging in inappropriate sexual conduct, she disposes her body for a man while standing within arm’s reach of him, she commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.30
Pali: Ayampīti 
English (brahmali, published): She too: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.31
Pali: purimāyo upādāya vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): this is said with reference to the preceding offenses entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.32
Pali: Pārājikā hotīti 
English (brahmali, published): Is expelled: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.33
Pali: seyyathāpi nāma tālo matthakacchinno abhabbo puna viruḷhiyā evameva bhikkhunī aṭṭhamaṁ vatthuṁ paripūrentī assamaṇī hoti asakyadhītā. Tena vuccati pārājikā hotīti. 
English (brahmali, published): just as a palm tree with its crown cut off is incapable of further growth, so is a nun who fulfills the eight parts not a monastic, not a daughter of the Sakyan. Therefore it is said “she is expelled.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.34
Pali: Asaṁvāsāti 
English (brahmali, published): Excluded from the community: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.35

English (brahmali, published): Community: joint legal procedures, a joint recitation, the same training—this is called “community”. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.1.36
Pali: So tāya saddhiṁ natthi. Tena vuccati asaṁvāsāti. 
English (brahmali, published): She does not take part in this—therefore it is called “excluded from the community”. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.2.2
Pali: asañcicca, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is unintentional; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.2.3
Pali: assatiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is not mindful; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.2.4
Pali: ajānantiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she does not know;

English (brahmali, published): if she is deranged;
