English (brahmali, published): if she is overwhelmed by pain; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.2.9
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.2.10
Pali: Catutthapārājikaṁ samattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The eighth offense entailing expulsion is finished. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.2.11
Pali: Uddiṭṭhā kho, ayyāyo, aṭṭha pārājikā dhammā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, the eight rules on expulsion have been recited. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.2.12
Pali: Yesaṁ bhikkhunī aññataraṁ vā aññataraṁ vā āpajjitvā na labhati bhikkhunīhi saddhiṁ saṁvāsaṁ, yathā pure tathā pacchā, pārājikā hoti asaṁvāsā. 
English (brahmali, published): If a nun commits any one of them, she is no longer part of the community of nuns. As before, so after: she’s expelled and excluded from the community. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.2.13
Pali: Tatthāyyāyo pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): In regard to this I ask you, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.2.14
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.2.15
Pali: Dutiyampi pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): A second time I ask, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.2.16
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.2.17
Pali: Tatiyampi pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): A third time I ask, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.2.18
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.2.19
Pali: Parisuddhetthāyyāyo, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmīti. 
English (brahmali, published): You are pure in this and therefore silent. I will remember it thus.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-pj8:2.2.20
Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅge pārājikakaṇḍaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing expulsion in the Nuns’ Analysis is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.2

English (brahmali, published): the nuns from the group of six wore their sarongs hanging down in front and behind. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.3
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.4
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo puratopi pacchatopi olambentī nivāsessanti, seyyathāpi gihiniyo kāmabhoginiyo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns wear their sarongs hanging down in front and behind? They’re just like householders who indulge in worldly pleasures!” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.5
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.6

English (brahmali, published): and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.7
Pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo puratopi pacchatopi olambentī nivāsessantī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can the nuns from the group of six wear their sarongs hanging down in front and behind?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.8

English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that those nuns do this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.9
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.10
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.11

English (brahmali, published): “How can the nuns from the group of six do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.12
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.13
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.14.1
Pali: “Parimaṇḍalaṁ nivāsessāmīti sikkhā karaṇīyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘“I will wear my sarong evenly all around,” this is how you should train.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.15
Pali: Parimaṇḍalaṁ nivāsetabbaṁ nābhimaṇḍalaṁ jāṇumaṇḍalaṁ paṭicchādentiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): One should wear one’s sarong evenly all around, covering the navel and the knees. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.16
Pali: Yā anādariyaṁ paṭicca purato vā pacchato vā olambentī nivāseti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If a nun, out of disrespect, wears her sarong hanging down in front or behind, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.17.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.18
Pali: asañcicca, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is unintentional; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.19
Pali: assatiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is not mindful; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.20
Pali: ajānantiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she does not know; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.21
Pali: gilānāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is sick; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.22
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.23
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.24
Pali: ādikammikāyāti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk1:1.25

English (brahmali, published): (Contracted.)

English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the nuns. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk75:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena gilānā bhikkhuniyo udake uccārampi passāvampi kheḷampi kātuṁ kukkuccāyanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards, being afraid of wrongdoing, sick nuns did not defecate, urinate, or spit in water. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk75:2.2

English (brahmali, published): They told the Buddha. He then had the Sangha gathered and addressed the monks:

English (brahmali, published): “Monks, I allow a sick nun to defecate, urinate, and spit in water. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk75:2.4
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): And so, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk75:2.5.1

English (brahmali, published): ‘“When not sick, I will not defecate, urinate, or spit in water,” this is how you should train.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk75:2.6
Pali: Na udake agilānāya uccāro vā passāvo vā kheḷo vā kātabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If one is not sick, one should not defecate, urinate, or spit in water. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk75:2.7

English (brahmali, published): If, out of disrespect, a nun who is not sick defecates, urinates, or spits in water, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk75:2.8.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk75:2.9
Pali: asañcicca, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is unintentional; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk75:2.10
Pali: assatiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is not mindful; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk75:2.11
Pali: ajānantiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she does not know; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk75:2.12
Pali: gilānāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is sick; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk75:2.13

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk75:2.14

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk75:2.15

English (brahmali, published): if she is overwhelmed by pain; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk75:2.18
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk75:2.19
Pali: Pannarasamasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The fifteenth training rule is finished. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk75:2.20
Pali: Pādukavaggo sattamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The seventh subchapter on shoes is finished.

English (brahmali, published): In regard to this I ask you, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk75:2.23

English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk75:2.24

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk75:2.25

English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk75:2.26

English (brahmali, published): A third time I ask, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk75:2.27
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk75:2.28
Pali: Parisuddhetthāyyāyo, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmīti. 
English (brahmali, published): You are pure in this and therefore silent. I will remember it thus.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-sk75:2.29

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on training is finished.

English (brahmali, published): and he said,

English (brahmali, published): But when they allocated it, it fell to the one without faith. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.23
Pali: Atha kho so assaddho appasanno bhikkhuniyo upasaṅkamitvā etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): He then went to the nuns and said, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.24
Pali: “nikkhamathāyye, amhākaṁ udosito”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please leave, venerables, this storehouse is mine.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.25
Pali: Evaṁ vutte thullanandā bhikkhunī taṁ purisaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): The nun Thullanandā said to him, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.26
Pali: “māyyo, evaṁ avaca, tumhākaṁ pitunā bhikkhunisaṅghassa dinno”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “No, your father gave it to the Sangha of nuns.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.27
Pali: “Dinno na dinno”ti vohārike mahāmatte pucchiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): Because they were unable to agree, they asked judges to decide on the matter. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.28
Pali: Mahāmattā evamāhaṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): They said, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.29
Pali: “ko, ayye, jānāti bhikkhunisaṅghassa dinno”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, who knows that it was given to the Sangha of nuns?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.30
Pali: Evaṁ vutte, thullanandā bhikkhunī te mahāmatte etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Thullanandā replied, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.31
Pali: “api nāyyā tumhehi diṭṭhaṁ vā sutaṁ vā sakkhiṁ ṭhapayitvā dānaṁ diyyamānan”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “But sirs, didn’t you appoint a witness who saw or heard the giving of the gift?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.32
Pali: Atha kho te mahāmattā—
English (brahmali, published): Saying, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.33
Pali: “saccaṁ kho ayyā āhā”ti taṁ udositaṁ bhikkhunisaṅghassa akaṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true what the venerable says,” the judges made the storehouse the property of the Sangha of nuns. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.34
Pali: Atha kho so puriso parājito ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—
English (brahmali, published): The defeated man complained and criticized the nuns, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.35
Pali: “assamaṇiyo imā muṇḍā bandhakiniyo. 
English (brahmali, published): “They’re not monastics, these shaven-headed sluts. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.36
Pali: Kathañhi nāma amhākaṁ udositaṁ acchindāpessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): How could they take my storehouse?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.37
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī mahāmattānaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): Thullanandā told the judges of this 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.38
Pali: Mahāmattā taṁ purisaṁ daṇḍāpesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): and they punished him. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.39
Pali: Atha kho so puriso daṇḍito bhikkhunupassayassa avidūre ājīvakaseyyaṁ kārāpetvā ājīvake uyyojesi—
English (brahmali, published): That man then made a dwelling place for Ājīvaka ascetics near the nunnery, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.40
Pali: “etā bhikkhuniyo accāvadathā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): inciting them to drown out the nuns. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.41
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī mahāmattānaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): Once again Thullanandā told the judges 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.42
Pali: Mahāmattā taṁ purisaṁ bandhāpesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): and the judges jailed him. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.43
Pali: Manussā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): People then complained and criticized those nuns, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.44
Pali: “paṭhamaṁ bhikkhuniyo udositaṁ acchindāpesuṁ, dutiyaṁ daṇḍāpesuṁ, tatiyaṁ bandhāpesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “First the nuns take his storehouse, then they have him punished, and then they have him jailed. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.45
Pali: Idāni ghātāpessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Next they’ll have him executed!” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.46
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Nuns heard the complaints of those people, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.47
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.48
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā ussayavādikā viharissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Thullanandā take legal action?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.49
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): Then those nuns told the monks … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.50
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī ussayavādikā viharatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā is taking legal action?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.51
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.52
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.53
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī ussayavādikā viharissati. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nun Thullanandā take legal action? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.54
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.55
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:1.56.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī ussayavādikā vihareyya gahapatinā vā gahapatiputtena vā dāsena vā kammakārena vā antamaso samaṇaparibbājakenāpi, ayaṁ bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun takes legal action against a householder or a householder’s offspring or a slave or a worker or even toward a monastic or a wanderer, then that nun has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.5
Pali: Ussayavādikā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Takes legal action: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.6
Pali: aḍḍakārikā vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): what is meant is that she is the initiator of a lawsuit. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.7
Pali: Gahapati nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A householder: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.8
Pali: yo koci agāraṁ ajjhāvasati. 
English (brahmali, published): anyone who lives at home. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.9
Pali: Gahapatiputto nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A householder’s offspring: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.10
Pali: ye keci puttabhātaro. 
English (brahmali, published): whoever is an offspring or a sibling. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.11
Pali: Dāso nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A slave: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.12
Pali: antojāto dhanakkīto karamarānīto. 
English (brahmali, published): one born in the household, one who has been bought, one who has been brought back as a captive. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.13
Pali: Kammakāro nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A worker: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.14
Pali: bhaṭako āhatako. 
English (brahmali, published): a paid worker, a servant. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.15
Pali: Samaṇaparibbājako nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A monastic or a wanderer: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.16
Pali: bhikkhuñca bhikkhuniñca sikkhamānañca sāmaṇerañca sāmaṇeriñca ṭhapetvā yo koci paribbājakasamāpanno. 
English (brahmali, published): anyone who is a wanderer apart from Buddhist monks, nuns, trainee nuns, novice monks, and novice nuns. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.17
Pali: Aḍḍaṁ karissāmīti dutiyaṁ vā pariyesati gacchati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, thinking, “I’ll initiate a lawsuit,” she looks for a companion or just goes there herself, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.18
Pali: Ekassa āroceti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she tells one other person, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.19
Pali: Dutiyassa āroceti, āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she tells a second person, she commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.20
Pali: Aḍḍapariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): At the end of the lawsuit, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.21
Pali: Paṭhamāpattikanti 
English (brahmali, published): An immediate offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.22
Pali: saha vatthujjhācārā āpajjati asamanubhāsanāya. 
English (brahmali, published): there is an offense as soon as the misconduct is committed, and no pressing is required. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.23
Pali: Nissāraṇīyanti 
English (brahmali, published): Entailing sending away: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.24
Pali: saṅghamhā nissārīyati. 
English (brahmali, published): she is sent away from the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.25
Pali: Saṅghādisesanti 
English (brahmali, published): Suspension: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.26
Pali: saṅghova tassā āpattiyā mānattaṁ deti mūlāya paṭikassati abbheti, na sambahulā na ekā bhikkhunī. Tena vuccati “saṅghādiseso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): only the Sangha gives the trial period for that offense, sends back to the beginning, and rehabilitates—not several nuns, not an individual nun. Therefore it is called an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.1.27
Pali: Tasseva āpattinikāyassa nāmakammaṁ adhivacanaṁ. Tenapi vuccati “saṅghādiseso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the name and designation of this class of offense. Therefore, too, it is called an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.2.2
Pali: manussehi ākaḍḍhīyamānā gacchati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she goes there because people pull her; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.2.3
Pali: ārakkhaṁ yācati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is asking for protection; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.2.4
Pali: anodissa ācikkhati, 
English (brahmali, published): if she tells without specifying a person; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.2.5
Pali: ummattikāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.2.6
Pali: 
English (brahmali, published): if she is deranged; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.2.7
Pali: 
English (brahmali, published): if she is overwhelmed by pain; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.2.8
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss1:2.2.9
Pali: Paṭhamasaṅghādisesasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The first offense entailing suspension is finished.

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing suspension

Pali: 2. Corīvuṭṭhāpikāsikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 2. Ordaining a criminal 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena vesāliyaṁ aññatarassa licchavissa pajāpati aticārinī hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time in Vesālī the wife of a certain Licchavī man was unfaithful. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.3
Pali: Atha kho so licchavi taṁ itthiṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): He said to her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.4
Pali: “sādhu viramāhi, anatthaṁ kho te karissāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Please stop. If you don’t, I’ll punish you.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.5
Pali: Evampi vuccamānā nādiyi. 
English (brahmali, published): But she did not listen. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.6
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena vesāliyaṁ licchavigaṇo sannipatito hoti kenacideva karaṇīyena. 
English (brahmali, published): Just then in Vesālī the Licchavī clan had gathered on some business. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.7
Pali: Atha kho so licchavi te licchavayo etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): That Licchavī man said to them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.8
Pali: “ekaṁ me, ayyo, itthiṁ anujānāthā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sirs, please give me permission in regard to one of my wives.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.9
Pali: “Kā nāma sā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it with her?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.10
Pali: “Mayhaṁ pajāpati aticarati, taṁ ghātessāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “She’s unfaithful. I wish to kill her.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.11
Pali: “Jānāhī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “You may go ahead.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.12
Pali: Assosi kho sā itthī—
English (brahmali, published): When his wife heard 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.13
Pali: “sāmiko kira maṁ ghātetukāmo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): that her husband wanted to kill her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.14
Pali: Varabhaṇḍaṁ ādāya sāvatthiṁ gantvā titthiye upasaṅkamitvā pabbajjaṁ yāci. 
English (brahmali, published): she took their most valuable possessions and went to Sāvatthī. There she went to the monastics of other religions and asked for the going forth, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.15
Pali: Titthiyā na icchiṁsu pabbājetuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): but they refused. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.16
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo upasaṅkamitvā pabbajjaṁ yāci. 
English (brahmali, published): She then went to the Buddhist nuns and again asked for the going forth, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.17
Pali: Bhikkhuniyopi na icchiṁsu pabbājetuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): but they too refused. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.18
Pali: Thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ upasaṅkamitvā bhaṇḍakaṁ dassetvā pabbajjaṁ yāci. 
English (brahmali, published): She then went to the nun Thullanandā, showed her the goods, and once again asked for the going forth. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.19
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī bhaṇḍakaṁ gahetvā pabbājesi. 
English (brahmali, published): Thullanandā took the goods and gave her the going forth. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.20
Pali: Atha kho so licchavi taṁ itthiṁ gavesanto sāvatthiṁ gantvā bhikkhunīsu pabbajitaṁ disvāna yena rājā pasenadi kosalo tenupasaṅkami; 
English (brahmali, published): That Licchavī man then went to Sāvatthī in search of his wife. When he saw that she had been given the going forth as a nun, he went to King Pasenadi of Kosala 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.21
Pali: upasaṅkamitvā rājānaṁ pasenadiṁ kosalaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): and said, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.22
Pali: “pajāpati me, deva, varabhaṇḍaṁ ādāya sāvatthiṁ anuppattā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, my wife took my most valuable possessions and came to Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.23
Pali: Taṁ devo anujānātū”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Please permit me to deal with her.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.24
Pali: “Tena hi, bhaṇe, vicinitvā ācikkhā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Well then, find her and then inform me.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.25
Pali: “Diṭṭhā, deva, bhikkhunīsu pabbajitā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I’ve seen her. She’s gone forth as a nun.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.26
Pali: “Sace, bhaṇe, bhikkhunīsu pabbajitā, na sā labbhā kiñci kātuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “If she’s gone forth as a nun, there’s nothing that can be done. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.27
Pali: Svākkhāto bhagavatā dhammo, caratu brahmacariyaṁ sammā dukkhassa antakiriyāyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Teaching of the Buddha is well-proclaimed. Let her practice the spiritual life for the full ending of suffering.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.28
Pali: Atha kho so licchavi ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—
English (brahmali, published): Then that Licchavī man complained and criticized the nuns, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.29
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo coriṁ pabbājessantī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nuns give the going forth to a criminal?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.30
Pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tassa licchavissa ujjhāyantassa khiyyantassa vipācentassa. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns heard the complaints of that Licchavī man, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.31
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Thullanandā give the going forth to a criminal?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.33
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns told the monks. … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.34
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī coriṁ pabbājetī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.35
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.”

Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.37
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī coriṁ pabbājessati. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nun Thullanandā give the going forth to a criminal? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.38
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.39
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:1.40.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī jānaṁ coriṁ vajjhaṁ viditaṁ anapaloketvā rājānaṁ vā saṅghaṁ vā gaṇaṁ vā pūgaṁ vā seṇiṁ vā aññatra kappā vuṭṭhāpeyya, ayampi bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun, without getting permission from the king or the Sangha or a community or an association or a society, knowingly gives the full admission to a female criminal who is known as sentenced to death, then, except when it is allowable, that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.5
Pali: Jānāti nāma 
English (brahmali, published): She knows: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.6
Pali: sāmaṁ vā jānāti aññe vā tassā ārocenti, sā vā āroceti. 
English (brahmali, published): she knows by herself or others have told her or she has told her. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.7
Pali: Corī nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A female criminal: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.8
Pali: yā pañcamāsakaṁ vā atirekapañcamāsakaṁ vā agghanakaṁ adinnaṁ theyyasaṅkhātaṁ ādiyati, esā corī nāma. 
English (brahmali, published): any female who has stolen anything worth five <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> coins or more is called “a female criminal”. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.9
Pali: Vajjhā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Sentenced to death: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.10
Pali: yaṁ katvā vajjhappattā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): she has been sentenced to death because of her action. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.11
Pali: Viditā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Is known: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.12
Pali: aññehi manussehi ñātā hoti “vajjhā esā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): it is known to other people that she has been sentenced to death. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.13
Pali: Anapaloketvāti 
English (brahmali, published): Without getting permission from: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.14
Pali: anāpucchā. 
English (brahmali, published): without having asked permission. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.15
Pali: Rājā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The king: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.16
Pali: yattha rājā anusāsati, rājā apaloketabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): where a king reigns, permission should be obtained from the king. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.17
Pali: Saṅgho nāma 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.18
Pali: bhikkhunisaṅgho vuccati, bhikkhunisaṅgho apaloketabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): what is meant is the Sangha of nuns, and permission should be obtained from that Sangha. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.19
Pali: Gaṇo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A community: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.20
Pali: yattha gaṇo anusāsati, gaṇo apaloketabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): where a community governs, permission should be obtained from that community. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.21
Pali: Pūgo nāma 
English (brahmali, published): An association: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.22
Pali: yattha pūgo anusāsati, pūgo apaloketabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): where an association governs, permission should be obtained from that association. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.23
Pali: Seṇi nāma 
English (brahmali, published): A society: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.24
Pali: yattha seṇi anusāsati, seṇi apaloketabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): where a society governs, permission should be obtained from that society. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.25
Pali: Aññatra kappāti 
English (brahmali, published): Except when it is allowable: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.26
Pali: ṭhapetvā kappaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): unless it is allowable. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.27
Pali: Kappaṁ nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Allowable: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.28
Pali: dve kappāni— titthiyesu vā pabbajitā hoti aññāsu vā bhikkhunīsu pabbajitā. 
English (brahmali, published): there are two allowable situations: she has gone forth with monastics of another religion or she has gone forth with other Buddhist nuns. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.29
Pali: Aññatra kappā “vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti gaṇaṁ vā ācariniṁ vā pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā pariyesati, sīmaṁ vā sammannati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to give the full admission, she searches for a group, a teacher, a bowl, or a robe, or she establishes a monastery zone, then, except when it is allowable, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.30
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.31
Pali: Dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā. 
English (brahmali, published): After each of the first two announcements, she commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.32
Pali: Kammavācāpariyosāne upajjhāyāya āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When the last announcement is finished, the preceptor commits an offense entailing suspension, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.33
Pali: Gaṇassa ca ācariniyā ca āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): and the group and the teacher commit an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.34
Pali: Ayampīti 
English (brahmali, published): That too: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.35
Pali: purimaṁ upādāya vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): this is said with reference to the preceding offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.36
Pali: Paṭhamāpattikanti 
English (brahmali, published): An immediate offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.37
Pali: saha vatthujjhācārā āpajjati asamanubhāsanāya. 
English (brahmali, published): there is an offense as soon as the misconduct is committed, and no pressing is required. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.38
Pali: Nissāraṇīyanti 
English (brahmali, published): Entailing sending away: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.39
Pali: saṅghamhā nissārīyati. 
English (brahmali, published): she is sent away from the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.40
Pali: Saṅghādisesanti 
English (brahmali, published): Suspension: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.1.41
Pali: …pe… tenapi vuccati saṅghādisesoti. 
English (brahmali, published): … Therefore, too, it is called an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.2.1
Pali: Coriyā corisaññā aññatra kappā vuṭṭhāpeti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is a criminal, and the nun perceives her as such, and she gives her the full admission, except when it is allowable, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.2.2
Pali: Coriyā vematikā aññatra kappā vuṭṭhāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is a criminal, but the nun is unsure of it, and she gives her the full admission, except when it is allowable, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.2.3
Pali: Coriyā acorisaññā aññatra kappā vuṭṭhāpeti, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is a criminal, but the nun does not perceive her as such, and she gives her the full admission, except when it is allowable, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.2.4
Pali: Acoriyā corisaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is not a criminal, but the nun perceives her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.2.5
Pali: Acoriyā vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is not a criminal, but the nun is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.2.6
Pali: Acoriyā acorisaññā, anāpatti. 
English (brahmali, published): If she is not a criminal, and the nun does not perceive her as such, there is no offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.3.2
Pali: ajānantī vuṭṭhāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she gives her the full admission without knowing that she is a criminal; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.3.3
Pali: apaloketvā vuṭṭhāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she gives her the full admission after getting permission; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.3.4
Pali: kappakataṁ vuṭṭhāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she gives her the full admission when it is allowable; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.3.5
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.3.6
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss2:2.3.7
Pali: Dutiyasaṅghādisesasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The second offense entailing suspension is finished.

Pali: 3. Ekagāmantaragamanasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 3. Being alone 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhaddāya kāpilāniyā antevāsinī bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi saddhiṁ bhaṇḍitvā gāmakaṁ ñātikulaṁ agamāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): a nun who was a pupil of Bhaddā Kāpilānī had an argument with the nuns and then went to her relatives’ village. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:1.3
Pali: Bhaddā kāpilānī taṁ bhikkhuniṁ apassantī bhikkhuniyo pucchi—“kahaṁ itthannāmā, na dissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Not seeing her pupil anywhere, Bhaddā Kāpilānī asked the nuns, “Where’s so-and-so? She’s disappeared.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:1.4
Pali: “Bhikkhunīhi saddhiṁ, ayye, bhaṇḍitvā na dissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “She disappeared, venerable, after arguing with the nuns.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:1.5
Pali: “Ammā, amukasmiṁ gāmake etissā ñātikulaṁ. Tattha gantvā vicinathā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “My dears, her relatives live in such-and-such a village. Go there and look for her.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:1.6
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo tattha gantvā taṁ bhikkhuniṁ passitvā etadavocuṁ—“kissa tvaṁ, ayye, ekikā āgatā, kaccisi appadhaṁsitā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns went there, and when they saw her, they said to her, “Why did you go alone, venerable? We hope you weren’t assaulted?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:1.7
Pali: “Appadhaṁsitāmhi, ayye”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I wasn’t.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:1.8
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:1.9
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhunī ekā gāmantaraṁ gacchissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could a nun walk to the next village by herself?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:1.10
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī ekā gāmantaraṁ gacchatī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that a nun did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:1.11
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:1.12
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her …

English (brahmali, published): “How could a nun do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:1.14
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:1.15
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:1.16.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī ekā gāmantaraṁ gaccheyya, ayampi bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun walks to the next inhabited area by herself, then that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:1.17
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhunīnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the nuns. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:2.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena dve bhikkhuniyo sāketā sāvatthiṁ addhānamaggappaṭipannā honti. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion two nuns were traveling from Sāketa to Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:2.2
Pali: Antarāmagge nadī taritabbā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): On the way they had to cross a river. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:2.3
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo nāvike upasaṅkamitvā etadavocuṁ—“sādhu no, āvuso, tārethā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): They went to a boatman and said, “Please take us across.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:2.4
Pali: “Nāyye, sakkā ubho sakiṁ tāretun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I’m not able, venerables, to take both of you across at the same time.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:2.5
Pali: Eko ekaṁ uttāresi. 
English (brahmali, published): And so they crossed individually, alone with the boatman. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:2.6
Pali: Uttiṇṇo uttiṇṇaṁ dūsesi. 
English (brahmali, published): When he had crossed with the first nun, he raped her. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:2.7
Pali: Anuttiṇṇo anuttiṇṇaṁ dūsesi. 
English (brahmali, published): And after returning to the first bank, he raped the other nun as well. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:2.8
Pali: Tā pacchā samāgantvā pucchiṁsu—
English (brahmali, published): Later, when they were reunited, they asked each other, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:2.9
Pali: “kaccisi, ayye, appadhaṁsitā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, I hope you weren’t assaulted?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:2.10
Pali: “Padhaṁsitāmhi, ayye. 
English (brahmali, published): “I was. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:2.11
Pali: Tvaṁ pana, ayye, appadhaṁsitā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): And you, venerable, were you assaulted?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:2.12
Pali: “Padhaṁsitāmhi, ayye”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I was, too.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:2.13
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo sāvatthiṁ gantvā bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They then continued on to Sāvatthī and told the nuns there what had happened. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:2.14
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

English (brahmali, published): “How could a nun cross a river by herself?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:2.16
Pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They told the monks,

Pali: Bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): who in turn told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:2.18
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī ekā nadīpāraṁ gacchatī”ti?

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:2.21

English (brahmali, published): “How could a nun do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:2.22
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:2.23
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And so, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:2.24.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī ekā vā gāmantaraṁ gaccheyya, ekā vā nadīpāraṁ gaccheyya, ayampi bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun walks to the next inhabited area by herself or crosses a river by herself, then that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:2.25
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhunīnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the nuns. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:3.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhuniyo kosalesu janapade sāvatthiṁ gacchantā sāyaṁ aññataraṁ gāmaṁ upagacchiṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion a number of nuns were walking through the Kosalan country on their way to Sāvatthī, when one evening they arrived at a certain village. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:3.2
Pali: Tattha aññatarā bhikkhunī abhirūpā hoti dassanīyā pāsādikā. Aññataro puriso tassā bhikkhuniyā saha dassanena paṭibaddhacitto hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): One of the nuns was beautiful and graceful, and a certain man fell in love with her as soon as he saw her. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:3.3
Pali: Atha kho so puriso tāsaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ seyyaṁ paññapento tassā bhikkhuniyā seyyaṁ ekamantaṁ paññāpesi. 
English (brahmali, published): Then, as he was preparing sleeping places for those nuns, he prepared hers to one side. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:3.4
Pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī sallakkhetvā—“pariyuṭṭhito ayaṁ puriso; sace rattiṁ āgacchissati, vissaro me bhavissatī”ti, 
English (brahmali, published): And that nun thought, “This man is obsessed with me. If I go there for the night, I’ll get into trouble.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:3.5
Pali: bhikkhuniyo anāpucchā aññataraṁ kulaṁ gantvā seyyaṁ kappesi. 
English (brahmali, published): Then, without informing the nuns, she went to a certain family and slept there. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:3.6
Pali: Atha kho so puriso rattiṁ āgantvā taṁ bhikkhuniṁ gavesanto bhikkhuniyo ghaṭṭesi. 
English (brahmali, published): When night arrived, that man went searching for that nun, and as he did so he bumped into the other nuns. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:3.7
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo taṁ bhikkhuniṁ apassantiyo evamāhaṁsu—“nissaṁsayaṁ kho sā bhikkhunī purisena saddhiṁ nikkhantā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Not seeing that nun anywhere, the nuns said, “No doubt she has left with a man.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:3.8
Pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī tassā rattiyā accayena yena tā bhikkhuniyo tenupasaṅkami. Bhikkhuniyo taṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavocuṁ—“kissa tvaṁ, ayye, purisena saddhiṁ nikkhantā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): The following morning that nun returned to the nuns, and they said to her, “Venerable, why did you leave with a man?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:3.9
Pali: “Nāhaṁ, ayye, purisena saddhiṁ nikkhantā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I didn’t leave with a man, venerables.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:3.10
Pali: Bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi. 
English (brahmali, published): She then told the nuns what had happened. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:3.11
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:3.12
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhunī ekā rattiṁ vippavasissatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could a nun spend the night apart by herself?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:3.13
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī ekā rattiṁ vippavasatī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that a nun did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:3.14
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:3.15
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:3.16
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī ekā rattiṁ vippavasissati. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could a nun do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:3.17
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:3.18
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And so, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:3.19.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī ekā vā gāmantaraṁ gaccheyya, ekā vā nadīpāraṁ gaccheyya, ekā vā rattiṁ vippavaseyya, ayampi bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun walks to the next inhabited area by herself or crosses a river by herself or spends the night apart by herself, then that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:3.20
Pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhunīnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the nuns. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:4.1
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhuniyo kosalesu janapade sāvatthiṁ addhānamaggappaṭipannā honti. 
English (brahmali, published): On one occasion a number of nuns were traveling through the Kosalan country on their way to Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:4.2
Pali: Tattha aññatarā bhikkhunī vaccena pīḷitā ekikā ohīyitvā pacchā agamāsi. 
English (brahmali, published): One of the nuns, needing to defecate, stayed behind by herself, and then followed behind the others. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:4.3
Pali: Manussā taṁ bhikkhuniṁ passitvā dūsesuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): People saw her and raped her. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:4.4
Pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī yena tā bhikkhuniyo tenupasaṅkami. Bhikkhuniyo taṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavocuṁ—“kissa tvaṁ, ayye, ekikā ohīnā, kaccisi appadhaṁsitā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): She then went to the other nuns, and they said to her, “Why did you stay behind by yourself, venerable? We hope you weren’t assaulted?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:4.5
Pali: “Padhaṁsitāmhi, ayye”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I was.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:4.6
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:4.7
Pali: kathañhi nāma bhikkhunī ekā gaṇamhā ohīyissatīti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could a nun lag behind her companions by herself?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:4.8
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī ekā gaṇamhā ohīyatī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that a nun did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:4.9
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:4.10
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:4.11
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī ekā gaṇamhā ohīyissati. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could a nun do this? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:4.12
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:4.13
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And so, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:4.14.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī ekā vā gāmantaraṁ gaccheyya, ekā vā nadīpāraṁ gaccheyya, ekā vā rattiṁ vippavaseyya, ekā vā gaṇamhā ohīyeyya, ayampi bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun walks to the next inhabited area by herself or crosses a river by herself or spends the night apart by herself or lags behind her companions by herself, then that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.5
Pali: Ekā vā gāmantaraṁ gaccheyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Walks to the next inhabited area by herself: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.6
Pali: parikkhittassa gāmassa parikkhepaṁ paṭhamaṁ pādaṁ atikkāmentiyā āpatti thullaccayassa, 
English (brahmali, published): if she crosses the boundary of an enclosed inhabited area with her first foot, she commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.7
Pali: dutiyaṁ pādaṁ atikkāmentiyā āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she then crosses it with her second foot, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.8
Pali: Aparikkhittassa gāmassa upacāraṁ paṭhamaṁ pādaṁ atikkāmentiyā āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she enters the vicinity of an unenclosed inhabited area with her first foot, she commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.9
Pali: Dutiyaṁ pādaṁ atikkāmentiyā āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she then enters it with her second foot, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.10
Pali: Ekā vā nadīpāraṁ gaccheyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Or crosses a river by herself: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.11
Pali: nadī nāma timaṇḍalaṁ paṭicchādetvā yattha katthaci uttarantiyā bhikkhuniyā antaravāsako temiyati. 
English (brahmali, published): A river: wherever, after covering the three circles, the sarong gets wet when the nun is crossing. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.12
Pali: Paṭhamaṁ pādaṁ uttarantiyā āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When she has crossed with the first foot, she commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.13
Pali: Dutiyaṁ pādaṁ uttarantiyā āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When she has crossed with the second foot, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.14
Pali: Ekā vā rattiṁ vippavaseyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Or spends the night apart by herself: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.15
Pali: saha aruṇuggamanā dutiyikāya bhikkhuniyā hatthapāsaṁ vijahantiyā āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if, at dawn, she is in the process of going beyond arm’s reach of her companion nun, she commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.16
Pali: Vijahite āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When she has gone beyond, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.17
Pali: Ekā vā gaṇamhā ohīyeyyāti 
English (brahmali, published): Or lags behind her companions by herself: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.18
Pali: agāmake araññe dutiyikāya bhikkhuniyā dassanūpacāraṁ vā savanūpacāraṁ vā vijahantiyā āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if, in an uninhabited area, in the wilderness, she is in the process of going beyond the range of sight or the range of hearing of her companion nun, she commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.19
Pali: Vijahite āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When she has gone beyond, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.20
Pali: Ayampīti 
English (brahmali, published): That too: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.21
Pali: purimāyo upādāya vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): this is said with reference to the preceding offenses. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.22
Pali: Paṭhamāpattikanti 
English (brahmali, published): An immediate offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.23
Pali: saha vatthujjhācārā āpajjati asamanubhāsanāya. 
English (brahmali, published): there is an offense as soon as the misconduct is committed, and no pressing is required. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.24
Pali: Nissāraṇīyanti 
English (brahmali, published): Entailing sending away: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.25
Pali: saṅghamhā nissārīyati. 
English (brahmali, published): she is sent away from the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.26
Pali: Saṅghādisesoti 
English (brahmali, published): Suspension: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.1.27
Pali: …pe… tenapi vuccati saṅghādisesoti. 
English (brahmali, published): … Therefore, too, it is called an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.2.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.2.2
Pali: dutiyikā bhikkhunī pakkantā vā hoti vibbhantā vā kālaṅkatā vā pakkhasaṅkantā vā, 
English (brahmali, published): if her companion nun has left or disrobed or died or joined another religion or sect; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.2.3
Pali: āpadāsu, 
English (brahmali, published): if there is an emergency; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.2.4
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.2.5
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss3:5.2.6
Pali: Tatiyasaṅghādisesasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The third offense entailing suspension is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:0.1
Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis

Pali: Saṅghādisesakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing suspension 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:0.4
Pali: 4. Ukkhittakaosāraṇasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 4. Readmitting an ejected nun 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī bhaṇḍanakārikā hoti kalahakārikā vivādakārikā bhassakārikā saṅghe adhikaraṇakārikā. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the nun Caṇḍakāḷī was quarrelsome and argumentative, and she created legal issues in the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.3
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī tassā kamme karīyamāne paṭikkosati. 
English (brahmali, published): But when a legal procedure was being done against her, the nun Thullanandā objected. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.4
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī gāmakaṁ agamāsi kenacideva karaṇīyena. 
English (brahmali, published): Soon afterwards Thullanandā went to a village on some business. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.5
Pali: Atha kho bhikkhunisaṅgho—
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha of nuns 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.6
Pali: “thullanandā bhikkhunī pakkantā”ti, caṇḍakāḷiṁ bhikkhuniṁ āpattiyā adassane ukkhipi. 
English (brahmali, published): took the opportunity to eject Caṇḍakāḷī for not recognizing an offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.7
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī gāmake taṁ karaṇīyaṁ tīretvā punadeva sāvatthiṁ paccāgacchi. 
English (brahmali, published): When Thullanandā had finished her business in that village, she returned to Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.8
Pali: Caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā āgacchantiyā neva āsanaṁ paññapesi na pādodakaṁ pādapīṭhaṁ pādakaṭhalikaṁ upanikkhipi na paccuggantvā pattacīvaraṁ paṭiggahesi na pānīyena āpucchi. 
English (brahmali, published): On her return, Caṇḍakāḷī neither prepared a seat for her, nor set out a foot stool, a foot scraper, or water for washing the feet; and she did not go out to meet her to receive her bowl and robe, nor ask whether she wanted water to drink. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.9
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī caṇḍakāḷiṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): Thullanandā asked her 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.10
Pali: “kissa tvaṁ, ayye, mayi āgacchantiyā neva āsanaṁ paññapesi na pādodakaṁ pādapīṭhaṁ pādakaṭhalikaṁ upanikkhipi na paccuggantvā pattacīvaraṁ paṭiggahesi na pānīyena āpucchī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): why she was acting like this. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.11
Pali: “Evañhetaṁ, ayye, hoti yathā taṁ anāthāyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): She replied, “That’s how it is, venerable, when you don’t have a protector.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.12
Pali: “Kissa pana tvaṁ, ayye, anāthā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “But how is it, venerable, that you don’t have a protector?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.13
Pali: “Imā maṁ, ayye, bhikkhuniyo—
English (brahmali, published): “When the nuns knew 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.14
Pali: ‘ayaṁ anāthā appaññātā, natthi imissā kāci paṭivattā’ti, āpattiyā adassane ukkhipiṁsū”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): that no-one would speak up for me because I am not esteemed by them and I didn’t have a protector, they ejected me for not recognizing an offense.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.15
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī—
English: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.16
Pali: “bālā etā abyattā etā, neva jānanti kammaṁ vā kammadosaṁ vā kammavipattiṁ vā kammasampattiṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): “They are incompetent fools. They don’t know about legal procedures or their flaws, nor what makes them fail or succeed. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.17
Pali: Mayaṁ kho jānāma kammampi kammadosampi kammavipattimpi kammasampattimpi. 
English (brahmali, published): But we know all these things. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.18
Pali: Mayaṁ kho akataṁ vā kammaṁ kāreyyāma kataṁ vā kammaṁ kopeyyāmā”ti, lahuṁ lahuṁ bhikkhunisaṅghaṁ sannipātetvā caṇḍakāḷiṁ bhikkhuniṁ osāresi. 
English (brahmali, published): We can get legal procedures done that haven’t been done, and we can get procedures that have been done overturned.” And she quickly gathered a sangha of nuns and readmitted the nun Caṇḍakāḷī. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.19
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.20
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā samaggena saṅghena ukkhittaṁ bhikkhuniṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anapaloketvā kārakasaṅghaṁ anaññāya gaṇassa chandaṁ osāressatī”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How could Venerable Thullanandā readmit a nun who had been ejected by a unanimous Sangha in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction, without first getting permission from the Sangha that did the legal procedure and without the consent of the community?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.21
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī samaggena saṅghena ukkhittaṁ bhikkhuniṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anapaloketvā kārakasaṅghaṁ anaññāya gaṇassa chandaṁ osāretī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.22
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.23
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.24
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī samaggena saṅghena ukkhittaṁ bhikkhuniṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anapaloketvā kārakasaṅghaṁ anaññāya gaṇassa chandaṁ osāressati. 
English (brahmali, published): “How could the nun Thullanandā readmit a nun who had been ejected by a unanimous Sangha in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction, without first getting permission from the Sangha that did the legal procedure and without the consent of the community? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.25
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.26
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:1.27.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī samaggena saṅghena ukkhittaṁ bhikkhuniṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anapaloketvā kārakasaṅghaṁ anaññāya gaṇassa chandaṁ osāreyya, ayampi bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun, without getting permission from the Sangha that did the legal procedure and without the consent of the community, readmits a nun who has been ejected by a unanimous Sangha in accordance with the Teaching and the Monastic Law and the Teacher’s instruction, then that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.5
Pali: Samaggo nāma saṅgho 
English (brahmali, published): A unanimous Sangha: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.6
Pali: samānasaṁvāsako samānasīmāyaṁ ṭhito. 
English (brahmali, published): those belonging to the same Buddhist sect and staying within the same monastery zone. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.7
Pali: Ukkhittā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Who has been ejected: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.8
Pali: āpattiyā adassane vā appaṭikamme vā appaṭinissagge vā ukkhittā. 
English (brahmali, published): who has been ejected for not recognizing an offense, for not making amends, or for not giving up a bad view. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.9
Pali: Dhammena vinayenāti 
English (brahmali, published): In accordance with the Teaching and the Monastic Law: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.10
Pali: yena dhammena yena vinayena. 
English (brahmali, published): in accordance with that Teaching, in accordance with that Monastic Law. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.11
Pali: Satthusāsanenāti 
English (brahmali, published): In accordance with the Teacher’s instruction: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.12
Pali: jinasāsanena buddhasāsanena. 
English (brahmali, published): in accordance with the Victor’s instruction, in accordance with the Buddha’s instruction. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.13
Pali: Anapaloketvā kārakasaṅghanti 
English (brahmali, published): Without getting permission from the Sangha that did the legal procedure: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.14
Pali: kammakārakasaṅghaṁ anāpucchā. 
English (brahmali, published): without having asked permission of the Sangha that did the legal procedure of ejection. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.15
Pali: Anaññāya gaṇassa chandanti 
English (brahmali, published): Without the consent of the community: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.16
Pali: gaṇassa chandaṁ ajānitvā. 
English (brahmali, published): without being aware of any consent from the community. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.17
Pali: “Osāressāmī”ti gaṇaṁ vā pariyesati, sīmaṁ vā sammannati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, intending to readmit her, she searches for a group or establishes a monastery zone, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.18
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ, dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā, kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. After each of the first two announcements, she commits a serious offense. When the last announcement is finished, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.19
Pali: Ayampīti 
English (brahmali, published): That too: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.20
Pali: purimāyo upādāya vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): this is said with reference to the preceding offenses. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.21
Pali: Paṭhamāpattikanti 
English (brahmali, published): An immediate offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.22
Pali: saha vatthujjhācārā āpajjati asamanubhāsanāya. 
English (brahmali, published): there is an offense as soon as the misconduct is committed, and no pressing is required. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.23
Pali: Nissāraṇīyanti 
English (brahmali, published): Entailing sending away: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.24
Pali: saṅghamhā nissārīyati. 
English (brahmali, published): she is sent away from the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.25
Pali: Saṅghādisesoti 
English (brahmali, published): Suspension: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.1.26
Pali: …pe… tenapi vuccati saṅghādisesoti. 
English (brahmali, published): … Therefore, too, it is called an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.2.1
Pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññā osāreti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, and she readmits her, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.2.2
Pali: Dhammakamme vematikā osāreti āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she readmits her, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.2.3
Pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññā osāreti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as illegitimate, and she readmits her, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.2.4
Pali: Adhammakamme dhammakammasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as legitimate, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.2.5
Pali: Adhammakamme vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.2.6
Pali: Adhammakamme adhammakammasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.3.2
Pali: kammakārakasaṅghaṁ apaloketvā osāreti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she readmits her after getting permission from the Sangha that did the procedure; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.3.3
Pali: gaṇassa chandaṁ jānitvā osāreti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she readmits her with the consent of the community; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.3.4
Pali: vatte vattantiṁ osāreti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she readmits one who is behaving properly; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.3.5
Pali: asante kammakārakasaṅghe osāreti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she readmits her when the Sangha that did the procedure is unavailable; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.3.6
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.3.7
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss4:2.3.8
Pali: Catutthasaṅghādisesasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The fourth offense entailing suspension is finished.

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss5:1.3

English (brahmali, published): When people saw her in the dining hall, they gave her the best food, both the donors and the recipient having lust. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss5:1.4

English (brahmali, published): Sundarīnandā ate as much as she liked,

English (brahmali, published): ‘If a lustful nun eats fresh or cooked food after receiving it directly from a lustful man, then that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.’”

English (brahmali, published): If she receives water or a tooth cleaner, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss5:2.2.2
Pali: Ekatoavassute “khādissāmi bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss5:2.2.3

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss5:2.2.4
Pali: Udakadantaponaṁ paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she receives water or a tooth cleaner, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss5:2.2.5
Pali: Ubhatoavassute yakkhassa vā petassa vā paṇḍakassa vā tiracchānagatamanussaviggahassa vā hatthato “khādissāmi bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If both of them have lust, and she receives fresh or cooked food with the intention of eating it directly from a male spirit, a male ghost, a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍaka</i>, or a male animal in human form, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss5:2.2.6
Pali: Ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For every mouthful swallowed, she commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss5:2.2.7
Pali: Udakadantaponaṁ paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she receives water or a tooth cleaner, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss5:2.2.8
Pali: Ekatoavassute “khādissāmi bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If only the male being has lust, and she receives fresh or cooked food with the intention of eating it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss5:2.2.9
Pali: Ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For every mouthful swallowed, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss5:2.2.10
Pali: Udakadantaponaṁ paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she receives water or a tooth cleaner, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss5:2.3.2
Pali: ubhatoanavassutā honti, 
English (brahmali, published): if both are without lust; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss5:2.3.3

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:1.4
Pali: Sundarīnandā bhikkhunī kukkuccāyantī na paṭiggaṇhāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Being afraid of wrongdoing, Sundarīnandā did not receive it.

English (brahmali, published): The nun next to her said,

English (brahmali, published): “Why didn’t you receive it, venerable?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:1.7
Pali: “Avassutā, ayye”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Because they have lust.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:1.8

English (brahmali, published): “But do you have lust?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:1.9
Pali: “Nāhaṁ, ayye, avassutā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “No.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:1.10
Pali: “Kiṁ te, ayye, eso purisapuggalo karissati avassuto vā anavassuto vā, yato tvaṁ anavassutā.

English (brahmali, published): Go on, venerable, receive it with your own hands, and eat whatever fresh or cooked food he gives you.”

English (brahmali, published): “How could a nun say,

English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:1.21
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī evaṁ vakkhati—
English (brahmali, published): “How could a nun say this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:1.22
Pali: ‘kiṁ te, ayye, eso purisapuggalo karissati avassuto vā anavassuto vā yato tvaṁ anavassutā; 
English: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:1.23
Pali: iṅgha, ayye, yaṁ te eso purisapuggalo deti khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā taṁ tvaṁ sahatthā paṭiggahetvā khāda vā bhuñja vā’ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:1.24
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:1.25
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:1.26.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī evaṁ vadeyya— ‘kiṁ te, ayye, eso purisapuggalo karissati avassuto vā anavassuto vā, yato tvaṁ anavassutā. Iṅgha, ayye, yaṁ te eso purisapuggalo deti khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā taṁ tvaṁ sahatthā paṭiggahetvā khāda vā bhuñja vā’ti, ayampi bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun says, “Venerable, what can this man do to you, whether he has lust or not, if you’re without? Go on, venerable, receive it with your own hands and then eat whatever fresh or cooked food he gives you,” then that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.1.5
Pali: Evaṁ vadeyyāti—
English (brahmali, published): Says: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.1.6
Pali: “kiṁ te, ayye, eso purisapuggalo karissati avassuto vā anavassuto vā, yato tvaṁ anavassutā. Iṅgha, ayye, yaṁ te eso purisapuggalo deti khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā taṁ tvaṁ sahatthā paṭiggahetvā khāda vā bhuñja vā”ti uyyojeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she urges her on, saying, “Venerable, what can this man do to you, whether he has lust or not, if you’re without? Go on, venerable, receive it with your own hands and then eat whatever fresh or cooked food he gives you,” then she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.1.7
Pali: Tassā vacanena “khādissāmi bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, because of her statement, the other nun receives fresh or cooked food with the intention of eating it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.1.8
Pali: Ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For every mouthful swallowed, she commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.1.9
Pali: Bhojanapariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): At the end of the meal, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.1.10
Pali: Ayampīti 
English (brahmali, published): That too: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.1.11
Pali: purimāyo upādāya vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): this is said with reference to the preceding offenses. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.1.12
Pali: Paṭhamāpattikanti 
English (brahmali, published): An immediate offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.1.13
Pali: saha vatthujjhācārā āpajjati asamanubhāsanāya. 
English (brahmali, published): there is an offense as soon as the misconduct is committed, and no pressing is required. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.1.14
Pali: Nissāraṇīyanti 
English (brahmali, published): Entailing sending away: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.1.15
Pali: saṅghamhā nissārīyati. 
English (brahmali, published): she is sent away from the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.1.16
Pali: Saṅghādisesoti 
English (brahmali, published): Suspension: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.1.17
Pali: …pe… tenapi vuccati saṅghādisesoti. 
English (brahmali, published): … Therefore, too, it is called an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.2.1
Pali: Udakadantaponaṁ “paṭiggaṇhā”ti uyyojeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she urges her on to receive water or a tooth cleaner, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.2.2
Pali: Tassā vacanena “khādissāmi bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, because of her statement, the other nun receives it with the intention of eating it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.2.3
Pali: Ekatoavassute yakkhassa vā petassa vā paṇḍakassa vā tiracchānagatamanussaviggahassa vā hatthato khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā “khāda vā bhuñja vā”ti uyyojeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, when only the male being has lust, she urges her on to eat fresh or cooked food received directly from a male spirit, a male ghost, a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍaka</i>, or a male animal in human form, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.2.4
Pali: Tassā vacanena “khādissāmi bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, because of her statement, the other nun receives it with the intention of eating it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.2.5
Pali: Ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): For every mouthful swallowed, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.2.6
Pali: Bhojanapariyosāne āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): At the end of the meal, she commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.2.7
Pali: Udakadantaponaṁ paṭiggaṇhāti uyyojeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she urges her on to receive water or a tooth cleaner, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.2.8
Pali: Tassā vacanena “khādissāmi bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If, because of her statement, the other nun receives it with the intention of eating it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.3.2
Pali: “anavassuto”ti jānantī uyyojeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she urges her on, knowing that he has no lust; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.3.3
Pali: “kupitā na paṭiggaṇhātī”ti uyyojeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she urges her on, thinking that she is not receiving because of anger; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.3.4
Pali: “kulānuddayatāya na paṭiggaṇhātī”ti uyyojeti, 
English (brahmali, published): if she urges her on, thinking that she is not receiving because of compassion for the family; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.3.5
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.3.6
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss6:2.3.7
Pali: Chaṭṭhasaṅghādisesasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The sixth offense entailing suspension is finished.
