English (brahmali, published): 10. Renouncing the training in anger 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

English (brahmali, published): the nun Caṇḍakāḷī had argued with the nuns. In anger she said this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:1.3

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:1.4

English (brahmali, published): The Sakyan daughters are not the only monastics. There are other monastics who have a sense of conscience, who are afraid of wrongdoing and fond of the training. I’ll practice the spiritual life with them.”

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:1.19.1

English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun says in anger, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:1.20
Pali: ‘buddhaṁ paccācikkhāmi, dhammaṁ paccācikkhāmi, saṅghaṁ paccācikkhāmi, sikkhaṁ paccācikkhāmi. Kinnumāva samaṇiyo yā samaṇiyo sakyadhītaro. Santaññāpi samaṇiyo lajjiniyo kukkuccikā sikkhākāmā, tāsāhaṁ santike brahmacariyaṁ carissāmī’ti, 
English (brahmali, published): “I renounce the Buddha, I renounce the Teaching, I renounce the Sangha, I renounce the training! The Sakyan daughters are not the only monastics. There are other monastics who have a sense of conscience, who are afraid of wrongdoing and fond of the training. I’ll practice the spiritual life with them,” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:1.21
Pali: sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi evamassa vacanīyā—
English (brahmali, published): then the nuns should correct her like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:1.22
Pali: ‘māyye, kupitā anattamanā evaṁ avaca— buddhaṁ paccācikkhāmi, dhammaṁ paccācikkhāmi, saṅghaṁ paccācikkhāmi, sikkhaṁ paccācikkhāmi. Kinnumāva samaṇiyo yā samaṇiyo sakyadhītaro. Santaññāpi samaṇiyo lajjiniyo kukkuccikā sikkhākāmā, tāsāhaṁ santike brahmacariyaṁ carissāmīti, abhiramāyye, svākkhāto dhammo; cara brahmacariyaṁ sammā dukkhassa antakiriyāyā’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, don’t say such things in anger, ‘I renounce the Buddha, I renounce the Teaching, I renounce the Sangha, I renounce the training! The Sakyan daughters are not the only monastics. There are other monastics who have a sense of conscience, who are afraid of wrongdoing and fond of the training. I’ll practice the spiritual life with them.’ Take delight, venerable; the Teaching is well proclaimed. Practice the spiritual life for the complete ending of suffering.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:1.23
Pali: Evañca sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi vuccamānā tatheva paggaṇheyya, sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsitabbā tassa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): If that nun continues as before, the nuns should press her up to three times to make her stop. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:1.24
Pali: Yāvatatiyañce samanubhāsīyamānā taṁ paṭinissajjeyya iccetaṁ kusalaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): If she then stops, all is well. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:1.25
Pali: no ce paṭinissajjeyya, ayampi bhikkhunī yāvatatiyakaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If she does not stop, then after the third announcement that nun too has committed an offense entailing sending away and suspension.’”

English (brahmali, published): ‘I renounce the Buddha, I renounce the Teaching, I renounce the Sangha, I renounce the training! 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.15

English (brahmali, published): The Sakyan daughters are not the only monastics.

English (brahmali, published): And they should correct her a second

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.26
Pali: ‘buddhaṁ paccācikkhāmi …pe… sikkhaṁ paccācikkhāmi.

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.27
Pali: Kinnumāva samaṇiyo yā samaṇiyo sakyadhītaro. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sakyan daughters are not the only monastics. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.28
Pali: Santaññāpi samaṇiyo lajjiniyo kukkuccikā sikkhākāmā, tāsāhaṁ santike brahmacariyaṁ carissāmī’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): There are other monastics who have a sense of conscience, who are afraid of wrongdoing and fond of the training. I’ll practice the spiritual life with them.’ 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.29
Pali: Abhiramāyye, svākkhāto dhammo; 
English (brahmali, published): Take delight, venerable; the Teaching is well proclaimed. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.30
Pali: cara brahmacariyaṁ sammā dukkhassa antakiriyāyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Practice the spiritual life for the complete ending of suffering.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.31
Pali: Dutiyampi vattabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): They should correct her a second 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.32
Pali: Tatiyampi vattabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): and a third time. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.33
Pali: Sace paṭinissajjati iccetaṁ kusalaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): If she stops, all is well. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.34
Pali: no ce paṭinissajjati āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she does not stop, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.35
Pali: Sā bhikkhunī samanubhāsitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): Should press her: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.36
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, samanubhāsitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, she should be pressed like this. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.37
Pali: Byattāya bhikkhuniyā paṭibalāya saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): A competent and capable nun should inform the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.38
Pali: “Suṇātu me, ayye, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.39
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā bhikkhunī kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vadeti—
English (brahmali, published): The nun so-and-so says this in anger: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.40
Pali: ‘buddhaṁ paccācikkhāmi, dhammaṁ paccācikkhāmi, saṅghaṁ paccācikkhāmi, sikkhaṁ paccācikkhāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): “I renounce the Buddha, I renounce the Teaching, I renounce the Sangha, I renounce the training! 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.41
Pali: Kinnumāva samaṇiyo yā samaṇiyo sakyadhītaro. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sakyan daughters are not the only monastics. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.42
Pali: Santaññāpi samaṇiyo lajjiniyo kukkuccikā sikkhākāmā tāsāhaṁ santike brahmacariyaṁ carissāmī’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): There are other monastics who have a sense of conscience, who are afraid of wrongdoing and fond of the training. I’ll practice the spiritual life with them.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.43
Pali: Sā taṁ vatthuṁ na paṭinissajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): And she keeps on saying it. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.44
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuniṁ samanubhāseyya tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should press the nun so-and-so to make her stop. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.45
Pali: Esā ñatti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the motion. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.46
Pali: Suṇātu me, ayye, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.47
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā bhikkhunī kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vadeti—
English (brahmali, published): The nun so-and-so says this in anger: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.48
Pali: ‘buddhaṁ paccācikkhāmi …pe… sikkhaṁ paccācikkhāmi. 
English (brahmali, published): “I renounce the Buddha, I renounce the Teaching, I renounce the Sangha, I renounce the training! 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.49
Pali: Kinnumāva samaṇiyo yā samaṇiyo sakyadhītaro. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sakyan daughters are not the only monastics. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.50
Pali: Santaññāpi samaṇiyo lajjiniyo kukkuccikā sikkhākāmā, tāsāhaṁ santike brahmacariyaṁ carissāmī’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): There are other monastics who have a sense of conscience, who are afraid of wrongdoing and fond of the training. I’ll practice the spiritual life with them.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.51
Pali: Sā taṁ vatthuṁ na paṭinissajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): And she keeps on saying it. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.52
Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuniṁ samanubhāsati tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha presses the nun so-and-so to make her stop. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.53
Pali: Yassā ayyāya khamati itthannāmāya bhikkhuniyā samanubhāsanā tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya, sā tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any nun who approves of pressing the nun so-and-so to make her stop should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.54
Pali: yassā nakkhamati sā, bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any nun who doesn’t approve should speak up. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.55
Pali: Dutiyampi etamatthaṁ vadāmi …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): For the second time, I speak on this matter. … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.56
Pali: tatiyampi etamatthaṁ vadāmi …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): For the third time, I speak on this matter. … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.57
Pali: Samanubhaṭṭhā saṅghena itthannāmā bhikkhunī tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has pressed the nun so-and-so to make her stop. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.58
Pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.59
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.60
Pali: Dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā. 
English (brahmali, published): After each of the first two announcements, she commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.61
Pali: Kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When the last announcement is finished, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.62
Pali: Saṅghādisesaṁ ajjhāpajjantiyā ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ, dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā paṭippassambhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): For one who commits the offense entailing suspension, the offense of wrong conduct and the serious offenses are annulled. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.63
Pali: Ayampīti 
English (brahmali, published): That too: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.64
Pali: purimāyo upādāya vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): this is said with reference to the preceding offenses. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.65
Pali: Yāvatatiyakanti 
English (brahmali, published): After the third announcement: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.66
Pali: yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya āpajjati, na saha vatthujjhācārā. 
English (brahmali, published): there is an offense when she has been pressed for the third time, not as soon as the misconduct has been committed. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.67
Pali: Nissāraṇīyanti 
English (brahmali, published): Entailing sending away: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.68
Pali: saṅghamhā nissārīyati. 
English (brahmali, published): she is sent away from the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.69
Pali: Saṅghādisesoti 
English (brahmali, published): Suspension: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.1.70
Pali: …pe… tenapi vuccati saṅghādisesoti. 
English (brahmali, published): … Therefore, too, it is called an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.2.1
Pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.2.2
Pali: Dhammakamme vematikā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.2.3
Pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as illegitimate, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.2.4
Pali: Adhammakamme dhammakammasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as legitimate, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.2.5
Pali: Adhammakamme vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.2.6
Pali: Adhammakamme adhammakammasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.3.2
Pali: asamanubhāsantiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she has not been pressed; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.3.3
Pali: paṭinissajjantiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she stops; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.3.4
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.3.5
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss10:2.3.6
Pali: Sattamasaṅghādisesasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The tenth offense entailing suspension is finished.

Pali: 11. Adhikaraṇakupitasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 11. Accusing nuns of acting from bias 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī kismiñcideva adhikaraṇe paccākatā kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vadeti—
English (brahmali, published): At that time the nun Caṇḍakāḷī was angry that she had lost a legal case, saying, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:1.3
Pali: “chandagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo, dosagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo, mohagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo, bhayagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “The nuns are acting out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, and fear.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:1.4
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:1.5
Pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā caṇḍakāḷī kismiñcideva adhikaraṇe paccākatā kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vakkhati—
English (brahmali, published): “How can Venerable Caṇḍakāḷī say such things just because she’s angry that she has lost a legal case?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:1.6
Pali: ‘chandagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo …pe… bhayagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo’”ti …pe… 
English: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:1.7
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī kismiñcideva adhikaraṇe paccākatā kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vadeti—
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that the nun Caṇḍakāḷī says this because she’s angry?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:1.8
Pali: ‘chandagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo …pe… bhayagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo’”ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:1.9
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:1.10
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked her … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:1.11
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī kismiñcideva adhikaraṇe paccākatā kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vakkhati—
English (brahmali, published): “How can the nun Caṇḍakāḷī say such things just because she’s angry that she has lost a legal case?

English: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:1.13

English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:1.14
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:1.15.1
Pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī kismiñcideva adhikaraṇe paccākatā kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vadeyya—
English (brahmali, published): ‘If a nun is angry because she has lost a legal case, saying, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:1.16
Pali: ‘chandagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo, dosagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo, mohagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo, bhayagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo’ti, 
English (brahmali, published): “The nuns are acting out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, and fear,” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:1.17
Pali: sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi evamassa vacanīyā—
English (brahmali, published): then the nuns should correct her like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:1.18
Pali: ‘māyye, kismiñcideva adhikaraṇe paccākatā kupitā anattamanā evaṁ avaca—
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, just because you’re angry that you’ve lost a legal case, don’t say, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:1.19
Pali: chandagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo dosagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo mohagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo bhayagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyoti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘The nuns are acting out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, and fear.’ 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:1.20
Pali: Ayyā kho chandāpi gaccheyya, dosāpi gaccheyya, mohāpi gaccheyya, bhayāpi gaccheyyā’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Perhaps it’s you who are acting out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, and fear.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:1.21
Pali: Evañca sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi vuccamānā tatheva paggaṇheyya, sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsitabbā tassa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): If that nun continues as before, the nuns should press her up to three times to make her stop. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:1.22
Pali: Yāvatatiyañce samanubhāsīyamānā taṁ paṭinissajjeyya, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): If she then stops, all is well. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:1.23
Pali: no ce paṭinissajjeyya, ayampi bhikkhunī yāvatatiyakaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If she does not stop, then after the third announcement that nun too has committed an offense entailing sending away and suspension.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.2
Pali: yā yādisā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): whoever … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.3
Pali: bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Nun: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.5
Pali: Kismiñcideva adhikaraṇeti 
English (brahmali, published): A legal issue: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.6
Pali: adhikaraṇaṁ nāma cattāri adhikaraṇāni— vivādādhikaraṇaṁ, anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ, kiccādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): there are four kinds of legal issues: legal issues arising from disputes, legal issues arising from accusations, legal issues arising from offenses, legal issues arising from business. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.7
Pali: Paccākatā nāma 
English (brahmali, published): Has lost: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.8
Pali: parājitā vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): what is meant is that she has been defeated. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.9
Pali: Kupitā anattamanāti 
English (brahmali, published): Angry: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.10
Pali: anabhiraddhā āhatacittā khilajātā. 
English (brahmali, published): discontent, having hatred, hostile. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.11
Pali: Evaṁ vadeyyāti—
English (brahmali, published): Saying: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.12
Pali: “chandagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo …pe… bhayagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “The nuns are acting out of favoritism … and fear.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.13
Pali: Sā bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Her: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.14
Pali: yā sā evaṁvādinī bhikkhunī. 
English (brahmali, published): that nun who speaks thus. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.15
Pali: Bhikkhunīhīti 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.16
Pali: aññāhi bhikkhunīhi. Yā passanti yā suṇanti tāhi vattabbā—
English (brahmali, published): other nuns who see it or hear about it. They should correct her like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.17
Pali: “māyye, kismiñcideva adhikaraṇe paccākatā kupitā anattamanā evaṁ avaca—
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, just because you’re angry that you’ve lost a legal case, don’t say,

English (brahmali, published): ‘The nuns are acting out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, and fear.’ 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.19
Pali: Ayyā kho chandāpi gaccheyya …pe… bhayāpi gaccheyyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Perhaps it’s you who are acting out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, and fear.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.20
Pali: Dutiyampi vattabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): And they should correct her a second 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.21
Pali: Tatiyampi vattabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): and a third time. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.22
Pali: Sace paṭinissajjati, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): If she stops, all is well. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.23
Pali: no ce paṭinissajjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she does not stop, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.24
Pali: Sutvā na vadanti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If those who hear about it do not say anything, they commit an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.25
Pali: Sā bhikkhunī saṅghamajjhampi ākaḍḍhitvā vattabbā—
English (brahmali, published): That nun, even if she has to be pulled into the Sangha, should be corrected like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.26
Pali: “māyye, kismiñcideva adhikaraṇe paccākatā kupitā anattamanā evaṁ avaca—
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, just because you’re angry that you’ve lost a legal case, don’t say, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.27
Pali: ‘chandagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo …pe… bhayagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘The nuns are acting out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, and fear.’ 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.28
Pali: Ayyā kho chandāpi gaccheyya …pe… bhayāpi gaccheyyā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Perhaps it’s you who are acting out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, and fear.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.29
Pali: Dutiyampi vattabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): They should correct her a second 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.30
Pali: Tatiyampi vattabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): and a third time. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.31
Pali: Sace paṭinissajjati, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): If she stops, all is well. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.32
Pali: no ce paṭinissajjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she does not stop, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.33
Pali: Sā bhikkhunī samanubhāsitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): Should press her: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.34
Pali: “Evañca pana, bhikkhave, samanubhāsitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, she should be pressed like this. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.35
Pali: Byattāya bhikkhuniyā paṭibalāya saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): A competent and capable nun should inform the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.36
Pali: ‘Suṇātu me, ayye, saṅgho. Ayaṁ itthannāmā bhikkhunī kismiñcideva adhikaraṇe paccākatā kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vadeti—
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. The nun so-and-so, because she’s angry that she has lost a legal case, says this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.37
Pali: “chandagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo… pe… bhayagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “The nuns are acting out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, and fear.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.38
Pali: Sā taṁ vatthuṁ na paṭinissajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): And she keeps on saying it. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.39
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuniṁ samanubhāseyya tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should press the nun so-and-so to make her stop. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.40
Pali: Esā ñatti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the motion. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.41
Pali: Suṇātu me, ayye, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.42
Pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā bhikkhunī kismiñcideva adhikaraṇe paccākatā kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vadeti—
English (brahmali, published): The nun so-and-so, because she’s angry that she has lost a legal case, says this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.43
Pali: “chandagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo …pe… bhayagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “The nuns are acting out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, and fear.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.44
Pali: Sā taṁ vatthuṁ na paṭinissajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): And she keeps on saying it. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.45
Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuniṁ samanubhāsati tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha presses the nun so-and-so to make her stop. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.46
Pali: Yassā ayyāya khamati itthannāmāya bhikkhuniyā samanubhāsanā tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya, sā tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any nun who approves of pressing the nun so-and-so to make her stop should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.47
Pali: yassā nakkhamati, sā bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any nun who doesn’t approve should speak up. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.48
Pali: Dutiyampi etamatthaṁ vadāmi …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): For the second time, I speak on this matter. … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.49
Pali: tatiyampi etamatthaṁ vadāmi …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): For the third time, I speak on this matter. … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.50
Pali: Samanubhaṭṭhā saṅghena itthannāmā bhikkhunī tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has pressed the nun so-and-so to make her stop. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.51
Pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.52
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.53
Pali: Dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā. 
English (brahmali, published): After each of the first two announcements, she commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.54
Pali: Kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When the last announcement is finished, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.55
Pali: Saṅghādisesaṁ ajjhāpajjantiyā ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): For one who commits the offense entailing suspension, the offense of wrong conduct 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.56
Pali: Dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā paṭippassambhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): and the serious offenses are annulled. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.57
Pali: Ayampīti 
English (brahmali, published): That too: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.58
Pali: purimāyo upādāya vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): this is said with reference to the preceding offenses. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.59
Pali: Yāvatatiyakanti 
English (brahmali, published): After the third announcement: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.60
Pali: yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya āpajjati, na saha vatthujjhācārā. 
English (brahmali, published): there is an offense when she has been pressed for the third time, not as soon as the misconduct has been committed. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.61
Pali: Nissāraṇīyanti 
English (brahmali, published): Entailing sending away: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.62
Pali: saṅghamhā nissārīyati. 
English (brahmali, published): she is sent away from the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.63
Pali: Saṅghādisesoti 
English (brahmali, published): Suspension: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.1.64
Pali: …pe… tenapi vuccati saṅghādisesoti. 
English (brahmali, published): … Therefore, too, it is called an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.2.1
Pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.2.2
Pali: Dhammakamme vematikā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.2.3
Pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as illegitimate, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.2.4
Pali: Adhammakamme dhammakammasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as legitimate, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.2.5
Pali: Adhammakamme vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.2.6
Pali: Adhammakamme adhammakammasaññā āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.3.2
Pali: asamanubhāsantiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she has not been pressed; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.3.3
Pali: paṭinissajjantiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she stops; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.3.4
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.3.5
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss11:2.3.6
Pali: Aṭṭhamasaṅghādisesasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The eleventh offense entailing suspension is finished.

Pali: Theravāda Vinaya 
English (brahmali, published): Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing suspension 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:0.4
Pali: 12. Pāpasamācārasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 12. Inappropriate socializing 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā antevāsikā bhikkhuniyo saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā. 
English (brahmali, published): At that time the nuns who were the pupils of the nun Thullanandā were socializing and behaving badly, had a bad reputation, and were harassing the Sangha of nuns and hiding each other’s offenses. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:1.3
Pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
English (brahmali, published): The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:1.4
Pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo saṁsaṭṭhā viharissanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā”ti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns behave in this way?” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:1.5
Pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it true, monks, that nuns are behaving like this?” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:1.6
Pali: “Saccaṁ, bhagavā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “It’s true, sir.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:1.7
Pali: Vigarahi buddho bhagavā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): The Buddha rebuked them … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:1.8
Pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo saṁsaṭṭhā viharissanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā. 
English (brahmali, published): “How can nuns behave in this way? 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:1.9
Pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): This will affect people’s confidence …” … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:1.10
Pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:1.11.1
Pali: “Bhikkhuniyo paneva saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘If nuns socialize, behave badly, have a bad reputation, are notorious, harass the Sangha of nuns, and hide each other’s offenses, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:1.12
Pali: Tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhunīhi evamassu vacanīyā—
English (brahmali, published): then the nuns should correct them like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:1.13
Pali: ‘bhaginiyo kho saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sisters, you socialize, behave badly, have a bad reputation, are notorious, harass the Sangha of nuns, and hide each other’s offenses. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:1.14
Pali: Viviccathāyye. Vivekaññeva bhaginīnaṁ saṅgho vaṇṇetī’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Be secluded, venerables. The Sangha praises seclusion for the sisters.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:1.15
Pali: Evañca tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhunīhi vuccamānā tatheva paggaṇheyyuṁ, tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhunīhi yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsitabbā tassa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): If those nuns continue as before, the nuns should press them up to three times to make them stop. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:1.16
Pali: Yāvatatiyañce samanubhāsīyamānā taṁ paṭinissajjeyyuṁ, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): If they then stop, all is well. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:1.17
Pali: no ce paṭinissajjeyyuṁ, imāpi bhikkhuniyo yāvatatiyakaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If they do not stop, then after the third announcement those nuns too have committed an offense entailing sending away and suspension.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.1
Pali: Bhikkhuniyo panevāti 
English (brahmali, published): Nuns: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.2
Pali: upasampannāyo vuccanti. 
English (brahmali, published): what is meant is that they are fully ordained. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.3
Pali: Saṁsaṭṭhā viharantīti 
English (brahmali, published): Socialize: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.4
Pali: saṁsaṭṭhā nāma ananulomikena kāyikavācasikena saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti. 
English (brahmali, published): they socialize with improper bodily and verbal action. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.5
Pali: Pāpācārāti 
English (brahmali, published): Behave badly: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.6
Pali: pāpakena ācārena samannāgatā. 
English (brahmali, published): having bad behavior. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.7
Pali: Pāpasaddāti 
English (brahmali, published): Have a bad reputation: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.8
Pali: pāpakena kittisaddena abbhuggatā. 
English (brahmali, published): a bad reputation has spread about them. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.9
Pali: Pāpasilokāti 
English (brahmali, published): Are notorious: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.10
Pali: pāpakena micchājīvena jīvitaṁ kappenti. 
English (brahmali, published): they make a living by means of a bad and wrong livelihood. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.11
Pali: Bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikāti 
English (brahmali, published): Harass the Sangha of nuns: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.12
Pali: aññamaññissā kamme karīyamāne paṭikkosanti. 
English (brahmali, published): they object on each other’s behalf when a legal procedure is being carried out against them. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.13
Pali: Aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikāti 
English (brahmali, published): Hide each other’s offenses: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.14
Pali: aññamaññaṁ vajjaṁ paṭicchādenti. 
English (brahmali, published): they hide one another’s offenses. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.15
Pali: Tā bhikkhuniyoti 
English (brahmali, published): Them: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.16
Pali: yā tā saṁsaṭṭhā bhikkhuniyo. 
English (brahmali, published): those nuns who socialize. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.17
Pali: Bhikkhunīhīti 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.18
Pali: aññāhi bhikkhunīhi. Yā passanti yā suṇanti tāhi vattabbā—
English (brahmali, published): other nuns who see it or hear about it. They should correct them like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.19
Pali: “bhaginiyo kho saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sisters, you socialize, behave badly, have a bad reputation, are notorious, harass the Sangha of nuns, and hide each other’s offenses. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.20
Pali: Viviccathāyye. Vivekaññeva bhaginīnaṁ saṅgho vaṇṇetī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Be secluded, venerables. The Sangha praises seclusion for the sisters.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.21
Pali: Dutiyampi vattabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): And they should correct them a second 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.22

English (brahmali, published): and a third time. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.23
Pali: Sace paṭinissajjanti, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): If they stop, all is well. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.24
Pali: no ce paṭinissajjanti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If they do not stop, they commit an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.25
Pali: Sutvā na vadanti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If those who hear about it do not say anything, they commit an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.26
Pali: Tā bhikkhuniyo saṅghamajjhampi ākaḍḍhitvā vattabbā—
English (brahmali, published): Those nuns, even if they have to be pulled into the Sangha, should be corrected like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.27
Pali: “bhaginiyo kho saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sisters, you socialize, behave badly, have a bad reputation, are notorious, harass the Sangha of nuns, and hide each other’s offenses. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.28
Pali: Viviccathāyye. Vivekaññeva bhaginīnaṁ saṅgho vaṇṇetī”ti.

Pali: Dutiyampi vattabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): They should correct them a second

English (brahmali, published): and a third time. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.31
Pali: Sace paṭinissajjanti, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): If they stop, all is well. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.32
Pali: no ce paṭinissajjanti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If they do not stop, they commit an offense of wrong conduct.

Pali: Tā bhikkhuniyo samanubhāsitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): Should press them: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.34
Pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, samanubhāsitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): “And, monks, they should be pressed like this. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.35
Pali: Byattāya bhikkhuniyā paṭibalāya saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): A competent and capable nun should inform the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.36
Pali: “Suṇātu me, ayye, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.37
Pali: Itthannāmā ca itthannāmā ca bhikkhuniyo saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns so-and-so and so-and-so socialize, behave badly, have a bad reputation, are notorious, harass the Sangha of nuns, and hide each other’s offenses. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.38
Pali: Tā taṁ vatthuṁ na paṭinissajjanti. 
English (brahmali, published): And they keep on doing it. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.39
Pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmañca itthannāmañca bhikkhuniyo samanubhāseyya tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha is ready, it should press the nuns so-and-so and so-and-so to make them stop. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.40
Pali: Esā ñatti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the motion. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.41
Pali: Suṇātu me, ayye, saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.42
Pali: Itthannāmā ca itthannāmā ca bhikkhuniyo saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns so-and-so and so-and-so socialize, behave badly, have a bad reputation, are notorious, harass the Sangha of nuns, and hide each other’s offenses. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.43
Pali: Tā taṁ vatthuṁ na paṭinissajjanti. 
English (brahmali, published): And they keep on doing it. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.44
Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmañca itthannāmañca bhikkhuniyo samanubhāsati tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha presses the nuns so-and-so and so-and-so to make them stop. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.45
Pali: Yassā ayyāya khamati itthannāmāya ca itthannāmāya ca bhikkhunīnaṁ samanubhāsanā tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya, sā tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any nun who approves of pressing the nuns so-and-so and so-and-so to make them stop should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.46
Pali: yassā nakkhamati, sā bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any nun who doesn’t approve should speak up. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.47
Pali: Dutiyampi etamatthaṁ vadāmi …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): For the second time, I speak on this matter. … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.48
Pali: tatiyampi etamatthaṁ vadāmi …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): For the third time, I speak on this matter. … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.49
Pali: Samanubhaṭṭhā saṅghena, itthannāmā ca itthannāmā ca bhikkhuniyo tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has pressed the nuns so-and-so and so-and-so to make them stop. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.50
Pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.51
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): After the motion, they commit an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.52
Pali: dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā, 
English (brahmali, published): After each of the first two announcements, they commit a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.53
Pali: kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When the last announcement is finished, they commit an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.54
Pali: Saṅghādisesaṁ ajjhāpajjantīnaṁ ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā paṭippassambhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): For those who commit the offense entailing suspension, the offense of wrong conduct and the serious offenses are annulled. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.55
Pali: Dve tisso ekato samanubhāsitabbā. Tatuttari na samanubhāsitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): Two or three may be pressed together, but not more than that. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.56
Pali: Imāpi bhikkhuniyoti 
English (brahmali, published): Those nuns too: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.57
Pali: purimāyo upādāya vuccanti. 
English (brahmali, published): this is said with reference to the preceding offenses. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.58
Pali: Yāvatatiyakanti 
English (brahmali, published): After the third announcement: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.59
Pali: yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya āpajjanti, na saha vatthujjhācārā. 
English (brahmali, published): there is an offense when they have been pressed for the third time, not as soon as the misconduct has been committed. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.60
Pali: Nissāraṇīyanti 
English (brahmali, published): Entailing sending away: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.61
Pali: saṅghamhā nissārīyati. 
English (brahmali, published): they are sent away from the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.62
Pali: Saṅghādisesoti 
English (brahmali, published): Suspension: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.1.63
Pali: …pe… tenapi vuccati saṅghādisesoti. 
English (brahmali, published): … Therefore, too, it is called an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.2.1
Pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññā na paṭinissajjanti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and they perceive it as such, and they do not stop, they commit an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.2.2
Pali: Dhammakamme vematikā na paṭinissajjanti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but they are unsure of it, and they do not stop, they commit an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.2.3
Pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññā na paṭinissajjanti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but they perceive it as illegitimate, and they do not stop, they commit an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.2.4
Pali: Adhammakamme dhammakammasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but they perceive it as legitimate, they commit an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.2.5
Pali: Adhammakamme vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but they are unsure of it, they commit an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.2.6
Pali: Adhammakamme adhammakammasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and they perceive it as such, they commit an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.3.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.3.2
Pali: asamanubhāsantīnaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if they have not been pressed; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.3.3
Pali: paṭinissajjantīnaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if they stop; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.3.4
Pali: ummattikānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if they are insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.3.5
Pali: ādikammikānanti. 
English (brahmali, published): if they are the first offenders. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss12:2.3.6
Pali: Navamasaṅghādisesasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The twelfth offense entailing suspension is finished.

Pali: 13. Dutiyapāpasamācārasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 13. Encouraging inappropriate socializing 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:1.2
Pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī saṅghena samanubhaṭṭhā bhikkhuniyo evaṁ vadeti—
English (brahmali, published): At that time the nun Thullanandā had been pressed by the Sangha. She then said to the nuns, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:1.3
Pali: “saṁsaṭṭhāva, ayye, tumhe viharatha. Mā tumhe nānā viharittha. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, you should socialize. Don’t live separately. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:1.4
Pali: Santi saṅghe aññāpi bhikkhuniyo evācārā evaṁsaddā evaṁsilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā. Tā saṅgho na kiñci āha. 
English (brahmali, published): There are other nuns in the Sangha who have such behavior, reputation, and notoriety,  and who harass the Sangha of nuns and hide each other’s offenses. The Sangha says nothing to them. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:1.5
Pali: Tumhaññeva saṅgho uññāya paribhavena akkhantiyā vebhassiyā dubbalyā evamāha— ‘bhaginiyo kho saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā. Viviccathāyye. Vivekaññeva bhaginīnaṁ saṅgho vaṇṇetī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): It’s because of disrespect, contempt, impatience, and slander, and because you are weak that the Sangha says to you, ‘Sisters, you socialize, behave badly, have a bad reputation, are notorious, harass the Sangha of nuns, and hide each other’s offenses. Be secluded, venerables. The Sangha praises seclusion for the sisters.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:1.6

Pali: Tumhaññeva saṅgho uññāya paribhavena akkhantiyā vebhassiyā dubbalyā evamāha— bhaginiyo kho saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā. Viviccathāyye. Vivekaññeva bhaginīnaṁ saṅgho vaṇṇetī’ti.

Be secluded, venerables. The Sangha praises seclusion for the sisters.”’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:1.32
Pali: Evañca sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi vuccamānā tatheva paggaṇheyya, sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsitabbā tassa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): If that nun continues as before, the nuns should press her up to three times to make her stop. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:1.33
Pali: Yāvatatiyañce samanubhāsīyamānā taṁ paṭinissajjeyya, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): If she then stops, all is well. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:1.34
Pali: no ce paṭinissajjeyya, ayampi bhikkhunī yāvatatiyakaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesan”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): If she does not stop, then after the third announcement that nun too has committed an offense entailing sending away and suspension.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.1
Pali: Yā panāti 
English (brahmali, published): A:

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.4
Pali: …pe… ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti. 
English (brahmali, published): … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.5

English (brahmali, published): Says:

English (brahmali, published): There are other nuns in the Sangha who have such behavior, reputation, and notoriety, and who harass the Sangha of nuns and hide each other’s offenses. The Sangha says nothing to them.”

Pali: apakkhatā.

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.18
Pali: Evamāha—
English (brahmali, published): Says: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.19
Pali: “bhaginiyo kho saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā. 
English (brahmali, published): “Sisters, you socialize, behave badly, have a bad reputation, are notorious, harass the Sangha of nuns, and hide each other’s offenses.

Pali: Viciccathāyye. Vivekaññeva bhaginīnaṁ saṅgho vaṇṇetī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): Be secluded, venerables. The Sangha praises seclusion for the sisters.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.21
Pali: Sā bhikkhunīti 
English (brahmali, published): Her: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.22

English (brahmali, published): that nun who speaks thus.

Pali: Bhikkhunīhīti 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns:

English (brahmali, published): other nuns who see it or hear about it. They should correct her like this: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.25
Pali: “māyye, evaṁ avaca—
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable, don’t say such things: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.26

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.28

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.29

English (brahmali, published): And they should correct her a second 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.30
Pali: Tatiyampi vattabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): and a third time. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.31
Pali: Sace paṭinissajjati, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): If she stops, all is well. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.32
Pali: no ce paṭinissajjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If she does not stop, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.33
Pali: Sutvā na vadanti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If those who hear about it do not say anything, they commit an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.34

English (brahmali, published): That nun, even if she has to be pulled into the Sangha, should be corrected like this:

English (brahmali, published): A competent and capable nun should inform the Sangha: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.46

English (brahmali, published): There are other nuns in the Sangha who have such behavior, reputation, and notoriety, and who harass the Sangha of nuns and hide each other’s offenses. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.51

English (brahmali, published): The Sangha says nothing to them. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.52

English (brahmali, published): It’s because of disrespect, contempt, impatience, and slander, and because you are weak that the Sangha says to you,

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.56

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.57

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.58

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.69
Pali: Sā taṁ vatthuṁ na paṭinissajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): And she keeps on saying it. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.70
Pali: Saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuniṁ samanubhāsati tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha presses the nun so-and-so to make her stop. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.71
Pali: Yassā ayyāya khamati itthannāmāya bhikkhuniyā samanubhāsanā tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya, sā tuṇhassa; 
English (brahmali, published): Any nun who approves of pressing the nun so-and-so to make her stop should remain silent. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.72
Pali: yassā nakkhamati, sā bhāseyya. 
English (brahmali, published): Any nun who doesn’t approve should speak up. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.73
Pali: Dutiyampi etamatthaṁ vadāmi …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): For the second time, I speak on this matter. … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.74
Pali: tatiyampi etamatthaṁ vadāmi …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): For the third time, I speak on this matter. … 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.75
Pali: Samanubhaṭṭhā saṅghena itthannāmā bhikkhunī tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha has pressed the nun so-and-so to make her stop. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.76
Pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.77
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.78
Pali: dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā, 
English (brahmali, published): After each of the first two announcements, she commits a serious offense. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.79
Pali: kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When the last announcement is finished, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.80
Pali: Saṅghādisesaṁ ajjhāpajjantiyā ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ, dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā paṭippassambhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): For one who commits the offense entailing suspension, the offense of wrong conduct and the serious offenses are annulled. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.81
Pali: Ayampīti 
English (brahmali, published): That too: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.82
Pali: purimāyo upādāya vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): this is said with reference to the preceding offenses. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.83
Pali: Yāvatatiyakanti 
English (brahmali, published): After the third announcement: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.84
Pali: yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya āpajjati, na sahavatthujjhācārā. 
English (brahmali, published): there is an offense when she has been pressed for the third time, not as soon as the misconduct has been committed. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.85
Pali: Nissāraṇīyanti 
English (brahmali, published): Entailing sending away: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.86
Pali: saṅghamhā nissārīyati. 
English (brahmali, published): she is sent away from the Sangha. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.87
Pali: Saṅghādisesoti 
English (brahmali, published): Suspension: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.88
Pali: saṅghova tassā āpattiyā mānattaṁ deti, mūlāya paṭikassati, abbheti, na sambahulā na ekā bhikkhunī. Tena vuccati “saṅghādiseso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): only the Sangha gives the trial period for that offense, sends back to the beginning, and rehabilitates—not several nuns, not an individual nun. Therefore it is called an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:2.89
Pali: Tasseva āpattinikāyassa nāmakammaṁ adhivacanaṁ, tenapi vuccati “saṅghādiseso”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the name and designation of this class of offense. Therefore, too, it is called an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.1
Pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.2
Pali: Dhammakamme vematikā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.3
Pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as illegitimate, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.4
Pali: Adhammakamme dhammakammasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as legitimate, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.5
Pali: Adhammakamme vematikā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.6
Pali: Adhammakamme adhammakammasaññā, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.7.1
Pali: Anāpatti—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.8
Pali: asamanubhāsantiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she has not been pressed; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.9
Pali: paṭinissajjantiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): if she stops; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.10
Pali: ummattikāya, 
English (brahmali, published): if she is insane; 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.11
Pali: ādikammikāyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): if she is the first offender. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.12
Pali: Dasamasaṅghādisesasikkhāpadaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The thirteenth offense entailing suspension is finished. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.13.0
Pali: Kuladūsakasikkhāpada 
English: 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.14
Pali: Uddiṭṭhā kho, ayyāyo, sattarasa saṅghādisesā dhammā—
English (brahmali, published): “Venerables, the seventeen rules on suspension have been recited, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.15
Pali: nava paṭhamāpattikā, aṭṭha yāvatatiyakā. 
English (brahmali, published): nine being immediate offenses, eight after the third announcement. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.16
Pali: Yesaṁ bhikkhunī aññataraṁ vā aññataraṁ vā āpajjati, tāya bhikkhuniyā ubhatosaṅghe pakkhamānattaṁ caritabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If a nun commits any one of them, she must undertake the trial period for a half-month toward both Sanghas. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.17
Pali: Ciṇṇamānattā bhikkhunī yattha siyā vīsatigaṇo bhikkhunisaṅgho tattha sā bhikkhunī abbhetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): When this is completed, she is to be rehabilitated wherever there is a sangha of at least twenty nuns. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.18
Pali: Ekāyapi ce ūno vīsatigaṇo bhikkhunisaṅgho taṁ bhikkhuniṁ abbheyya. 
English (brahmali, published): If that nun is rehabilitated by a sangha of nuns of even one less than twenty, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.19
Pali: Sā ca bhikkhunī anabbhitā, tā ca bhikkhuniyo gārayhā, ayaṁ tattha sāmīci. 
English (brahmali, published): that nun is not rehabilitated and those nuns are at fault. This is the proper procedure. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.20
Pali: Tatthāyyāyo pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): In regard to this I ask you, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.21
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.22
Pali: Dutiyampi pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): A second time I ask, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.23
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.24
Pali: Tatiyampi pucchāmi—
English (brahmali, published): A third time I ask, 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.25
Pali: “kaccittha parisuddhā”? 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Are you pure in this?’ 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.26
Pali: Parisuddhetthāyyāyo, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmīti. 
English (brahmali, published): You are pure in this and therefore silent. I will remember it thus.” 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.27
Pali: Sattarasakaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The group of seventeen is finished. 

pli-tv-bi-vb-ss13:3.28
Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅge saṅghādisesakaṇḍaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The chapter on offenses entailing suspension in the Nuns’ Analysis is finished.
